title: [code-review] optimize_jaxpr default remains aggressive after safe-mode hardening
description: Follow-up from the last-six-hours egraph commits: public dispatch now calls optimize_jaxpr_with_config(..., OptimizationConfig::safe()) when egraph_optimize=true, but the public fj_egraph::optimize_jaxpr() and algebraic_rules() helpers still use OptimizationConfig::default(), and Default currently means numerical_safety_mode=false. That keeps bare optimize_jaxpr on the aggressive rule set after the safe-mode hardening work. Repro: build log(exp(x)) or sin(x)^2+cos(x)^2 as a Jaxpr, call fj_egraph::optimize_jaxpr directly, and evaluate with x=1000.0 or +Inf; the default optimizer may return 1000.0 or 1.0 instead of preserving +Inf/NaN. Expected: the default semantics-preserving helper is safe by default, or the aggressive API is explicitly named/documented and all tests that depend on unsafe rewrites opt into OptimizationConfig::aggressive(). Update conformance helper coverage so semantics-preservation tests do not accidentally exercise aggressive mode under a safe-sounding name.
close_reason: Fixed review findings: egraph lowering now returns typed errors for malformed variables/arity, optimize_jaxpr defaults to safe mode, and conformance coverage opts unsafe rewrites into aggressive mode.

title: [code-review] safe egraph select rewrites skip predicate validation
description:
Review evidence from the last-six-hours safe-egraph hardening:
- safe_algebraic_rules still includes select-true, select-false, and select-same in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.
- fj-lax eval_select validates the condition and rejects complex conditions with 'select condition must be boolean, got complex dtype' in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs. It also validates scalar/tensor kind and shape compatibility across the condition and branch values.
- select-same rewrites select(c, a, a) directly to a. With a complex condition, the original program fails closed, while the safe/default optimized program can return a without evaluating the invalid predicate.
- select-true/select-false have the same validation-skipping shape/kind risk for malformed branch inputs, because the original select checks matching scalar/tensor kinds and branch dtype promotion even when the selected predicate is a literal.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph regression that builds out = Select(cond, x, x) with invars [cond, x].
2. Evaluate the original with args [Value::scalar_complex128(1.0, 0.0), Value::scalar_f64(7.0)]. Expected: eval_jaxpr returns the existing fj-lax TypeMismatch for complex select condition.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe()) and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with the same args. Current safe/default can apply select-same and return Value::scalar_f64(7.0), converting a fail-closed input error into success.
4. Add a literal-condition malformed-branch case such as Select(Literal::I64(1), scalar, tensor) to cover select-true/false skipping matching-kind validation.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_select_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph select_validation -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: remove select-same/select-true/select-false from safe mode unless the egraph carries enough type/shape/predicate validity evidence, or keep them only in a typed lowering phase after validation has already been proven.
close_reason: Moved validation-skipping select rewrites out of safe/default egraph rules and added select validation regressions

title: Implement U32/U64 unary arithmetic in eval_unary_int_or_float (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Handle Literal::U32 and Literal::U64 in eval_unary_int_or_float() at crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:942-945.
WHERE: The match arm at line 942 returns "unsigned unary arithmetic not yet implemented" for both U32 and U64.
HOW: For each unary op that calls this function:
  - neg: U32/U64 negation is wrapping in JAX (twos complement). Implement as wrapping_neg() or cast to i64, negate, cast back. Check JAX oracle behavior.
  - abs: Identity for unsigned types (always non-negative). Return input unchanged.
  - sign: 0 for 0, 1 for any positive value. Trivial for unsigned.
  - Other unary ops routed here should be checked case-by-case.
Check which callers use eval_unary_int_or_float: grep for it and ensure each caller passes appropriate uint_op alongside int_op and float_op. May need to add a uint_op parameter or handle U32/U64 separately in the match.
WHY: U32/U64 are valid JAX dtypes. Any program traced with unsigned integer inputs will fail at these unary ops. The AD pipeline can generate unsigned intermediates during type promotion.
TESTING: Test neg(U32(5)), abs(U32(5)), sign(U32(0)), sign(U32(7)). Test neg(U64(1)). Verify against JAX oracle if available.
ACCEPTANCE: The string "unsigned unary arithmetic not yet implemented" no longer exists in the codebase.
close_reason: Completed: implemented U32/U64 unary neg/abs/sign handling in fj-lax with scalar and tensor coverage; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph max/min commutativity loses signed-zero order
description:
Review evidence:
- safe_algebraic_rules still includes max-comm/min-comm and max-assoc/min-assoc.
- fj-lax implements JAX/NumPy-style NaN propagation with f64::max/min after the NaN check (crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs:137-151). Rust and NumPy preserve operand-order-visible signed zeros: max(-0.0, +0.0) returns +0.0, while max(+0.0, -0.0) returns -0.0; min has the same order-visible behavior.
- Treating max/min as commutative/associative in the safe egraph marks these distinct bit patterns equivalent, so extraction can change the sign bit even though safe mode is supposed to preserve observable boundaries.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add an egraph preservation test for out = Max(v1, v2) and out = Min(v1, v2).
2. Evaluate original and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with args [Value::scalar_f64(0.0), Value::scalar_f64(-0.0)].
3. Compare f64::to_bits(), not ==, because +0.0 == -0.0. Expected: optimized result preserves the original sign bit.
4. Add a 3-input associativity case: max(max(a,b),c) with signed-zero permutations and compare output bits.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_maxmin_zero RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics max_min_signed_zero_order -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: move max/min commutativity and associativity out of safe mode unless a canonical signed-zero policy is introduced and covered by conformance tests.
close_reason: Moved max/min commutativity and associativity out of safe mode; added signed-zero bit preservation regression.

title: Cumsum/Cumprod divide by zero on empty selected axis

For a valid empty tensor on the selected axis, e.g. TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![]), axis_dim == 0 and total == 0, so Primitive::Cumsum/Primitive::Cumprod panic with division by zero. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::Cumsum, &[input], &BTreeMap::new()); the same applies to Cumprod and axis=-1. Expected JAX/NumPy behavior is to return an empty tensor with the same shape/dtype. Add regression tests for shape [0] and a zero-sized selected axis such as [2,0] axis 1, then return early when axis_dim == 0. Note: DB-backed create produced frankenjax-go1z but br sync is currently blocked by stale DB/JSONL divergence, so this JSONL-only bead preserves the finding for implementers.

title: Implement complex rem and fix catch-all error messages in apply_complex_binary and eval_binary_elementwise_complex
description:
CONTEXT: apply_complex_binary() at fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:163-165 has a catch-all returning complex arithmetic not yet implemented for binary ops not yet handled. Max/min/pow are already implemented. The remaining ops hitting this catch-all are: Rem, Atan2, and any other binary op applied to complex (Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge, Dot, bitwise ops).

Similarly, eval_binary_elementwise_complex() at line ~255 has a gated allowlist. It currently allows Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Pow/Max/Min. Rem needs to be added to the allowlist.

SCOPE:
  - Rem (complex remainder): z1 % z2 = z1 - z2*round(z1/z2). Implement in apply_complex_binary.
  - Atan2: Not defined for complex. Change error to atan2 is not defined for complex operands.
  - Other binary ops (Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge): These need complex-specific comparison. JAX compares by (real, imag) lexicographically for Eq/Ne, and raises errors for Lt/Le/Gt/Ge on complex. Change errors to not supported for complex (permanent, correct).
  - Dot: operates on tensors, handled elsewhere. Not in scope.
  - Bitwise ops: not applicable to complex. Change error to not supported for complex.
  - eval_binary_elementwise_complex: add Rem to the allowlist.

CHANGES: 1) Add Rem arm. 2) Change catch-all error from not yet implemented to unsupported for complex operands. 3) Add Rem to eval_binary_elementwise_complex allowlist.

TESTING: Test rem((4+3i), (2+1i)), test atan2(complex,complex) returns not defined error, test Lt(complex,complex) returns not supported error. Verify all existing tests pass.

ACCEPTANCE: Lines 163-165 and ~255 no longer contain not yet implemented. Catch-all says unsupported for complex operands.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex rem and replaced complex binary catch-all wording for unsupported ops; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph mul-neg-one changes promoted unsigned dtype
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `mul-neg-one` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at line around 359.
- The rewrite `mul(a, neg(1)) => neg(a)` is not dtype-preserving for promoted unsigned/integer inputs. The original `Mul` sees both operands and applies binary promotion; the rewritten program applies unary `Neg` to `a` first.
- `fj-lax` `eval_neg` supports unsigned inputs via wrapping negation, so the optimized program can return an unsigned wrapping value where the original promoted multiply returns a signed result.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Build a Jaxpr with `v2 = Neg(Literal::I64(1))` and `out = Mul(VarId(1), VarId(2))`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_u32(7)]`. Expected under current binary promotion: the multiply observes the I64 `-1` operand and returns the promoted signed result (not a U32 wrapping negation).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `Neg(VarId(1))`; `eval_neg` on U32 returns the wrapping U32 value.
4. Assert both value and dtype equality between original and safe/default optimized evaluations.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_mul_neg_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph mul_neg_one_dtype -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `mul-neg-one` out of safe mode until typed rewrites can prove the unary negation preserves the binary promoted output contract.
close_reason: Moved mul-neg-one out of safe mode and added promoted dtype regression coverage.

title: Add integration tests validating no not-yet-implemented strings remain in error paths
description:
CONTEXT: After all stub resolutions, this test prevents regressions by asserting that banned substrings never appear in any error message across the entire primitive x dtype matrix.

SCOPE: Create a new test file crates/fj-conformance/tests/no_stub_regression.rs that:
  1. EVAL SWEEP: For every Primitive variant (110 total), attempt eval_primitive with representative inputs for each dtype (I64, U32, U64, F64, Complex128, Bool). Collect errors. Assert no error .to_string() contains: not yet implemented, not yet supported, Default placeholder, or stub/placeholder/mock.
  2. EGRAPH SWEEP: For every Primitive variant, attempt jaxpr_to_egraph lowering. For errors, verify message says unsupported or excluded (not not yet supported).
  3. DISPLAY SWEEP: Construct every EvalError and EGraphLoweringError variant, call .to_string(), verify none contain banned substrings.
  4. LOGGING: Print a summary table at the end: primitive | dtype | result (ok/error) | error_msg_snippet. Use --nocapture to see.

DEPENDS ON: All other tasks in the epic: frankenjax-5a3 (complex binary), frankenjax-hdk (complex unary), frankenjax-cmb (complex select/clamp), frankenjax-znf (egraph categorization), frankenjax-fad (fuzzer).

TESTING: This IS the test. Run with cargo test -p fj-conformance --test no_stub_regression -- --nocapture.

ACCEPTANCE: Test passes with zero banned-substring matches across all 110 primitives x 6 dtypes = 660 combinations, plus all egraph lowering paths, plus all error Display strings.
close_reason: Added no-stub regression integration coverage across all primitives and representative dtypes, plus egraph/error-display sweeps; verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test no_stub_regression -- --nocapture and workspace check/clippy/fmt

title: [code-review] safe egraph bitwise idempotence skips integer validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `bitwise-not-not`, `bitwise-and-self`, and `bitwise-or-self` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 448-450.
- `fj-lax` `eval_bitwise_binary` and unary bitwise evaluation reject non-integer scalar/tensor inputs and mismatched tensor dtype/shape.
- Safe egraph idempotence rewrites can remove those validation operations and return the original value directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression for `out = BitwiseAnd(x, x)` with one input `x`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_f64(1.0)]`. Expected: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing `TypeMismatch` (`bitwise ops require integer types`).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return `Value::scalar_f64(1.0)`.
4. Add a unary case `BitwiseNot(BitwiseNot(x))` with the same F64 input; original should error before the second not, while safe/default can rewrite to `x`.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_bitwise_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph bitwise_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move bitwise self/not-not rewrites out of safe mode or guard them behind typed integer evidence from an earlier validation/lowering phase.
close_reason: Moved bitwise validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph identity rewrites still erase promoted dtypes
description:
Review evidence:
- Commit c8e070a moved pow-zero, integer-pow-zero, bitwise-xor-self, and is-finite-const-* out of safe mode because untyped egraph constants erase output dtype.
- The same issue remains in safe_algebraic_rules: add-zero, sub-zero, mul-one, div-one, pow-one, and integer-pow-one all drop an I64 literal operand and return ?a without checking the promoted output dtype.
- Current fj-lax promotion says U32 + I64 promotes to I64 (crates/fj-lax/src/type_promotion.rs:107-114), while safe egraph add-zero can return the original U32 input.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-conformance or fj-egraph regression that builds one equation: out = Add(v1, Literal::I64(0)).
2. Evaluate the original with Value::scalar_u32(7). Expected current eval output is Value::scalar_i64(7) because promote_dtype(U32, I64) => I64.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with the same input. Safe/default egraph can apply add-zero and return Value::scalar_u32(7).
4. Repeat for Mul(v1, Literal::I64(1)) and Pow(v1, Literal::I64(1)) over U32/U64.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_safe_dtype_identities RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics safe_identity_rewrites_preserve_promoted_dtype -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: remove these identity rewrites from safe mode until egraph lowering can prove/result-type the replacement, or add typed identity rules that preserve the promoted output dtype.
close_reason: Moved promoted-dtype-erasing identity rewrites out of safe mode; added dtype preservation regression.

Add a narrow behavior-preserving dedupe pass for extracted egraph equations and prove identical outputs with targeted tests/benchmarks.
close_reason: Completed shared egraph extraction dedupe and duplicate-root id-map hardening

title: Concatenate overflows output axis for zero-element huge dims
description:
[HIGH] Latest Concatenate oracle coverage added normal and empty-array cases, but crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs still computes the concatenated axis with unchecked u32 addition at line 849: tensors.iter().map(|t| t.shape.dims[axis]).sum(). Valid zero-element tensors can carry very large dimensions because Shape::element_count multiplies in u64 and any zero dimension yields element_count=0, so TensorValue::new accepts shapes like [u32::MAX, 0] with an empty element vector.

Repro: create a = TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![u32::MAX, 0] }, vec![]) and b = TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![1, 0] }, vec![]), wrap them as Value::Tensor, set params { "dimension": "0" }, then call eval_primitive(Primitive::Concatenate, &[a, b], &params). Current debug/test builds panic when summing u32::MAX + 1; optimized builds can wrap the output axis to 0 and return the wrong shape.

Expected: use checked addition for the concatenated axis and return a typed EvalError when the output axis exceeds u32::MAX; also use checked multiplication for total output element count before allocating. Add a regression test using the zero-element huge-dim repro so it does not allocate.
close_reason: Rejected overflowing Concatenate output axes and checked output element products

title: ReduceWindow trace panics on zero stride
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs still has a loose ReduceWindow shape-inference path that parses window_dimensions/window_strides with filter_map and never rejects zero. Runtime eval_reduce_window was hardened in fj-lax, but trace inference was not. Repro: create a TraceContext with one rank-1 F64 input shaped [3], call process_primitive(Primitive::ReduceWindow, &[input], params) with window_dimensions="2", window_strides="0", padding="same". Expected: Err(TraceError::ShapeInferenceFailed or InvalidPrimitiveParam) matching fj-lax runtime rejection. Actual: line 1761 reaches input_dim.div_ceil(stride) with stride == 0 and panics in debug/test builds. The same parser also silently drops malformed stride/window tokens instead of rejecting them, so fix should use strict parsing, require lengths equal rank, require positive windows/strides, and add trace regression coverage for zero, malformed, and short params.
close_reason: Fixed: strict parsing with validation for window_dimensions/window_strides length==rank and positive values. Added 5 regression tests.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph self-cancellation erases NaN/Inf
description: Review finding from the last-six-hours egraph safe-mode commits: OptimizationConfig::safe still keeps algebraic rules like sub-self, add-neg-self, and mul-zero in safe_algebraic_rules. Repro: optimize a Jaxpr for x - x with OptimizationConfig::safe and evaluate at x=+Inf or NaN. JAX/NumPy observable behavior is NaN (inf - inf and nan - nan), but the safe optimizer can rewrite to literal 0. Similarly x * 0 at x=+Inf should remain NaN but can fold to 0. title: Harden cache golden verifier against truncated snapshots
description: The fj-cache golden stability harness in crates/fj-cache/src/stability.rs verifies expected cache-key snapshots by zipping the supplied golden vector with the current generated vector. That silently ignores length mismatches, so a truncated golden snapshot can pass without checking newly added reference cases. It also ignores description mismatches, so reordered or renamed golden entries can produce confusing drift reports. Fix verify_golden_keys to report reference-count mismatches and per-index description mismatches before digest comparison, then add regression tests showing truncated and renamed golden snapshots fail. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-cache stability, rch cargo check -p fj-cache --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-cache --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Hardened fj-cache golden verifier to reject truncated, renamed, and extra cache-key snapshot vectors; added regression coverage; validated with remote rch cache stability tests, remote rch check/clippy, fmt, and UBS.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph reciprocal-as-div and neg-zero erase output dtype
description:
Review finding:
- safe_algebraic_rules contains reciprocal-as-div: (reciprocal ?a) => (div 1 ?a)
- safe_algebraic_rules contains neg-zero: (neg 0) => 0
- Both introduce untyped literal constants (1 and 0) that erase the output dtype
- E.g., reciprocal(x_f32) should return f32, but (div 1 x_f32) uses untyped literal 1
- E.g., neg(0.0_f32) should return f32, but the rewrite returns untyped 0

Fix: Move both rules to numerically_unsafe_rules.
close_reason: Moved neg-zero and reciprocal-as-div to numerically_unsafe_rules; both erase output dtype via untyped literals

title: [parity-gap] Primitive max/min NaN semantics use fmax/fmin behavior
description: Review of the recent safe-egraph max/min absorption changes exposed an underlying parity gap in fj-lax and its oracle tests. crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs evaluates Primitive::Max and Primitive::Min with f64::max/f64::min, and crates/fj-conformance/tests/min_max_oracle.rs asserts max(NaN, 5)=5 and min(NaN, 5)=5 while calling that Rust/JAX behavior. Official JAX docs for jax.numpy.maximum and jax.numpy.minimum say one NaN operand produces NaN; JAX documents fmax/fmin as the NaN-ignoring variants. Repro: eval_primitive(Primitive::Max, &[Value::scalar_f64(f64::NAN), Value::scalar_f64(5.0)], &BTreeMap::new()) returns 5.0; eval_primitive(Primitive::Min, same inputs) returns 5.0. Expected for JAX maximum/minimum/lax max-min parity is NaN propagation unless this primitive is intentionally re-scoped as fmax/fmin, in which case ProgramSpec/tests/docs/egraph rules must say so. Fix the primitive contract, elementwise and reduction oracle tests, and egraph max/min safety coverage against real JAX semantics.
close_reason: Fixed Primitive::Max/Min and ReduceMax/ReduceMin float NaN semantics to propagate NaN like JAX maximum/minimum; added scalar, tensor, and reduction coverage; validated with rch conformance/fj-lax tests, fmt, workspace check, clippy, and UBS.

Complements the reduce_sum metamorphic tests added in frankenjax-f4nf.
close_reason: Added 5 metamorphic tests to reduce_prod_oracle.rs: distributivity over concat, one identity, reciprocal cancellation, commutativity, zero absorption. All 5 tests pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_dist: zero-distance + symmetry + triangle inequality

title: [modes-of-reasoning] functional-purity-vs-python-compat
description:
FrankenJAX quarantines impurity at boundaries but cannot enforce it. Callback flavors (Pure/Io) declare intent without verification. Effect system is tracking-only and does not inform optimization. FFI boundary trusts C functions without runtime contracts.

Key locations:
- fj-ffi/src/callback.rs:3-19 (unverified Pure/Io distinction)
- fj-core/src/lib.rs:974,990 (effect tracking as metadata only)
- fj-dispatch/src/lib.rs:30 (V1 scope: tracking only comment)
- fj-ffi/src/call.rs:26-34 (trust-based FFI safety contract)

Suggested work:
1. Add runtime callback purity inference (detect if pure callback mutates shared state)
2. Promote effect tokens from observational to participatory in dispatch ordering
3. Add FFI contract assertions (pre/post-condition hooks for C boundary)
close_reason: Added PurityConfig with verify_idempotency flag in 0bb2491. Strict mode double-invokes Pure callbacks to detect non-determinism; impure callbacks that return different results trigger PurityViolation error. Addresses the gap between declared and actual purity.

title: [code-review] Egraph bitwise_xor self rewrite erases unsigned dtype
description:
Review finding from commit 1ccef8f in `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs`.

Defect: safe egraph rule `bitwise-xor-self` rewrites `(bitwise_xor ?a ?a)` to the bare egraph literal `0`. `egraph_to_jaxpr()` lowers bare numeric literals as `Literal::I64`, so optimizing an unsigned bitwise XOR self-cancel changes the output dtype/value representation from `U32(0)`/`U64(0)` to `I64(0)`. The type-blind egraph cannot safely introduce an untyped integer literal for typed bitwise operations.

Concrete repro:
1. Build a Jaxpr with one equation: `out = BitwiseXor(v1, v1)`.
2. Evaluate the original with `Value::scalar_u64(9_007_199_254_740_993)`: expected/output is `Value::scalar_u64(0)`.
3. Optimize with `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` and evaluate with the same input.
4. Actual optimized program returns an I64 zero (or otherwise violates unsigned dtype preservation) because the rewrite emits bare `Num(0)`.
5. Add/run a conformance regression with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics bitwise_xor_self_preserves_unsigned_dtype -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: remove `bitwise-xor-self` from the type-blind egraph rules, or add typed-zero lowering/proofs so U32/U64 inputs produce same-dtype zero. Cover scalar and tensor U32/U64 cases.
close_reason: Duplicate/subset of frankenjax-hf2l, which tracks the broader egraph literal dtype erasure issue including bitwise_xor unsigned zeros.

title: BroadcastInDim silently wraps oversized shape dims
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs eval_broadcast_in_dim parses the required shape param as i64, accepts any non-negative value, then casts with d as u32 at line 612. A shape dimension above u32::MAX silently wraps instead of returning an error. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::BroadcastInDim, &[Value::scalar_i64(1)], &params) with params shape=4294967296. The implementation converts that dim to 0, computes total=0, and can return an empty tensor with shape [0] instead of rejecting the impossible requested output shape. Larger multi-dimensional shapes can also overflow the product at line 625/702 or attempt enormous allocation. Expected behavior: reject negative and out-of-u32-range dimensions before casting, and use checked multiplication for output element counts. Add regression tests covering shape=4294967296 and a product overflow case.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-range BroadcastInDim shape dims and checked output element products

title: test(conformance): add Complex64/Complex128 dot product oracle tests
Implement complex rem and atan2 in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex remainder and atan2 to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: Same match arm as complex pow — the catch-all at line 160.
HOW: Complex rem: z1 % z2 = z1 - z2 * floor(z1/z2) using complex division and floor on real/imag parts separately. Complex atan2: generally not well-defined for complex numbers — JAX raises an error. We should match JAX behavior: return a principled UnsupportedOperation error (not "not yet implemented").
WHY: Completing the binary op coverage. For atan2, the right answer is an explicit "not supported for complex" error rather than "not yet implemented" (which implies deferred work).
DEPENDS ON: Complex pow implementation (shares the same code path).
TESTING: Test complex rem for simple cases. Test that complex atan2 returns UnsupportedOperation.
ACCEPTANCE: No "not yet implemented" string remains in apply_complex_binary.
close_reason: Superseded: restructuring complex arithmetic beads. The dependency on frankenjax-fij was wrong (pow already implemented), and the scope needs expansion.

title: [code-review] egraph Rev cancellation is order-sensitive on axes
description:
**File:** crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:471
**Severity:** MEDIUM (missed optimization, not correctness)

The `cancels_shape_chain` function uses order-sensitive comparison for Rev axes:

```rust
(Primitive::Rev, Primitive::Rev) => rev_axes(&previous.params) == rev_axes(&current.params),
```

When axes are specified in different orders (e.g., `axes="0,1"` vs `axes="1,0"`), the comparison fails even though the operations are identical and should cancel.

**Fix:** Sort the axes before comparison or use a BTreeSet.
close_reason: Fixed Rev cancellation by canonicalizing axes and adding regression coverage

title: Resolve complex select condition and clamp stubs in fj-lax arithmetic.rs (7 stubs)
description:
CONTEXT: 7 of the 14 stubs in fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs are in Select and Clamp: 4x select with complex condition (lines ~1110, ~1162, ~1240, ~1315) say complex condition not yet implemented for select. 3x clamp with complex value/lo/hi (lines ~1401, ~1414, ~1426) say complex arithmetic not yet implemented.

ANALYSIS: Neither needs real implementation. JAX does NOT support complex conditions for select (must be bool) or complex clamp (no total order). These are correct errors with wrong wording that implies deferred work.

CHANGES: 1) Replace all 4 select messages with: select condition must be boolean, got complex dtype. 2) Replace all 3 clamp messages with: clamp is not supported for complex dtypes. 3) Ensure none contain not yet.

TESTING: Test select(complex_cond, a, b) returns error mentioning boolean. Test clamp(complex_value, lo, hi) returns error mentioning complex dtypes. Add targeted error-message tests. Verify all existing tests pass.

ACCEPTANCE: All 7 stubs replaced with permanent descriptive errors. Zero not yet implemented in select/clamp.
close_reason: Completed: replaced complex select/clamp stub wording with permanent descriptive errors and added regression tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: Pad overflows output dimension for huge zero-element shapes
description: eval_pad computes interior_span = (dim - 1) * interior and out_dim = low + dim + interior_span + high with unchecked i64 arithmetic. Valid zero-element tensors can have huge nonzero dimensions paired with a zero dimension, so padding a shape like [u32::MAX, 0] with a very large interior padding on axis 0 can panic in debug builds or wrap in optimized builds before the existing u32 bound check. Expected: compute padded dimensions with checked arithmetic and return a typed EvalError on overflow; add a regression that does not allocate.
close_reason: Checked pad dimension arithmetic and output element counts before allocation

title: [code-review] safe egraph neg-neg skips bool validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `neg-neg` in `safe_algebraic_rules` while default optimization is safety mode.
- `fj-lax` `eval_neg` rejects bool scalar/tensor inputs.
- The safe rewrite `neg(neg(x)) => x` can remove both validation operations and return an invalid input directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Build a Jaxpr with one input `x` and equations `v2 = Neg(x)`, `out = Neg(v2)`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::Scalar(Literal::Bool(true))]`. Expected/current original behavior: `eval_jaxpr` returns `EvalError::TypeMismatch` from `eval_neg` with detail `expected numeric scalar, got bool`.
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return `Value::Scalar(Literal::Bool(true))`.
4. Add regression coverage beside the existing `numerical_safety_mode_preserves_*` tests and run `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_neg_neg_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph neg_neg_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `neg-neg` out of safe mode, or require typed lowering evidence proving the inner neg validation already succeeded before eliminating both operations. Audit similar remaining safe unary rewrites such as `abs-neg`, trig/hyperbolic neg rules, `reciprocal-as-div`, and `pow-neg-one` for validation/dtype parity before declaring the family safe.
close_reason: Moved neg-neg to numerically_unsafe_rules; bypasses eval_neg bool validation

title: Build oracle parity recapture matrix and drift gate
description: Reality-check gap: README/FEATURE_PARITY report 848 JAX 0.9.2 fixture cases, but the V1 spec requires scoped compatibility to stay tied to the live legacy oracle rather than a one-time capture. Acceptance: enumerate every committed fixture bundle and its legacy test anchor; add a periodic strict recapture command path for transforms/RNG/linalg/FFT/dtype/composition; emit a machine-readable drift report with added/removed/changed cases; document the exact JAX/jaxlib/Python version; add a conformance test or artifact-schema test that fails if the recapture manifest omits a fixture family.
design: Revised plan-space design: make oracle evidence a maintained product surface, not a one-time fixture dump. Build a registry of fixture families, legacy anchors, capture commands, environment versions, expected artifact paths, drift classes, and replay commands. The work should improve future contributors by making it obvious when fixture evidence is stale, missing, or weaker than README/FEATURE_PARITY claims.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Inventory every committed oracle fixture family and link each to legacy test/module anchors, capture script, output artifact, parity runner, and current case count.
2. Add or update a machine-readable oracle recapture manifest covering transforms, RNG, linalg/FFT, dtype promotion, transform composition, higher-rank/manual oracle families, and any intentionally excluded scope.
3. Add a strict recapture/drift command path that records JAX, jaxlib, Python, platform, x64 flag, seed policy, and legacy source revision; no fallback-generated data may be labeled strict.
4. Emit a drift report with added, removed, changed, flaky, timeout, unsupported, and excluded-scope buckets plus replay commands for changed cases.
5. Unit tests: validate manifest parsing, family completeness, fixture-count reconciliation, version-field presence, and drift classification.
6. E2E script/logging: add or update a script/test path that runs a representative strict recapture dry run or fixture verification and writes a detailed JSON log with command, env, fixture family, counts, duration, and artifact refs.
7. CI/schema gate: fail if README/FEATURE_PARITY fixture claims exceed manifest-backed evidence or if a parity_green row lacks fixture/report coverage.
8. Update docs only from generated manifest/report data; preserve all existing fixture families and do not drop any prior coverage.
close_reason: Oracle recapture matrix and drift gate are implemented and validated; remaining live fixture refresh failures are tracked in frankenjax-cstq.21.

Fixed E2E replay validation to scan env-prefixed commands for transient cargo target binaries while allowing stable cargo commands with CARGO_TARGET_DIR assignments. Hardened gate binaries and wrapper scripts so value flags reject accidental flag tokens such as --report --enforce. Added targeted unit/e2e coverage and ran fmt, bash -n, diff check, UBS, cargo check, clippy, and workspace tests.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: Cache fresh-eyes audit slice completed: traced fj-cache LRU/TTL eviction from CacheKey construction through in-memory backend storage and conformance cache journeys. Fixed namespace-loss bug where LRU/TTL tracked only formatted key strings and reconstructed evictions with hard-coded fjx namespace, so non-default namespaces could survive eviction pressure. Switched LRU order and TTL metadata to full CacheKey values, pruned TTL timestamps for LRU-evicted keys, skipped TTL bookkeeping work when ttl_secs=0, and added regression tests for custom-namespace eviction plus timestamp pruning. Validation: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, UBS on touched files, fj-cache eviction tests, fj-conformance cache-filtered tests, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test --workspace all passed via RCH where appropriate.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: FFI fresh-eyes audit slice completed: traced fj-ffi buffer/value marshalling through call and conformance FFI flows. Fixed silent usize-to-u32 shape narrowing by rejecting dimensions above the core Shape limit, made tensor marshalling fail closed on declared-dtype/literal mismatches, added I32 tensor and scalar-buffer unmarshal support using the existing I64-backed core literal representation, and enabled complex scalar/tensor marshalling without per-element allocation churn. Validation: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, UBS on touched files, cargo test -p fj-ffi -- --nocapture, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test -p fj-conformance --test ffi_oracle -- --nocapture all passed.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: AD fresh-eyes audit slice completed: traced fj-ad reverse-mode execution from forward_with_tape through backward/vjp and dispatch AD oracle coverage. Fixed two fail-closed boundary defects: public vjp now rejects an empty cotangent list with a typed CotangentArity error instead of panicking, and forward_with_tape now validates primitive output arity against equation output bindings before recording tape/env state so malformed Jaxprs cannot panic during backward replay or silently drop outputs. Added regression tests for both cases. Validation: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, UBS on crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs, targeted new tests, cargo test -p fj-ad, cargo test -p fj-conformance --test dispatch_ad_oracle, cargo check --workspace --all-targets via RCH, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings via RCH, and cargo test --workspace via RCH all passed.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: Cache-key fresh-eyes audit slice completed: traced fj-cache stability/key generation through cache lifecycle/error taxonomy/golden E2E evidence. Fixed delimiter-joined cache-key material that allowed semantically distinct compile options, custom_hook=None vs Some("none"), and hardened unknown feature lists to alias. Switched owned+borrowed builders to a shared typed length-framed streaming encoder, bumped namespace to fjx-v2, updated stability digests/docs/evidence, and added unit/conformance regressions for exact alias cases. Validation: fmt, diff check, UBS, fj-cache tests, cache_keying_oracle, cache_lifecycle_gate, error_taxonomy_gate, cache/error scripts, P2C001/P2C004/golden E2E regeneration, workspace check, clippy, workspace tests. br sync --flush-only remains blocked by stale DB/JSONL mismatch.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: AD empty-shape fresh-eyes audit slice completed: traced fj-ad derivative shape arithmetic across reduce_window VJP, dynamic_update_slice VJP, scatter VJP, reduce_prod VJP/JVP, and cumprod JVP. Fixed unchecked usize shape products, row-major stride products, and flat-index arithmetic that could overflow or panic on semantically valid empty tensors with huge dimensions. Added empty huge-shape regression coverage for reverse and forward AD paths. Validation: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, UBS on crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs, cargo test -p fj-ad empty_huge -- --nocapture, cargo test -p fj-ad --lib -- --nocapture, cargo check --workspace --all-targets via RCH, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings via RCH, and cargo test --workspace via RCH all passed.

title: Add machine-checked vision-to-evidence coverage dashboard
description: Reality-check gap: docs, specs, artifacts, tests, and beads can drift independently. Acceptance: define a machine-readable coverage map from V1 spec clauses and FEATURE_PARITY rows to tests, artifacts, and open/closed beads; add a checker that flags any parity_green row without current evidence and any V1 MUST without test/artifact/bead coverage; update TODO and FEATURE_PARITY from this map rather than hand-maintained optimism.
close_reason: Completed: vision evidence map bound to cstq14 in ebc9caa

title: Bootstrap live vision-to-evidence map before implementation
description:
Reality-check gap: the final vision-to-evidence dashboard is intentionally downstream of the evidence lanes, but users and implementers need an early red/yellow control surface before those lanes complete. This bead bootstraps the map and checker skeleton without pretending parity is green.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define the initial machine-readable evidence-map schema for README claims, FEATURE_PARITY rows, V1 spec MUSTs, artifact families, tests, e2e scripts, replay commands, and bead links.
2. Seed the map from current docs and open beads with explicit red/yellow states for missing evidence; no green row is allowed unless the current artifact/test is named and replayable.
3. Add unit tests for schema parsing, required-field validation, duplicate claim ids, missing replay command detection, stale artifact references, and green-without-evidence rejection.
4. Add an e2e bootstrap script with detailed JSON logs: scanned docs, seeded claim rows, linked beads, missing evidence counts, red/yellow/green counts, artifact refs, and replay command.
5. Emit a human-readable dashboard preview generated from the same JSON so users can inspect current gaps before implementation starts.
6. Preserve all current docs and claims in place; this bead only maps and classifies them, it does not hide or delete uncomfortable gaps.
7. The final dashboard bead must consume this bootstrap map and turn it into the hard acceptance gate after the evidence lanes land.
close_reason: Completed vision-to-evidence map bootstrap with schema, script, examples, and conformance tests

title: Define shared E2E forensic logging contract
description:
Reality-check gap: the current child beads require e2e scripts, detailed JSON logs, and replay commands, but users need one shared forensic logging contract so evidence is comparable across oracle, transform, TTL, cache, performance, durability, docs, and integration lanes.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a shared JSON schema for e2e evidence logs covering bead id, scenario id, command, working directory, environment fingerprint, feature flags, fixture/oracle ids, transform stack, strict/hardened mode, inputs, expected result, actual result, tolerance policy, error taxonomy class, timings, allocation counters, artifact paths, artifact hashes, replay command, status, and human-readable failure summary.
2. Define script conventions for location, naming, stdout/stderr, exit codes, artifact directories, sidecar eligibility, and stable log filenames so local runs, CI runs, and dashboard ingestion all behave the same way.
3. Add unit tests for schema parsing, required-field rejection, malformed log rejection, replay command presence, artifact hash validation, redaction policy, status classification, and forward-compatible unknown-field handling.
4. Add at least one bootstrap e2e script and golden/sample log that demonstrates detailed logging, replayability, failure classification, and dashboard-ready artifact references without requiring mocks.
5. Update implementation guidance so child beads cannot claim e2e coverage complete with ad hoc logging; they must either emit this shared schema or record a temporary adapter with an explicit migration bead.
6. Preserve existing logs and artifacts; migrate or adapt them without deleting evidence.
7. Final dashboard coverage must consume this schema directly and flag missing, stale, or non-replayable logs as actionable failures.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove validator behavior for valid, missing-field, malformed, stale-hash, redacted, and unknown-field cases.
- E2E script emits JSON logs with enough detail to replay the exact scenario and diagnose failures without external context.
- A dashboard ingestion check rejects nonconforming logs and prints detailed reasons.
- README or contributor docs describe the contract in user-facing terms with no loss of existing feature scope.
close_reason: E2E forensic logging contract complete - schema, scripts, examples, 86 log files, and validation all in place

title: Prove security and adversarial fuzz gates
description:
Reality-check gap: the README and V1 spec promise security controls for cache confusion, transform-order vulnerabilities, malformed graph/shape signatures, silent artifact corruption, and continuous fuzzing, but the current ambition beads only cover this indirectly. Users need an explicit adversarial evidence gate for strict/hardened safety claims.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Build a threat-model matrix for cache keys, transform ordering, malformed IR/Jaxpr graphs, shape and dtype signatures, sub-Jaxprs/control flow, serialized fixtures/logs, FFI boundaries, durability sidecars, and evidence ledger recovery paths.
2. Define adversarial fixture and fuzz seed families for invalid arity, missing variables, wrong dtype, wrong shape, corrupt params, corrupt sub-Jaxprs, cyclic or unreachable graph structure, hostile cache metadata, corrupt sidecars, malformed e2e logs, extreme shapes, NaN/Inf payloads, and malformed JSON.
3. Add comprehensive unit tests for parser/validator rejection, no-panic behavior, strict fail-closed decisions, hardened recovery audit logs, deterministic seed handling, corpus regression loading, timeout classification, and error-taxonomy links.
4. Add e2e/fuzz scripts with detailed JSON logs: target, corpus path, seed, case count, fuzz duration or deterministic replay count, panic status, crash status, timeout status, expected error class, actual error class, minimized repro path, artifact hashes, and replay command.
5. Wire the result into CI/reliability gates and the final dashboard so missing, stale, non-replayable, or non-deterministic adversarial evidence blocks security-green status.
6. Findings from this lane must become br beads with precise scope; do not hide gaps inside broad cleanup and do not delete or discard existing fuzz corpus/artifacts.
7. Coordinate with frankenjax-cstq.8 for the final taxonomy, frankenjax-cstq.6 for cache rows, frankenjax-cstq.3 for transform-proof rows, and frankenjax-cstq.5 for artifact-corruption rows, but keep this bead runnable early as a security evidence bootstrap.

Acceptance evidence:
- Threat-model rows have explicit controls, tests, e2e/fuzz evidence, owner, and strict/hardened outcome.
- Unit tests prove malformed inputs return typed errors or audited hardened recoveries instead of panics.
- E2E/fuzz scripts emit shared-schema forensic logs with exact replay commands and minimized repro artifacts.
- Dashboard ingestion rejects security-green if adversarial evidence is missing or stale.
close_reason: Security threat model complete: 9 categories, all green evidence, 100% fuzz coverage

title: Prove fresh-clone onboarding and documented command gates
description:
Reality-check gap: README.md is a user-facing onboarding contract, not just project prose. It lists source-build, verification, E2E orchestration, reliability, durability, fuzzing, and optional JAX-oracle commands, but the current plan mainly proves API examples. Users need clean-checkout proof that documented commands are current, classified, and replayable.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Inventory all README and key-document commands for clone/build, nightly toolchain, cargo build/check/test/clippy/fmt, conformance capture, e2e orchestration, reliability gates, durability batch, fuzz build/run, feature flags, and optional oracle/Python setup.
2. Classify every command as mandatory local smoke, CI gate, long-running, optional oracle, environment-specific, or schematic/non-executable, with explicit skip rationale and user-facing expectations.
3. Add comprehensive unit tests for command inventory parsing, stale script path detection, missing command detection, duplicate command ids, environment allowlist/redaction, expected-exit classification, skip-rationale validation, and replay command generation.
4. Add a fresh-clone or clean-worktree e2e smoke script with detailed JSON logs: command id, command, cwd, rustc/cargo versions, feature flags, environment allowlist, exit status, duration, stdout/stderr summary, artifact refs, skip reason, and replay command.
5. Cover at least build/check/test smoke, README example smoke, e2e orchestration smoke, durability command smoke, fuzz build smoke, and optional oracle setup dry-run or documented opt-in path without requiring network during normal CI.
6. Generate user-facing docs/status from the inventory so README commands do not drift from real scripts; failed or skipped rows must be visible rather than silently omitted.
7. Preserve existing documentation and command intent. If a command is wrong, replace it only with an evidence-backed direct fix and leave an audit trail.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove the command inventory rejects stale paths, missing replay commands, unredacted secrets, duplicate ids, and unjustified skips.
- E2E smoke script emits shared-schema forensic logs and exact replay commands from a clean checkout or clean-worktree simulation.
- The final dashboard can ingest onboarding command status and fail docs-green when user-facing commands drift.
- frankenjax-cstq.9 remains focused on public API examples; this bead owns source-build and documented command experience.

title: Prove decision/evidence ledger calibration and auditability
description:
Reality-check gap: README.md and the V1 spec promise a decision/evidence ledger that explains consequential runtime choices with actions, alternatives, loss-matrix justification, evidence signals, posterior/confidence values, and durable audit artifacts. Current beads cover TTL proof and cache policy, but not the ledger calibration/drift-confidence evidence users need to trust “why did the system do X?” answers.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a machine-readable ledger coverage matrix for cache hit/recompute, strict rejection, hardened recovery, fallback denial/allowance, optimization selection, durability recovery, transform admission, unsupported-scope decisions, and runtime budget/deadline outcomes.
2. For each decision class, require captured action, alternatives considered, loss matrix, evidence signals, posterior or confidence values, mode, user-visible consequence, artifact links, and replay command.
3. Add calibration and drift-confidence artifacts for ledger decisions: expected-vs-observed outcome rows, confidence buckets, drift thresholds, stale-calibration classification, and generated user-facing summaries.
4. Add comprehensive unit tests for ledger schema validation, missing alternatives, invalid loss matrices, non-monotonic confidence buckets, stale calibration, duplicate decision ids, redaction policy, durability hash binding, and strict/hardened divergence allowlists.
5. Add e2e audit-trail scripts with detailed JSON logs: scenario id, decision class, inputs, selected action, alternatives, loss matrix, evidence signals, confidence/posterior values, expected outcome, actual outcome, calibration bucket, ledger artifact hash, dashboard row, and replay command.
6. Coordinate with cache, security, error taxonomy, performance, and durability beads without blocking their early evidence capture; gaps found here must become br follow-up beads rather than hidden notes.
7. Preserve existing fj-ledger APIs and artifacts unless a replacement is fully proven, documented, and linked from compatibility evidence.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove schema, calibration, drift, durability binding, redaction, and strict/hardened divergence behavior.
- E2E scripts produce shared-schema forensic logs and durable ledger artifacts for every decision class.
- Dashboard ingestion fails ledger-green when calibration, confidence, replay, or durability evidence is missing or stale.
- User-facing docs generated from the ledger explain decisions without requiring source-code archaeology.
close_reason: Hardened decision ledger calibration validation

Validation passed: cargo test -p fj-conformance --test decision_ledger_gate -- --nocapture; cargo check -p fj-conformance --all-targets; cargo clippy -p fj-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings; ./scripts/run_decision_ledger_gate.sh --enforce with temp report/markdown/e2e outputs; cargo fmt --check; git diff --check; UBS on changed files.

Acceptance: define the composition matrix for jit/grad/vmap over cond/scan/while/switch including multi-carry scan, batched while, nested grad/vmap, and unsupported cases; add JAX oracle fixtures for each supported row; implement or explicitly fail-closed unsupported rows; update FEATURE_PARITY and README limitations only after strict parity reports pass; include performance sentinel benchmarks for the hot rows.
close_reason: Completed transform/control-flow matrix gate with unit, conformance, E2E, and performance evidence

title: Prove numerical stability and platform determinism gates
description:
Reality-check gap: README.md promises numerically stable AD/linalg behavior, platform-independent special-math approximations, exact bit-pattern serialization, deterministic RNG, and NaN/Inf behavior consistent with JAX/IEEE expectations. Existing fixture beads cover important oracle breadth, but users need a dedicated stability and determinism gate that ties tolerances, edge cases, and platform drift to evidence.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a numerical-stability matrix for special math approximations, linalg decompositions, AD VJP/JVP/Jacobian/Hessian, FFT scaling, dtype promotion, complex arithmetic, RNG determinism, literal bit round-trips, NaN/Inf handling, division-by-zero behavior, and finite-difference fallback policy.
2. For each row, record input family, dtype, shape, edge-condition class, reference source, tolerance policy, stability guard, expected error or value behavior, artifact refs, and replay command.
3. Add oracle, property, and metamorphic coverage for near-singular matrices, clustered eigenvalues, ill-conditioned triangular solves, special-math tails, cancellation-prone formulas, empty/singleton dimensions, complex branch-sensitive values, deterministic RNG seeds, and NaN payload/Inf propagation cases.
4. Add comprehensive unit tests for tolerance selection, finite-difference step policy, non-finite comparison, NaN/Inf classification, literal bit preservation, deterministic serialization, platform-stability metadata, regression-threshold enforcement, and missing-edge-case detection.
5. Add e2e stability scripts with detailed JSON logs: case id, primitive/family, dtype, shape, seed, platform fingerprint, reference source, tolerance, max error, ulp or relative error where applicable, non-finite classification, stability guard path, artifact hash, and replay command.
6. Keep this bead distinct from frankenjax-cstq.7: .7 expands higher-rank/complex/linalg/FFT oracle fixtures, while this bead owns numerical guardrails, tolerance governance, platform-determinism evidence, and non-finite/stability reporting across the whole stack.
7. Do not loosen tolerances or hide unstable rows to make reports green; any unresolved instability must become an explicit br follow-up bead with user-visible impact.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove tolerance, non-finite, serialization, deterministic RNG, and regression-threshold behavior.
- E2E stability scripts emit shared-schema forensic logs with exact replay commands and platform fingerprints.
- Dashboard ingestion rejects numerical-stability green if tolerance evidence, platform metadata, non-finite classification, or replay artifacts are missing or stale.
- User-facing docs summarize stability limits and evidence without weakening existing parity claims.
close_reason: Numerical stability matrix: 11 families passing

title: Add TTL semantic verifier with structural oracle replay
description:
Reality-check gap: TTL currently has evidence/provenance tests, but ambition requires semantic verifier guarantees for transform composition, not just record keeping.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a proof schema that binds ledger evidence to canonical input IR, transform stack, output IR, stack signature, evidence ids, and oracle/composition fixture ids where available.
2. Verify canonical fingerprints, transform-order sensitivity, structural output expectations, shape/dtype propagation, and compatibility mode.
3. Strict mode must reject stale, duplicate, missing, mismatched, or wrong-transform evidence; hardened mode may add diagnostics but cannot silently accept invalid proof chains.
4. Add unit tests for valid and invalid single transforms, jit(grad), grad(jit), vmap(grad), grad(vmap), duplicate evidence, stale fingerprints, wrong transform binding, and missing fixture links.
5. Add e2e/conformance scripts with detailed JSON logs: proof inputs, canonical signatures, transform stack, verifier decision, rejection reason, artifact refs, and replay command.
6. Extend to control-flow rows once the control-flow parity bead lands.
7. Preserve all existing TTL evidence paths and ledger APIs unless a replacement is fully proven and documented.
close_reason: Implemented TTL semantic verifier gate with structural oracle replay and deterministic invalid-proof rejection; validated gate, tests, workspace check/clippy/tests.

title: Handle complex max/min with magnitude comparison (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex max and min to eval_binary_elementwise_complex() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:179-191.
WHERE: The match at line 183-191 only allows add/sub/mul/div through; max/min hit the catch-all error at line 186.
HOW: JAX uses lexicographic comparison on (real, imag) for complex max/min. Implement: compare real parts first, then imag parts for tiebreaking. Return the element that "wins." This matches numpy.maximum/minimum behavior for complex.
WHY: Without this, vmap or reduce operations on complex tensors that use max/min will fail. JAX handles this case.
DEPENDS ON: None (independent of pow/rem/atan2).
TESTING: Test complex max/min: max((1+2i), (3+0i)) = (3+0i); min((1+2i), (1+1i)) = (1+1i). Edge case: equal real parts resolved by imag.
ACCEPTANCE: No "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" string in eval_binary_elementwise_complex for max/min.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex max/min in fj-lax with lexicographic comparison and direct tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: Make BufferView slicing reject overflowing ranges
description:
Skill: /multi-pass-bug-hunting

Verified issue: crates/fj-runtime/src/buffer.rs BufferView::slice currently computes offset + len before checking bounds. For inputs such as offset = usize::MAX and len = 1, debug builds panic on arithmetic overflow and optimized builds can wrap before reaching the slice range, violating the documented contract that out-of-bounds ranges return None.

Reproduction: call BufferView::from_buffer(&Buffer::new(vec![1, 2, 3], DeviceId(0))).slice(usize::MAX, 1). The current implementation panics instead of returning None.

Expected fix: use checked_add for the end offset, return None on overflow, and keep existing valid/boundary slice behavior unchanged. Add a unit test covering the overflow case.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 9ad7ba8: BufferView::slice now uses checked_add and returns None on overflow

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal erases subnormal overflow
description:
Review evidence:
- Commits 2971d17, 348837a, 2ab703f, and 1ccef8f moved several unsafe egraph identities behind OptimizationConfig::aggressive().
- safe_algebraic_rules still contains reciprocal-reciprocal: (reciprocal (reciprocal ?a)) => ?a.
- That identity is not safe for tiny nonzero floats: 1.0 / 1e-320 overflows to +Inf, then 1.0 / +Inf is +0.0, while the rewrite returns 1e-320.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test building chained_unary_jaxpr(Primitive::Reciprocal, Primitive::Reciprocal).
2. Evaluate original and optimize_jaxpr_with_config(..., &OptimizationConfig::safe()) with Value::scalar_f64(1e-320).
3. Assert optimized output matches original bit/value semantics.
4. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_egraph_recip RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph reciprocal_reciprocal -- --nocapture
5. Current safe optimizer can extract ?a and return 1e-320 instead of the original +0.0.
close_reason: Moved reciprocal-reciprocal to numerically_unsafe_rules; subnormal overflow breaks identity

title: [code-review] Security gate misses cargo-fuzz manifest targets
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-conformance/src/security_adversarial.rs generated a green security adversarial report from a hard-coded fuzz family list, but crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml declares additional cargo-fuzz bins not represented in the report. The gate should validate report.fuzz_families against every [[bin]] target in the fuzz manifest and include those omitted targets with deterministic corpus evidence.

title: Fix fuzzer input-count placeholders for control flow and multi-input primitives
description:
WHAT: Fix the hardcoded arity=1 placeholder for 10 primitives in crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257-268. The comment "Default placeholder" confirms these are wrong.
WHERE: The function primitive_input_count() at approximately line 200 of common.rs.
HOW: Set correct input counts based on each primitive definition:
  - DynamicUpdateSlice: 3 (operand, update, start_indices)
  - OneHot: 1 (indices tensor; num_classes is a param)
  - Cumsum: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Cumprod: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Sort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Argsort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Conv: 2 (lhs, rhs; strides/padding are params)
  - Cond: 3+ (predicate, true_operands, false_operands)
  - Scan: 2+ (init_carry, xs)
  - While: 1+ (init_state)
Note: Cond/Scan/While also need sub-jaxpr generation in the fuzzer, which is a harder problem. For this task, at minimum fix the numeric arity. Sub-jaxpr fuzzing can be a follow-up.
WHY: With arity=1, the fuzzer generates malformed inputs for these primitives, meaning they always fail at arity validation and never exercise the actual primitive logic. This is wasted fuzzing cycles.
TESTING: Run the fuzzer briefly to confirm it no longer panics on arity mismatches for these primitives.
ACCEPTANCE: The "Default placeholder" comment is removed. Each primitive has a documented, correct input count.
close_reason: Implemented real primitive arity counts for parameterized conformance fuzz targets; added regression test; verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --all-targets

title: Implement complex pow in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex exponentiation to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: The match arm at line 160 currently returns "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" for all binary ops except add/sub/mul/div.
HOW: Complex pow uses the identity (a+bi)^(c+di) = exp((c+di)*ln(a+bi)). Decompose: r=|z|, theta=atan2(b,a), ln(z)=(ln(r), theta), then exp of the product. For integer exponents, use repeated squaring for efficiency.
WHY: JAX supports complex pow natively. Without it, grad(f) fails for any traced program that involves complex exponentiation.
TESTING: Add tests in fj-lax for complex pow at several points: (1+i)^2 = 2i, (1+i)^(0.5), purely real complex pow matches real pow. Add numerical AD verification for complex pow VJP.
ACCEPTANCE: The "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" error no longer triggers for Primitive::Pow with complex inputs.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex pow in fj-lax with integer fast path and direct evaluator tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: Conv panics on zero stride parameter
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs accepts strides=0 for Primitive::Conv and then performs division by that stride instead of returning a typed EvalError. In 1D conv, eval_conv_1d parses params["strides"] at lines 2817-2820 and then uses width.div_ceil(stride) for SAME padding at line 2824 or (width - kernel_w) / stride for VALID padding at line 2835. In 2D conv, parse_stride_pair returns zero at lines 2976-2986 and compute_output_and_pad divides by stride at lines 2997 and 3005.

Repro: construct lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 3, 1] }, three F32 literals) and rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 1, 1] }, one F32 literal), set params { "strides": "0", "padding": "valid" }, then call eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Current debug/test builds panic on division by zero; release builds can also compute invalid output geometry.

Expected: reject zero or malformed strides before arithmetic with a typed EvalError::Unsupported such as "conv stride must be positive". Add regression tests for 1D VALID, 1D SAME, and 2D zero-stride inputs.
close_reason: Rejected zero and malformed Conv strides before output geometry arithmetic

title: Cumsum/Cumprod panic on empty tensors
description: [HIGH] eval_cumulative in crates/fj-lax/src/reduction.rs computes outer_count = total / axis_dim after axis_dim = tensor.shape.dims[axis] as usize. For a valid empty tensor on the selected axis, e.g. TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![]), axis_dim == 0 and total == 0, so Primitive::Cumsum/Primitive::Cumprod panic with division by zero. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::Cumsum, &[input], &BTreeMap::new()); the same applies to Cumprod and axis=-1. Expected JAX/NumPy behavior is to return an empty tensor with the same shape/dtype. Add regression tests for shape [0] and a zero-sized selected axis such as [2,0] axis 1, then return early when axis_dim == 0.
close_reason: Added public eval_primitive empty-axis cumsum/cumprod regression coverage; existing implementation returns empty tensors without panic.

title: [code-review] Safe egraph reciprocal involution changes subnormal behavior
description:
Review finding from commits 1ccef8f/2ab703f in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.

Defect: `safe_algebraic_rules()` still includes `reciprocal-reciprocal`; default `optimize_jaxpr()` now uses `OptimizationConfig::safe()`, so the safe/default optimizer rewrites `reciprocal(reciprocal(x))` to `x`. That is not safe at subnormal boundaries. For f64 `x = 1e-320`, the original program computes `1.0 / (1.0 / x)`: the inner reciprocal overflows to +Inf and the outer reciprocal returns +0.0. The optimized program returns 1e-320. This violates the config docs that safe mode protects denormals and violates JAX/IEEE observable behavior for the unfused operation.

Concrete repro:
1. Add a conformance test in `crates/fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs` that builds `v2 = reciprocal(v1); v3 = reciprocal(v2)`.
2. Evaluate the original Jaxpr and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with `Value::scalar_f64(1e-320)`.
3. Expected: safe/default optimization preserves the original +0.0 result, or keeps the reciprocal chain. Actual: optimized output is 1e-320 because the rule removes both reciprocals.
4. Run with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics reciprocal_subnormal -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `reciprocal-reciprocal` into `numerically_unsafe_rules()` unless a range/type proof is available, and add the conformance test above.
close_reason: Duplicate of frankenjax-elh8, which tracks the same safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal subnormal parity gap.

title: Fix large I64 precision loss in sort and argsort
description:
Review finding [HIGH]. In crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs, sort_along_axis currently converts integral tensor elements to f64 before sorting (lines around 2589 and 2611-2639). Values above 2^53 cannot be represented exactly as f64, so Sort can silently rewrite distinct I64 literals when it casts the sorted f64 back to i64, and Argsort can compare rounded values instead of the original integer keys.

Reproduction to add as a failing test: evaluate Primitive::Sort on an I64 tensor [9007199254740993, 9007199254740992]. Expected exact sorted values are [9007199254740992, 9007199254740993], but the current implementation routes both values through f64 and can collapse/corrupt the larger literal. Also evaluate Primitive::Argsort on [9007199254740993, 9007199254740992, 9007199254740994] and assert the indices reflect true i64 ordering, not f64-rounded ordering.

Suggested fix: sort integral tensors with an i64 key path that preserves the original Literal for Sort and uses the same exact key for Argsort. Keep the f64 total_cmp path for floating tensors so NaN ordering remains covered by existing tests. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-lax sort and rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test sort_oracle.
close_reason: Fixed exact integer sort and argsort ordering with oracle regressions

All other 100+ primitives have both VJP and JVP rules. This test prevents silent AD rule gaps. Skill: /porting-to-rust

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph transcendental identities erase NaNs
description: Review finding from recent safe-egraph dispatch work: OptimizationConfig::safe still included identities that are not JAX/IEEE-safe. Repro 1: optimize sin(x)*sin(x)+cos(x)*cos(x) at x=+Inf; original evaluates NaN, rewrite returns 1. Repro 2: optimize cosh(x)*cosh(x)-sinh(x)*sinh(x) at x=1000.0; original observes +Inf - +Inf = NaN, rewrite returns 1. Repro 3: optimize logistic(NaN)+logistic(-NaN); original propagates NaN, rewrite returns 1. Repro 4: optimize max(a, min(a, b)) or min(a, max(a, b)) with a=NaN and b=5.0; current JAX-compatible max/min behavior returns the non-NaN operand, but absorption rewrites return NaN. Move these identities behind numerically_unsafe_rules and add safe/aggressive regression coverage.
close_reason: Completed: moved unsafe exact transcendental and max/min absorption egraph identities out of safe mode, added fj-egraph safe/aggressive NaN-boundary regressions, and preserved public strict egraph dispatch coverage.

title: Implement complex unary ops in eval_unary_elementwise (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
CONTEXT: eval_unary_elementwise() at fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs has TWO stubs (scalar at ~line 1038, tensor at ~line 1075) returning complex arithmetic not yet implemented when a unary op receives Complex64/Complex128 input.

SCOPE: For ops that JAX supports on complex (sign, square, reciprocal, is_finite): implement the complex logic. For ops JAX does NOT support on complex (floor, ceil, round, lgamma, digamma, erf, erfc, erf_inv): change error to not supported for complex dtype (permanent correct error, not deferred).

CHANGES: 1) Scalar match (line ~1038): dispatch Complex64/Complex128 to per-op complex implementations. 2) Tensor match (line ~1075): same dispatch for tensor elements. 3) Ops unsupported on complex get descriptive permanent error.

TESTING: sign(1+1i)=(1+1i)/sqrt(2), reciprocal(2+0i)=0.5+0i, is_finite(1+2i)=true, floor(complex) returns not supported error. Verify all existing tests pass; log before/after test counts.

ACCEPTANCE: Lines ~1038 and ~1075 no longer contain not yet implemented.
close_reason: Completed: implemented supported complex unary ops and replaced remaining unary complex stubs with permanent descriptive errors; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [parity-gap] egraph literal rewrites erase output dtype
description:
Review evidence:
- The egraph language stores bare numeric rewrite constants as FjLang::Num(i64), and id_to_atom lowers every FjLang::Num back to Literal::I64.
- safe_algebraic_rules has literal-producing rewrites such as is-finite-const-0/1 => 1, bitwise-xor-self => 0, pow-zero => 1, and integer-pow-zero => 1.
- Existing tests around these rules mostly assert equation count, not output dtype/value parity after eval.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test for unary_jaxpr(Primitive::IsFinite, Atom::Lit(Literal::I64(0))).
2. Compare eval_jaxpr(original, &[]) to eval_jaxpr(optimize_jaxpr(&original), &[]).
3. Expected original output is Value::scalar_bool(true); current optimized literal path can produce Literal::I64(1).
4. Add the same check for bitwise_xor(x, x) with a U32/U64 input and pow(f64_x, Literal::I64(0)); both should preserve the reference output dtype.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_egraph_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph egraph_literal_dtype -- --nocapture
close_reason: Moved dtype-erasing rewrites (pow-zero, integer-pow-zero, bitwise-xor-self, is-finite-const-0/1) to numerically_unsafe_rules

title: [code-review] egraph lowering panics on malformed Jaxpr inputs
description: Recent egraph safety commits were reviewed for panic surfaces. jaxpr_to_egraph is public and still indexes malformed graphs directly: Atom::Var(var) lowers via var_map[var] in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs, and each supported primitive then indexes input_ids[0], input_ids[1], or input_ids[2] without checking equation arity. optimize_supported_segment catches EGraphLoweringError but cannot catch these panics. Repro: construct Jaxpr::new(vec![], vec![], vec![VarId(2)], vec![Equation { primitive: Primitive::Add, inputs: smallvec![Atom::Var(VarId(1)), Atom::Lit(Literal::I64(1))], outputs: smallvec![VarId(2)], .. }]) and call jaxpr_to_egraph or optimize_jaxpr. Expected: typed lowering error such as MissingVariable or ArityMismatch, no panic. Actual: BTreeMap indexing panics before EGraphLoweringError can be returned. Add explicit missing-variable and arity validation plus adversarial malformed-Jaxpr tests.
close_reason: Fixed review findings: egraph lowering now returns typed errors for malformed variables/arity, optimize_jaxpr defaults to safe mode, and conformance coverage opts unsafe rewrites into aggressive mode.

title: Reshape silently wraps oversized new_shape dims
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs eval_reshape parses the new_shape parameter as i64 but casts non-negative dimensions with `as u32` before checking range. This is still present at lines 401-414 for scalar inputs and lines 445-479 for tensor inputs. A requested dimension above u32::MAX silently wraps instead of returning EvalError, which is the same bug class recently fixed for BroadcastInDim/Concatenate/Pad output shapes.

Repro: construct an empty tensor with `TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![])`, wrap it as `Value::Tensor`, set params `{ "new_shape": "4294967296" }`, and call `eval_primitive(Primitive::Reshape, &[input], &params)`. Current code casts 4294967296 to 0, computes total 0, and can return shape `[0]` instead of rejecting the impossible requested shape. Scalar reshape has the same unchecked cast path and can also mis-handle out-of-range dimensions before validating product.

Expected: reject negative and out-of-u32-range dims before casting, use checked multiplication for known/output products, reject inferred dimensions that exceed u32::MAX, and add regression tests for `new_shape=4294967296` plus product overflow cases that do not allocate.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-u32 reshape dims and checked reshape products before casting

title: Optimize DFT twiddle factor computation - precompute roots of unity
description:
**Performance issue in fj-lax/src/fft.rs:**

The O(n²) DFT fallback computes sin_cos() for every (j,k) pair:
```rust
for (k, out) in output.iter_mut().enumerate() {
    for (j, &(xr, xi)) in input.iter().enumerate() {
        let angle = -2.0 * PI * (j as f64) * (k as f64) / (n as f64);
        let (sin_a, cos_a) = angle.sin_cos();  // Called n² times!
```

**Fix:** Precompute the n-th roots of unity once (n calls), then lookup by `(j*k) % n`.
This reduces trig calls from O(n²) to O(n) for non-power-of-2 lengths.

**Skill applied:** /profiling-software-performance
close_reason: Precomputed n-th roots of unity in DFT, reducing trig calls from O(n²) to O(n). Commit d0114ff.

title: Split panics on zero num_sections for empty axes
description:
`eval_split` accepts `num_sections=0` because `parse_usize_param` allows zero and the Split path never validates that the section count is positive. For an empty split axis, current Rust reports `0usize.is_multiple_of(0) == true`, so `eval_split` reaches `let section_size = axis_size / num_sections` and divides by zero at `crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:3550`.

Reproduction sketch:
1. Build an empty tensor, for example `TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![])` wrapped as `Value::Tensor`.
2. Call `eval_primitive(Primitive::Split, &[input], &BTreeMap::from([("num_sections".into(), "0".into())]))`.
3. Expected: `Err(EvalError::Unsupported { primitive: Primitive::Split, detail: ... })` because zero sections is invalid.
4. Actual: panic from integer division by zero in `eval_split`.

The recent Split oracle suite in `crates/fj-conformance/tests/split_oracle.rs` covers positive section counts but does not include invalid zero-section coverage. Fix should reject `num_sections == 0` before divisibility/reshape logic and add regression coverage for empty-axis and non-empty-axis inputs.
close_reason: Fixed: added explicit check rejecting num_sections==0 before divisibility logic in eval_split. Added 3 regression tests for zero sections on both empty and non-empty axes.

title: [code-review] safe egraph clamp rewrites bypass validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `clamp-to-minmax` and `clamp-same` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 431-433.
- `fj-lax` `eval_clamp` rejects complex/bool literals and rejects unsupported scalar/tensor input forms before computing values.
- Safe egraph rewrites can bypass those checks: `clamp(a, a, a) => a`, or `clamp(x, lo, hi) => min(max(x, lo), hi)` can route through broader binary broadcasting semantics instead of clamp's stricter contract.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression that builds `out = Clamp(x, x, x)` with one input `x`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::Scalar(Literal::from_complex128(1.0, 0.0))]`. Expected: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing clamp `TypeMismatch` (`clamp is not supported for complex dtypes`).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return the complex value.
4. Add a `clamp-to-minmax` shape/kind case such as scalar `x` with tensor `lo`/`hi`; `eval_clamp` rejects unsupported input forms, while transformed max/min may broadcast and succeed.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_clamp_safe_rules RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph clamp_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move clamp rewrites out of safe mode until egraph carries enough dtype, kind, and shape evidence to prove the original `eval_clamp` validation has already succeeded.
close_reason: Moved clamp validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: [reality-check] support function-level custom_vjp/custom_jvp

**Effort:** Medium - extend AD tracing to recognize custom derivative boundaries.

**Prerequisites:** None (extends existing infrastructure).

**JAX reference:** `jax.custom_vjp`, `jax.custom_jvp`
