title: Make batched fixture timeouts return without joining timed-out workers
description:
Review finding [HIGH]. Commit 0985d27 changed run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs to record timed-out worker handles, but the function then joins all timed_out_handles before returning (current lines around 1145-1186). That means case_timeout does not bound wall-clock runtime: a timed-out case with a long or hung worker still blocks the whole batched run at the final join.

Reproduction to add: construct a TransformFixtureBundle with one case having simulated_delay_ms = 5000 and BatchRunnerConfig { case_timeout: Duration::from_millis(1) }. Measure elapsed time for run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched. Expected behavior for a timeout runner is to return a Timeout report promptly after roughly the configured timeout; current behavior waits for the delayed worker during the final join and returns only after the worker finishes. A genuinely hung worker would hang the harness indefinitely despite emitting a timeout report.

Suggested fix: preserve bounded fan-out without joining timed-out workers on the critical return path. Options include a scoped worker pool with cooperative cancellation, a persistent bounded runner, or clearly documenting and renaming the timeout as classification-only. Add an elapsed-time regression test so future code cannot reintroduce timeout-as-blocking-join semantics. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance test_batch_runner.
close_reason: Detached timed-out batch workers from the return path and added elapsed-time regression coverage

title: Batch runner timeout leaks between test cases
description:
## Problem
The `test_batch_runner_marks_timeout` test is failing because timeout state leaks between test cases.

Test setup:
- Case 1: `batch_timeout` with `simulated_delay_ms: 200`, timeout of 50ms → Expected: Timeout
- Case 2: `batch_fast_after_timeout` with `simulated_delay_ms: 0`, same 50ms timeout → Expected: Pass

Actual behavior: Case 2 is being classified as Timeout instead of Pass.

## Location
`crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs:2203` - `test_batch_runner_marks_timeout`

## Root cause
The batch runner likely isn't resetting timeout state between cases, or there's a timing issue with how the timeout check is implemented.

## Fix needed
Review `run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched` to ensure timeout tracking is per-case, not cumulative.

Replace with validated shape/result paths and NaN-safe comparator behavior that matches legacy JAX oracle expectations; add focused parity tests for scalar/empty-dim/NaN edge cases.

title: [code-review] public compile options can spoof custom VJP rule keys

Repro: create a custom_vjp wrapper, read its rule key from an in-crate test, then call compose(the same Jaxpr, vec![Transform::Grad]).with_compile_option("custom_vjp_rule_key", &wrapped.rule_key).call([3.0]). The plain composed grad uses the wrapper's private VJP rule instead of ordinary AD, so wrapper-scoped derivative callbacks can be injected through user compile options. Strip reserved custom-derivative keys from user compile options before dispatch and add a regression test.

title: Avoid holding LRU order lock during backend eviction
description:
Skill: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer

Verified hazard: crates/fj-cache/src/eviction.rs LruCache::enforce_budget holds the internal order Mutex while invoking CacheBackend::stats and CacheBackend::evict. CacheBackend is a public trait for arbitrary backends, so calling backend code while holding the wrapper lock creates a reentrancy/deadlock hazard if a backend observes or re-enters the LRU wrapper, logs through shared cache state, or blocks behind code that needs the order lock.

Reproduction target: add a custom test backend whose stats/evict methods try_lock the LruCache order mutex. On the current implementation, inserting beyond max_entries or max_bytes fails because enforce_budget still holds order while calling into the backend.

Expected fix: shorten the order critical section, pop eviction keys into local values, drop the lock, and only then call backend stats/evict. Keep LRU ordering behavior unchanged and add a regression test that proves backend calls are made without the order lock held.
close_reason: Completed: moved LRU budget eviction backend calls outside the order mutex critical section and added a reentrancy regression test proving backend stats/evict run without the order lock held.

Repro: optimize a Jaxpr for (x * y) + (x * z) with x=1e308, y=2.0, z=-2.0. JAX/IEEE evaluates the original as +Inf + -Inf = NaN, but factor can rewrite it to x * (y + z) = 0. Move distributive/factor and non-associative floating association rules behind numerically_unsafe_rules, and add safe/aggressive regression coverage.

Audit these clusters, refactor any real runtime risk, and document or suppress verified false positives.

Verified fj-cache/fj-ad already recover poisoned locks, fj-trace Rc<RefCell> tracing borrows are single-threaded and short-lived, and fj-conformance simulated delay is synchronous fixture timeout plumbing rather than async blocking.

title: Conv trace shape inference panics on zero 2D stride
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs Conv shape inference parses params["strides"] into usize values but does not reject zero in the rank-4 path. Repro: create a SimpleTraceContext with lhs ShapedArray { dtype: F64, shape: [1,3,3,1] } and rhs ShapedArray { dtype: F64, shape: [1,1,1,1] }, then call process_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs_id, rhs_id], params) with padding="same" and strides="0,1" or "0,0". Expected: Err(TraceError::InvalidPrimitiveParam or ShapeInferenceFailed) matching fj-lax runtime stride validation. Actual: the 2D path assigns stride_h=0 at lines around 1490-1492, then SAME padding reaches height.div_ceil(stride_h) around line 1522 and panics in debug/test builds. The 1D path also uses strides[0].max(1), silently accepting strides="0" instead of rejecting it, so the fix should share strict positive stride parsing across 1D and 2D trace inference and add regression tests for 1D zero stride and 2D zero height/width stride.
close_reason: Fixed: removed .max(1) workaround in 1D, added explicit zero stride validation in both 1D and 2D paths. Added 5 regression tests.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph pow-two rewrites erase promoted dtypes
description:
Review evidence from the last-six-hours egraph safety commits:
- 14fcdc9 moved add/sub zero, mul/div/pow/integer_pow one, and max/min commutativity/associativity out of safe mode, but safe_algebraic_rules still keeps pow-two and integer-pow-two in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.
- Primitive::Pow evaluates through fj-lax binary dtype promotion. For a U32 input and an I64 literal exponent 2, promote_dtype(U32, I64) => I64, so the original Jaxpr returns Value::Scalar(Literal::I64(49)).
- The safe egraph rule pow-two rewrites pow(x, 2) to mul(x, x). Evaluating Mul over two U32 operands returns Value::Scalar(Literal::U32(49)), so safe/default optimization changes the observable dtype.
- Primitive::IntegerPow currently returns F64 for numeric scalar/tensor inputs. The safe egraph rule integer-pow-two rewrites integer_pow(x, exponent=2) to mul(x, x), so a U32 input changes from F64(49.0) to U32(49).

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add cases to numerical_safety_mode_preserves_promoted_dtype_identity_boundaries in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs for Pow(VarId(1), Literal::I64(2)) and integer_pow_jaxpr(2) with args [Value::scalar_u32(7)].
2. Evaluate the original with eval_jaxpr; expected outputs are I64(49) for Pow and F64(49.0) for IntegerPow under the current fj-lax contracts.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe()) and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr); current safe/default can emit Mul(var,var) and return U32(49).
4. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_pow_two_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph numerical_safety_mode_preserves_promoted_dtype_identity_boundaries -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: move pow-two and integer-pow-two to numerically_unsafe_rules until egraph lowering can prove and preserve the result dtype, or replace them with typed rewrites that keep the promoted output contract.
close_reason: Moved pow-two and integer-pow-two out of safe mode; added promoted dtype regression coverage.

title: [parity-gap] reduce_window max/min still skip NaNs
description:
Review evidence:
- Commit 521de15 changed Primitive::Max/Min and ReduceMax/ReduceMin to JAX-style NaN propagation.
- crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs reduce_window_accumulate_literal still uses f64::max/f64::min via (*value).max(input) and (*value).min(input), so sliding-window max/min keep Rust maxNum/minNum behavior and can return a non-NaN from a window containing NaN.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a conformance test in crates/fj-conformance/tests/reduce_window_oracle.rs using Primitive::ReduceWindow with params reduce_op=max, window_dimensions=2, window_strides=1, padding=VALID and input Value::vector_f64(&[1.0, f64::NAN, 3.0]).
2. Assert both output windows are NaN, matching JAX lax.reduce_window(..., lax.max).
3. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_reduce_window_nan RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test reduce_window_oracle reduce_window_nan -- --nocapture
4. Current code returns non-NaN values because reduce_window_accumulate_literal uses .max/.min instead of the JAX NaN-propagating helpers.
close_reason: Fixed reduce_window max/min to use jax_max_f64/jax_min_f64 helpers for NaN propagation. Added 4 conformance tests.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph trig identities erase NaN boundaries

For inputs like x=+Inf or NaN, JAX/Rust evaluation of the original program observes NaN, while safe egraph extraction can choose constant 1. Repro: build a Jaxpr for add(mul(sin(x),sin(x)),mul(cos(x),cos(x))), run compose(..., vec![Transform::Jit]).with_mode(Strict).with_egraph_optimization(true).call([Value::scalar_f64(f64::INFINITY)]); expected NaN boundary preserved, observed risk: Some(1.0) after rewrite.

title: Harden dynamic_update_slice empty huge-shape stride handling

title: Default vmap pad interior padding in BatchTrace
description: BatchTrace pad batching currently requires padding_interior even though fj-lax eval and fj-trace shape inference default omitted interior padding to zero. Add default handling and regression coverage so vmap over pad preserves the scoped API contract.

title: Bound batched fixture timeout thread fan-out
description: The fj-conformance batched transform fixture runner in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs currently clones the entire bundle, spawns one OS thread per case up front, and then waits on each receiver with a timeout. Large oracle bundles can therefore create hundreds of threads immediately, and timed-out cases keep running in detached background threads. Change run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched so it starts each case only when it is being waited on and joins successful case threads after receiving their result, preserving report order while preventing all cases from fanning out at once. Add regression coverage with multiple delayed cases to prove timeout reporting still preserves case order. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance test_batch_runner, rch cargo check -p fj-conformance --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Changed the batched transform fixture runner to start each case only when waiting on it, join completed worker threads, retain timeout reporting order, and added delayed-plus-fast regression coverage. Validated fmt, UBS, targeted fj-conformance test, fj-conformance check/clippy, and full workspace tests via rch.

title: [parity-gap] public egraph cbrt triple rewrite is algebraically wrong
description: Review finding from commits 9c21ee1 and recent egraph safety work: public strict dispatch now routes egraph_optimize=true through the safe optimizer, but safe_algebraic_rules still contains cbrt(cbrt(cbrt(x))) => cbrt(x). That identity is false for ordinary finite inputs: x=64 evaluates to cbrt(cbrt(4.0)) in the original program, while the rewrite returns 4.0. Repro: compose a Jaxpr with three Cbrt equations under Transform::Jit, enable with_egraph_optimization(true), and call with Value::scalar_f64(64.0). Expected strict result is nested cbrt semantics, not cbrt(x). Remove the invalid rewrite and add public strict egraph conformance coverage.
close_reason: Removed the invalid cbrt(cbrt(cbrt(x))) rewrite, replaced the old rule-shape assertion with semantic preservation coverage, and added public strict egraph API conformance coverage.

title: [reality-check] support jax.checkpoint/remat for memory-efficient AD
description:
JAX's checkpoint (aka remat) enables memory-efficient gradient computation by recomputing forward-pass intermediates during the backward pass instead of storing them.

**Gap:** FrankenJAX tape-based AD stores all intermediates. For large models, this limits batch size.

**Effort:** Medium - requires modifying fj-ad tape structure to support checkpoint boundaries.

**Prerequisites:** None (works on CPU).

**JAX reference:** `jax.checkpoint`, `jax.remat`
close_reason: Implemented: checkpoint() wrapper in fj-api with grad_jaxpr_with_cotangent() helper. Re-runs forward during backward for memory efficiency. 3 tests verify semantics: forward preservation, gradient correctness, memory savings reporting. See commits 4911b5d, 36326ce.

title: [code-review] safe egraph complex rewrites skip dtype validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `real-complex`, `imag-complex`, and `complex-real-imag` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 436-438.
- `fj-lax` `eval_real`/`eval_imag` reject non-complex inputs, and `eval_complex` validates part dtypes/shapes before constructing a complex value.
- Safe egraph extraction can remove those validation operations and return an input variable directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression in `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` or `crates/fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs` that builds `out = Complex(Real(z), Imag(z))` with one input `z`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_f64(3.0)]`. Expected under the current fj-lax contract: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing `TypeMismatch` from `Real`/`Imag` because the input is not complex.
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to just `z` and return `Value::scalar_f64(3.0)`, converting an input validation error into success.
4. Add a second case for `Real(Complex(r, i))` with `r = Value::scalar_f64(1.0)` and `i = Value::scalar_bool(true)`. Original `Complex` should reject the bool part; safe/default can rewrite to `r`.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_complex_safe_rules RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph complex_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move the complex safe rewrites to `numerically_unsafe_rules` or a typed/validated lowering phase that proves complex input and part validity before eliminating operations.
close_reason: Moved complex validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: Harden BufferView slice range overflow
description: Multi-pass bug hunt found crates/fj-runtime/src/buffer.rs BufferView::slice computes offset + len before validating bounds. For adversarial or saturated usize inputs this can panic in debug builds due integer overflow, and in optimized builds can wrap before the subsequent slice expression panics or misclassifies the range. Replace the addition with checked_add so out-of-range or overflowing slices return None as documented. Add a regression test using usize::MAX to prove BufferView::slice is panic-free and returns None for overflowing ranges. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-runtime, rch cargo check -p fj-runtime --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-runtime --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Hardened BufferView::slice with checked_add so overflowing offset+len returns None without panic; regression test covers usize::MAX overflow; workspace fmt/check/clippy/test pass on current HEAD.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_dist: zero-distance + symmetry + triangle inequality
description: tensor_dist(a, b, p) = norm(a-b, p). MR: dist(x, x, p) = 0, dist(a, b, p) = dist(b, a, p) (symmetry), Minkowski triangle inequality dist(a, b, p) <= dist(a, c, p) + dist(c, b, p) for p>=1. No MR currently exercises these.

title: ReduceWindow same padding is offset incorrectly
description: eval_reduce_window computes SAME output length with ceil(input/stride) but starts every window at out_index * stride, so padding is effectively only on the high side. JAX/XLA SAME semantics split total padding with low padding floor(total/2), so examples like input [1,2,3,4,5], window=3, stride=1 should produce [3,6,9,12,9] instead of [6,9,12,9,5]. Implement low-padding offsets for SAME in rank-N reduce_window and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Implemented centered SAME padding offsets for reduce_window and added rank-1/rank-2 regressions

title: [modes-of-reasoning] numerical-stability-vs-JIT-correctness
description:
E-graph rewrites (exp-log, div-self, reassociation) are mathematically valid but numerically treacherous near domain boundaries. Eager vs JIT diverge on edge cases (0, denormal, NaN, Inf). Conformance detects drift post-hoc but cannot prevent unsafe rewrites.

Key locations:
- fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:269-324 (rewrite rules without domain guards)
- fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:219-222 (underflow-prone complex reciprocal)
- fj-conformance/src/numerical_stability.rs:857-920 (tolerance enforcement gap)

Suggested work:
1. Add domain predicates to e-graph rules (div-self only when provably non-zero)
2. Implement numerical safety mode that disables associativity rewrites
3. Extend conformance gate to compare JIT vs eager on edge-case inputs
close_reason: Added OptimizationConfig with numerical_safety_mode flag in ceea42f. Safe mode disables 6 unsafe rewrites: exp-log, div-self, mul-reciprocal, div-mul-cancel, log-product, log-quotient.

title: Conv SAME padding panics on empty spatial dimensions
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs Conv SAME padding assumes the computed output spatial dimension is nonzero. For empty spatial axes, width.div_ceil(stride) or input_size.div_ceil(stride) can return 0, then line 3123 and helper line 3324 compute out_w - 1 / out - 1 and underflow in debug/test builds. Repro 1D: lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F64, Shape { dims: vec![1,0,1] }, vec![]), rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F64, Shape { dims: vec![1,1,1] }, vec![1.0]), params { padding="same", strides="1" }, then eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Expected: either an empty output tensor with shape [1,0,1] or a typed EvalError, but no panic. Actual: SAME geometry computes out_w=0 and underflows at ((out_w - 1) * stride + kernel_w). The 2D path uses compute_output_and_pad and has the same out == 0 underflow for any empty height/width with SAME padding. Add regression tests for 1D width=0 and 2D height/width=0.
close_reason: Fixed: added early return for input_size=0 in compute_output_and_pad and 1D conv path. Added 5 regression tests for empty spatial dims.

title: Hoist reduce_window input strides
description: eval_reduce_window recomputes row-major input stride multipliers inside every window element. Precompute checked input strides once per call and reuse them in flat-index calculation while preserving overflow errors and iteration order.
close_reason: Hoisted reduce_window input stride calculation, added reduce_window Criterion baseline, and aligned the transform fixture with typed I64 reduce_window output

title: Replace e-graph not-yet-supported comment with principled UnsupportedPrimitive categorization
description:
CONTEXT: crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:598 has comment Shape ops require params -- not yet supported followed by 34 primitives returning UnsupportedPrimitive. The not yet wording is misleading -- these are excluded BY DESIGN.

CHANGES REQUIRED:
  1. Replace comment with accurate doc: these primitives are architecturally excluded because the s-expression egraph cannot represent parameterized operations (shapes, axes, sub-jaxprs).
  2. Split the 34-primitive catch-all into categorized groups with per-group doc comments:
     - Shape manipulation (13): Reshape, Slice, DynamicSlice, Gather, Scatter, Transpose, BroadcastInDim, Concatenate, Pad, Rev, Squeeze, Split, ExpandDims
     - Linear algebra (5): Cholesky, Qr, Svd, TriangularSolve, Eigh
     - FFT (4): Fft, Ifft, Rfft, Irfft
     - Control flow (4): Cond, Scan, While, Switch
     - Sorting (2): Sort, Argsort
     - Convolution (1): Conv
     - Index/utility (3): Iota, BroadcastedIota, DynamicUpdateSlice
     - Type conversion (2): BitcastConvertType, ReducePrecision
     - Cumulative (2): Cumsum, Cumprod
     - Windowed (1): ReduceWindow
     - Encoding (1): OneHot
  3. Add ExclusionReason enum or string field to EGraphLoweringError::UnsupportedPrimitive so error messages say e.g. reshape excluded: requires shape parameters (not representable in algebraic e-graph).
  4. Add test: for each category, verify the error message contains the category reason.

TESTING: Existing egraph tests must still pass. New test: construct Jaxpr with each excluded primitive, call jaxpr_to_egraph, assert error message contains category keyword. Log all 34 exclusion messages.

ACCEPTANCE: The string not yet supported gone. Each exclusion has a documented reason. Error messages are informative.
close_reason: Centralized egraph exclusion metadata by category, surfaced reasons in UnsupportedPrimitive errors, added full exclusion-message coverage, and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-egraph plus workspace check/clippy/fmt

title: Repair fj-conformance fuzz coverage for pmap enum additions
description:
testing-fuzzing audit finding from cod_4.

Evidence:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_cod_4_fuzz RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml` fails with E0004 in `crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257`: missing `Primitive::Psum`, `Primitive::Pmean`, `Primitive::AllGather`, `Primitive::AllToAll`, and `Primitive::AxisIndex`.
- `fj-core::Primitive` defines those pmap collective variants at `crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:196-201`; `common.rs` `ALL_PRIMITIVES` and `primitive_arity` only cover pre-pmap variants.
- `fj-core::Transform` includes `Transform::Pmap`, but `sample_transform` in `common.rs:241-246` samples only `Jit`, `Grad`, and `Vmap`, so dispatch/composition fuzzing never exercises the V1 pmap fail-closed path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update the fuzz helper manually so primitive sampling and arity handling are exhaustive for the current `Primitive` enum, including pmap collectives.
- Include `Transform::Pmap` in transform fuzz sampling with expectations that pmap dispatch fails closed rather than panicking.
- Add/extend helper-level tests or compile checks so future enum additions break clearly.
- Verify with `rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml`.
close_reason: Fixed: fuzz helper now covers pmap collective primitives and samples Transform::Pmap; added fuzz_common test target. Verified cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml --all-targets and cargo test --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml --test fuzz_common via rch. cargo test -p fj-conformance is blocked by unrelated stale README artifact hash in e2e_forensic_log_contract.

title: Add NaN/Inf gradient propagation tests for AD edge cases
description:
CONTEXT: The AD engine (fj-ad) has no tests verifying how NaN and Inf propagate through VJP/JVP rules. JAX propagates NaN faithfully: grad(sin)(NaN) = NaN, grad(exp)(Inf) = Inf. Silent NaN-to-zero or Inf-to-zero conversion in gradient rules would be a correctness bug that goes undetected.

SCOPE: Add targeted tests in crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs verifying:
  1. NaN propagation: grad(sin)(NaN)=NaN, grad(cos)(NaN)=NaN, grad(exp)(NaN)=NaN, grad(log)(NaN)=NaN, grad(tanh)(NaN)=NaN, grad(x^2)(NaN)=NaN
  2. Inf propagation: grad(exp)(Inf)=Inf, grad(log)(0+)=Inf or -Inf, grad(x^2)(Inf)=Inf, grad(1/x)(0)=(-Inf or NaN)
  3. Mixed: grad(x*y) where x=NaN, y=finite
  4. Backward pass with NaN cotangent: vjp(sin, 1.0, NaN) should produce NaN gradient
  5. JVP with NaN tangent: jvp(sin, 1.0, NaN) should produce NaN output tangent

TESTING: Each test checks is_nan() or is_infinite() on the gradient output. Use descriptive assertion messages. Log each test case.

ACCEPTANCE: All NaN/Inf propagation tests pass. No gradient rule silently converts NaN to 0 or Inf to finite.
close_reason: Already covered by AD NaN/Inf propagation tests; verified in full workspace test run

title: Extend scalar dtype promotion oracle to half precision
description: dtype_promotion_matches_jax still excludes f16 and bf16 scalar cases as a known gap. Include f16/bf16 in the scalar oracle set, update the stale gap comment, and fix any implementation mismatch revealed by the JAX fixture.
close_reason: Extended scalar dtype promotion oracle coverage to f16/bf16; dtype_promotion_matches_jax now exercises 128 cases and workspace gates pass.

title: Reduce comparison vector allocation overhead
description: Optimize fj-lax tensor comparison evaluator hot paths by preallocating bool output vectors and pushing results directly instead of collecting iterator Result<Vec<_>>, preserving scalar/tensor comparison semantics and shape checks.
close_reason: Completed comparison preallocated output loop optimization; eq_1k benchmark improved about 50.4%

title: Add torch.float_power subprocess conformance oracle
description: /testing-conformance-harnesses found tensor_float_power only has ft-api alias unit tests, while nearby parity-sensitive ops such as ldexp, fmax/fmin, nextafter, and IEEE edge ops have live PyTorch subprocess conformance. Acceptance: add a focused torch.float_power subprocess oracle in ft-conformance that covers positive, negative, fractional, signed-zero, infinity, and NaN cases; compare FrankenTorch tensor_float_power outputs to torch.float_power with NaN/signed-zero-aware scalar decoding; validate via focused RCH ft-conformance test.
close_reason: Completed: added torch.float_power subprocess conformance and fixed signed-zero fractional pow parity; code shipped in beb4005.

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] last 3 user-facing AutogradError diagnostics still unpinned (TensorRootDoesNotRequireGrad, TensorGraphConsumed, TensorRequiresGradNonFloating)
description:
Earlier hcyx + 5zjw pinned 6 user-facing AutogradError variants. Three more remain that are equally part of the public-contract surface:

  1. TensorRootDoesNotRequireGrad { node }
     'cannot run tensor backward: root node 0 does not require grad'
     Tensor-path analog of RootDoesNotRequireGrad — surfaces in
     code that calls tensor_backward on a non-grad root.

  2. TensorGraphConsumed
     'cannot run tensor backward: graph already consumed by a previous backward pass'
     Tensor-path analog of GraphConsumed — IDE quick-fix code
     parses both forms.

  3. TensorRequiresGradNonFloating { node, dtype }
     'requires_grad_ expects floating-point dtype at node 5 but found I32'
     Surfaces when user tries requires_grad_ on integer tensor.
     The {dtype:?} format means a Debug-derive change on DType
     would silently break this — pinning catches that.

Acceptance: 3 insta snapshot tests pinning the exact error strings; ft-autograd lib tests +3, snapshots/ +3 .snap files. Combined with hcyx + 5zjw, 9 user-facing AutogradError variants will be pinned.
close_reason: Shipped in c8555fe — last 3 user-facing AutogradError diagnostics pinned (TensorRootDoesNotRequireGrad, TensorGraphConsumed, TensorRequiresGradNonFloating). ft-autograd now has 9 *_diagnostic_snapshot tests covering all user-facing variants, including the DType Debug-derive guard. All 9 pass.

title: Validate CSR crow_indices values
description: Ensure crow_indices starts at 0, has non-negative monotonic values, and reject invalid crow entries to avoid out-of-bounds CSR access.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add E2E training test for bilinear interpolate-based upsampler
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression test that wires Conv2d + bilinear interpolate (upsampling 2x) + MSE into an Adam training loop. Validates the recently fixed tensor_interpolate bilinear autograd path (frankentorch-3t1t) end-to-end through a learnable conv with the upsampling chain.

Setup:
- 1x1x2x2 learnable input
- conv2d (1->1, 3x3, padding 1) → still 1x1x2x2
- interpolate (bilinear, scale 2) → 1x1x4x4
- MSE against target 4x4 ones tensor
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: Add STFT and ISTFT for spectral/signal processing
close_reason: Completed

title: tensor_pdist severs autograd; composable through index_select for finite p > 0
description:
tensor_pdist at lib.rs:4349 computes pairwise L_p distances between rows of a 2-D input. For finite p > 0 the math is composable through autograd-aware primitives (index_select pairs of rows, abs, pow, sum_dim, pow). For p = inf it's argmax-routing; for p = 0 it's a non-differentiable counting metric.

Strategy: build row_i and row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 each, use tensor_index_select to gather row pairs, then compute:
   diff = left - right
   per_pair = (sum_k |diff[k]|^p)^(1/p)
All ops autograd-aware. Add fail-loud guards for p == 0 (counting metric, non-diff) and p == +inf (could be added with max_dim but defer).
notes: Fixed in this commit. For finite p > 0, composed tensor_pdist through index_select + sub + abs + pow + sum_dim + pow primitives. Pre-built row_i / row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 enumerate the upper-triangular pairs; index_select gathers the row-pair tensors so subsequent ops broadcast naturally. p == 0 (counting metric) and p == +inf (max-routing) now fail-loud on requires_grad=true rather than silently severing — autograd implementations for those cases are deferred. Added pdist_l2_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test. ft-api lib tests: 1169.

title: Harden runtime durability envelope metadata normalization
close_reason: Completed

title: Bound conformance parser diagnostics for fuzzed string inputs
description: testing-fuzzing audit found unbounded diagnostic echo in crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs parser helpers. parse_binary_op, parse_dtype, parse_device, and parse_keyset include the full rejected input in error strings. These functions consume values from JSON conformance fixtures and fuzzed/adversarial inputs; a single oversized token can produce multi-megabyte error/log output even though the harness already has bounded_diagnostic for legacy oracle errors. Fix by bounding rejected parser tokens through the existing bounded_diagnostic helper, add regression tests with oversized strings, and preserve existing valid parse behavior.
close_reason: Bounded conformance parser diagnostics for oversized fuzzed tokens

title: tensor_kthvalue severs autograd; index-routing backward needed
description:
tensor_kthvalue at lib.rs:12512 returns the k-th smallest element of a 1-D tensor. The value branch is just input[idx] where idx is the rank-k sorted index. PyTorch's torch.kthvalue IS differentiable: grad_input[idx] = grad_value, all other positions zero.

Wrap value branch in tensor_apply_function with this single-index routing backward. Index branch stays a non-grad leaf (integer).
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_kthvalue's value branch in tensor_apply_function with single-index routing backward: grad_input[idx] = grad_value where idx is the sort-rank-k index. The index branch stays a non-grad leaf. Added kthvalue_propagates_gradient_to_routed_index regression test (x=[3,1,4,1,5], k=3 → 3rd smallest is at idx 0 with value 3, grad_input = [1,0,0,0,0]). ft-api lib tests: 1163.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for softmax_dim + log_softmax_dim contiguous f64
description:
softmax_tensor_dim_contiguous_f64 and log_softmax_tensor_dim_contiguous_f64 are numerically delicate (exp/log composition with the max-subtraction stabilization trick). Both are widely used in the attention / classifier paths via ft-api.

No fuzz target currently exercises shape/dim validation or the inner max-subtract-exp-sum-divide loop on arbitrary shapes. A fuzz target that drives both with random shape + dim + input slices would catch:
- dim-out-of-range guard regressions
- shape-volume overflow path (checked_dim_loop_sizes)
- numerical pathologies (output should always be in [0,1] for softmax; sum-along-dim should be ~1 within tolerance)
- log_softmax output should always be <= 0

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_softmax exercising BOTH softmax_tensor_dim_contiguous_f64 AND log_softmax_tensor_dim_contiguous_f64
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok, asserts:
  * output.len == numel
  * softmax: all elements in [0.0 - eps, 1.0 + eps]
  * log_softmax: all elements <= eps (allow small positive epsilon for FP slack)
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_softmax fuzz target. Drives both softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 and log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 with shared shape/dim/input. Asserts: (1) per-element range [0,1] for softmax, <=0 for log_softmax with non-finite skipping; (2) sum-along-dim==1.0 within 1e-6 (the strong correctness contract); (3) empty-tensor (numel==0) success path. Shape-volume cap 4096. Two corpus seeds (2D dim=0, 3D dim=2 forcing inner_size>1 strided path). Builds clean.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_unique contracts: sortedness, membership, inverse mapping, counts
description: tensor_unique(x, sorted=true, return_inverse=true, return_counts=true) must satisfy: |unique| <= |x|, unique sorted (total_cmp), every x[i] appears in unique, unique[inverse[i]] == x[i] (handling NaN!=NaN), sum(counts) == |x|, counts[i] == #{x[j] : x[j] == unique[i]}. No MR exercises these together.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_unique_contract checking sortedness, membership, inverse map, count sum, and per-value frequency. Lib test passes; ft-conformance now 360 tests in feature=fuzz lib.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for sqrt + square roundtrip (square(sqrt(x)) for x >= 0)
description:
square ∘ sqrt is an inverse pair for non-negative inputs. For x >= 0: square(sqrt(x)) ≈ x within ULP tolerance.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_square_of_sqrt_recovers_nonneg
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: fuzz_metamorphic_square_of_sqrt_recovers_nonneg. square(sqrt(x)) ≈ x for x >= 0 within 8 ULP. Catches sqrt precision regressions. x = 0 case round-trip exact. Passes on first run.

title: Add gradient clipping utilities: clip_grad_norm_, clip_grad_value_
close_reason: Implemented clip_grad_norm_ and clip_grad_value_ with 6 tests

title: Adaptive pooling should use ceil end indices for upsampling bins
description: AdaptiveAvgPool/AdaptiveMaxPool currently compute bin starts and ends with floor(i * input / output) for both boundaries. PyTorch adaptive pooling uses floor for the start and ceil for the end, keeping every bin non-empty when output_size exceeds input_size. Current 1D/2D/3D avg/max paths can create zero-length bins and then error or compute invalid reductions for adaptive upsampling. Acceptance: add checked adaptive pool start/end helpers, use ceil division for end indices across AdaptiveAvgPool1d/2d/3d and AdaptiveMaxPool1d/2d/3d, add upsampling regressions, and keep focused ft-nn adaptive pool tests green.
close_reason: Shipped ceil end-index adaptive pooling bins across avg/max 1D/2D/3D.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for masked_select_tensor_contiguous_f64
description:
masked_select_tensor_contiguous_f64 (7183) returns 1-D selected elements where mask is non-zero. Variable-length output (filter-collect). Not fuzzed.

Risks: (1) mask-length boundary at offset > 0; (2) output length determined by mask pattern; (3) NaN/±inf inputs must pass through bit-exactly; (4) non-boolean mask values (any non-zero is treated as 'select').

Acceptance: ft_kernel_cpu_masked_select fuzz target with: success path mixing finite/NaN/±inf input + non-boolean mask, truncated-mask Err path. On success: output length == count of non-zero mask cells; every output bit-equals input[i] for some i where mask[i] != 0; ≥2 corpus seeds.
close_reason: Shipped — ft_kernel_cpu_masked_select fuzz target with output-length contract + bit-exact order-preserving cell validation, non-boolean mask values, truncated-mask Err path. Two corpus seeds. Compiles clean.

title: Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops
description:
## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods:
   - sparse_coo_tensor(coords, values, shape, dtype)
   - sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(indices, values, shape, sparse_dim, coalesced)
   - sparse_csr_tensor(crow_indices, col_indices, values, shape)
   - tensor_to_sparse_coo(input) - dense to sparse conversion
   - sparse_coo_to_dense / sparse_csr_to_dense
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations:
   - sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64 - sparse @ dense matmul
   - sparse_coo_coalesce - merge duplicate indices
   - sparse_coo_add - element-wise addition
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation
8. sparse_gradients field in TensorBackwardReport for sparse gradient storage
9. Embedding.sparse field in ft-nn with with_options(sparse) constructor
10. SparseAdam now checks report for sparse gradients and iterates efficiently over COO entries

### Tests (11 core sparse + 177 optim + 299 core/kernel passing)
- 8 in ft-core: COO/CSR creation, to_dense roundtrip, error handling
- 3 in ft-kernel-cpu: matmul, coalesce, add
- All existing tests pass with new sparse gradient infrastructure

### Remaining for full sparse gradient flow
- Wire sparse backward path in IndexSelect when source tensor is marked sparse
- Test sparse gradient round-trip with Embedding + SparseAdam
close_reason: Wired sparse backward path in IndexSelect (sparse_dim=1, dim=0) — Embedding(sparse=true) now surfaces SparseCOOTensor on TensorBackwardReport.sparse_gradient(weight); SparseAdam updates only touched rows; full Embedding+SparseAdam roundtrip test added in ft-optim; 4 sparse-grad tests added across ft-nn/ft-optim, all passing.

title: porting: add tensor_view_as for torch.Tensor.view_as parity
description: torch.Tensor.view_as(other) reshapes input to the shape of other. Trivial wrapper around tensor_view (same as reshape). Useful for porting code that uses view_as for shape-matching.
close_reason: Added tensor_view_as in ft-api as a thin wrapper over tensor_view that takes a reference tensor instead of an explicit shape. Strict view (element counts must match), not a broadcast. Autograd-aware via the underlying view. 2 unit tests cover shape propagation and gradient routing. ft-api now at 1292 lib tests.

title: [porting-to-rust] unblock tensor_special_i0e autograd via product rule (now that i1e is shipped)
description:
Now that ner6 shipped tensor_special_i1e (and ar9i unblocked i1's autograd), tensor_special_i0e's fail-loud guard on requires_grad can be replaced with the analytical backward:

  d/dx i0e(x) = d/dx (exp(-|x|) * i0(x))
             = -sign(x) * exp(-|x|) * i0(x) + exp(-|x|) * i1(x)
             = i1e(x) - sign(x) * i0e(x)

Note: i0e has a corner at x = 0 (derivative jumps from -1 to +1 across zero). Convention: sign(0) = 0 gives gradient = i1e(0) - 0 = 0, matching torch's autograd choice.

Wire via tensor_apply_function with bessel_i0e_scalar + bessel_i1e_scalar in the backward closure. Replace the existing fail-loud test with a gradient verification at x = 1.

Acceptance: tensor_special_i0e forward+backward with the product-rule gradient; remove fail-loud guard; new gradient lib test verifying d/dx i0e(1) ≈ exp(-1)*(i1(1) - i0(1)) ≈ -0.258.
close_reason: Shipped — tensor_special_i0e backward via product rule (i1e(x) - sign(x)*i0e(x)) using ner6's i1e + scalar helpers. Removed fail-loud guard. New gradient lib test verifies d/dx i0e(1) ≈ -0.2579.

title: [profiling-software-performance] tensor_softshrink has three redundant full(shape, lambd) allocations
description:
Same compose-anti-pattern family as cc2o (heaviside/pow_tensor), d521 (sinc), 4w7g (atanh/focal_loss), and x8r1 (entr). tensor_softshrink at ft-api lib.rs:5375 allocates the same lambd-valued tensor THREE times within a single forward call:

  let lambd_t      = full(shape, lambd)?;     // for tensor_gt mask
  let lambd_const  = full(shape, lambd)?;     // for tensor_sub
  let lambd_const2 = full(shape, lambd)?;     // for tensor_add

All three are operands of read-only ops (tensor_gt / tensor_sub / tensor_add); one shared lambd tensor suffices.

Net per-call savings: -2 full() allocations + -2 autograd-tape nodes. tensor_softshrink is a torch activation function used in sparse-coding / wavelet thresholding heads, so the wasted work compounds in training.

Acceptance:
- tensor_softshrink reuses one shared lambd tensor across gt mask, sub, and add call sites
- existing softshrink tests still pass — no behavior change
close_reason: Shipped: 4c0eacf. tensor_softshrink shares one lambd_t across the gt mask, the sub-shift, and the add-shift. -2 full() + -2 tape nodes per call. 2 softshrink tests including gradient regression pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_le(a, b) == lt(a, b) || eq(a, b) for non-NaN
description:
le(a, b) == lt(a, b) + eq(a, b) for non-NaN inputs (since exactly one of lt, gt, eq is 1, and le == lt || eq). Similar for ge.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_le_equals_lt_or_eq
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: le(a, b) == lt(a, b) + eq(a, b). Catches le/lt/eq dispatch drift. Passes on first run.

title: Align proposed architecture crate map with current workspace layers
description: Update PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md so its crate map and runtime flow mention the in-tree ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data layers instead of stopping at ft-api/ft-conformance.
notes: Updated the proposed architecture doc so the crate map includes ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and the runtime flow now acknowledges those higher-level layers before conformance/durability.

title: Enable F32 autograd for bilinear tensor_grid_sample
description: Discovery after frankentorch-qlxc and frankentorch-8mrg: tensor_grid_sample's bilinear autograd path still requires both input and grid tensors to be F64. The forward/backward helper already converts tensor data to f64 internally, and apply_function now materializes typed F32 outputs. Allow matching F32 input/grid tensors through the analytical bilinear backward while preserving F32 output dtype, with regression coverage for input and grid gradients.
close_reason: Completed in 252b702: bilinear tensor_grid_sample autograd now accepts matching F32/F32 and F64/F64 input-grid tensors, preserves F32 output dtype, and has F32 input/grid gradient regression coverage. rch ft-api grid_sample tests/fmt/check/clippy passed.

title: MaxUnpool1d should check output shape overflow before allocation
description: MaxUnpool1d::forward_with_indices computes n * c * l_pooled and n * c * output_size with unchecked usize multiplication before allocating output and gradient buffers. The 2D/3D variants are now fail-closed; 1D can still wrap/allocate the wrong shape for adversarial dimensions. Acceptance: use checked_shape_numel/checked_mul for input/output size arithmetic and add a regression test with an overflowing output_size.
close_reason: Shipped checked MaxUnpool1d input and output shape arithmetic before allocation.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_lt + tensor_gt + tensor_eq trichotomy ordering
description:
For non-NaN inputs: exactly one of lt(a, b), gt(a, b), eq(a, b) is 1.0; the other two are 0.0. Their sum is 1.0.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_lt_gt_eq_trichotomy
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: lt + gt + eq == 1.0 for non-NaN, == 0 for NaN. Trichotomy of ordering. Companion to kernel-level k2br at ft-api level. Passes on first run.

title: Metamorphic property test: det(AB) = det(A) * det(B)
description: Now that tensor_linalg_det is autograd-aware (frankentorch-pvfk), add a metamorphic property test that locks the multiplicative invariant det(A @ B) = det(A) * det(B) on small (2x2 / 3x3) random matrices. Exercises the new TensorNodeId-returning code path through composition with tensor_matmul and tensor_mul, surfacing any regressions in the kernel or the autograd-aware shim.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_det_of_product_equals_product_of_dets — proptest with 2x2/3x3 random matrices (entries bounded by ±128/23 ≈ ±5.6). Asserts |det(AB) - det(A)*det(B)| within a Hadamard-style bound proportional to (n*|entry|max)^n * n^2 * EPSILON. Exercises tensor_linalg_det's autograd-aware code path (added under pvfk) through composition with tensor_matmul. ft-conformance now at 189 fuzz tests (up from 188).

title: Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild
description:
## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence
close_reason: Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption

title: Packed sequence utilities should reject invalid lengths before panic or truncation
description: pack_padded_sequence currently accepts zero-length and over-max lengths, which can produce empty/inconsistent PackedSequence metadata. pad_packed_sequence then indexes batch_sizes[0] or silently truncates when total_length is smaller than the packed max length. Acceptance: fail closed for zero lengths, lengths greater than input time dimension, empty PackedSequence metadata, and too-small total_length; add focused ft-nn RNN utility regressions and keep the ft-nn packed sequence tests green.
close_reason: Shipped fail-closed guards for invalid packed sequence lengths, metadata, and short total_length.

title: porting: add tensor_matrix_transpose for torch.Tensor.mT parity
description: torch.Tensor.mT (and torch.transpose(x, -2, -1)) is the standard 'transpose last two dims' op, works on any rank ≥ 2. Common in batched matmul code where you can't use the 2-arg tensor_transpose easily without computing rank. Add it as a thin wrapper that resolves to tensor_transpose(input, ndim-2, ndim-1).
close_reason: Added tensor_matrix_transpose in ft-api: transpose of last two dims for any rank ≥ 2. Composes via tensor_transpose(input, ndim-2, ndim-1); rejects rank < 2 with a clear error. Equivalent to torch.Tensor.mT and torch.transpose(x, -2, -1). Common in batched matmul code where the rank may not be known at the call site. 5 unit tests cover 2-D row/col swap, batched 3-D forward correctness, double-apply identity, rank-below-2 rejection (1-D and 0-D), and gradient propagation. ft-api now at 1333 lib tests. Also re-closes frankentorch-pvfk and frankentorch-3v6e in JSONL — they keep getting reverted to open by other agents' br operations writing through the drifted DB.

title: Enforce index_add/index_copy shape + rank rules
description: Audit: tensor_index_add/index_copy accept mismatched src/index shapes and non-1D indices; they silently ignore extra indices. Fix by enforcing index rank=1 and src shape matches input except dim, with src_dim == index length. Add tests for mismatch errors and f32 parity.
close_reason: Completed

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] TensorIOError::Io + TensorError wrapper diagnostics not pinned
description:
ft-serialize has 8 insta snapshots pinning major JSON contracts and 3 of the 5 TensorIOError variants (InvalidMagic, UnsupportedVersion, Corrupt-truncated-shape). The remaining 2 variants — Io and TensorError — also have public-contract Display formats:

  Io { path, message } → "I/O error at '{path}': {message}"
  TensorError(e) → "tensor error: {e}"

Log scrapers and corruption recovery scripts parse these formats. Pinning catches accidental refactors that drop the surrounding context (path, message, or wrapper prefix).

Acceptance: 2 insta snapshots in ft-serialize covering both wrappers via direct construction (no fs/decode triggers needed).
close_reason: Shipped — 2 snapshots pin TensorIOError::Io and TensorError wrapper Display formats. Constructed directly without fs/decode triggers.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] no torch oracle for tensor_diag (1-D embed and 2-D extract paths)
description:
tensor_diag (added in 5a11cb5) dispatches by rank — 1-D becomes a 2-D matrix with input on the requested diagonal, 2-D extracts the diagonal as a 1-D tensor. Six lib unit tests cover the dispatch logic. No subprocess oracle pins the actual upstream torch.diag values for non-zero offsets, where the indexing semantics are easy to get subtly wrong (off-by-one between super and sub diagonals, off-by-one between 1-D embed offset and the resulting matrix size).

Concrete repro: a regression to tensor_diag_embed offset arithmetic that flips super/sub direction would still pass the lib unit test (the direction is consistent within tensor_diag_embed) but diverge from torch. The oracle catches that.

Fix direction: add torch_diag_subprocess_conformance with a fixed grid of (input, diagonal) pairs that exercises:
  - 1-D embed at offset 0 (main), +1 (super), -1 (sub)
  - 2-D extract at offset 0 (main), +1 (super), -1 (sub)
  - rectangular 2-D extraction (m != n)
For each case, run torch.diag on the same input/offset and assert ft-api returns the same shape and bit-equal values.

Acceptance:
- new test torch_diag_subprocess_conformance using the existing torch_*_subprocess_conformance pattern
- 6+ cases covering both rank dispatch paths
- skips silently if torch unavailable
- ft-conformance lib tests +1
close_reason: Shipped: 7224820. torch_diag_subprocess_conformance pins both rank-dispatch paths (1-D embed and 2-D extract) across main/super/sub diagonals + rectangular 2-D case. 8 cases, bit-equal via to_bits().

title: porting: add tensor_take + tensor_swapaxes/swapdims for torch parity
description: torch.take(input, indices) returns input.flatten()[indices], reshaped like indices. Useful for advanced indexing (the same primitive numpy.take provides). torch.swapaxes / torch.swapdims are direct aliases for transpose (different name conventions for numpy compatibility). Add tensor_take by composing tensor_reshape (flatten) + tensor_index_select; add swapaxes/swapdims as one-line wrappers around tensor_transpose.
close_reason: Added tensor_take, tensor_swapaxes, and tensor_swapdims for torch parity. tensor_take(input, indices) = input.flatten()[indices].reshape(indices.shape) — composes through tensor_reshape (flatten input + flatten indices) + tensor_index_select + tensor_reshape (back to indices shape); autograd-aware via index_select backward including repeated-index gradient accumulation. swapaxes/swapdims are one-line aliases for tensor_transpose (numpy compat names). 4 unit tests cover forward correctness, gradient routing, repeated-index accumulation, and alias equivalence to transpose. ft-api now at 1245 lib tests.

title: Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes
description: Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.
close_reason: Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.

title: Fix ft-nn MultiMarginLoss autograd via one_hot + relu + pow [child of cq0b]
description:
ft-nn MultiMarginLoss::forward at lib.rs:11852 has the same per-sample severance pattern as the session multi_margin_loss method (already fixed under frankentorch-x9cr).

Compose through session.one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div for per-sample loss, then apply the Reduction enum's mean/sum/none.
notes: Fixed via composition through one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div primitives — same recipe as session multi_margin_loss (frankentorch-x9cr) but with the Reduction enum dispatch at the end. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] no insta snapshots pinning native state-file error diagnostics (InvalidMagic / UnsupportedVersion / Corrupt-truncated)
description:
ft-serialize has 8 insta-pinned JSON / diagnostic contracts (strict_checkpoint_json_contract, hardened_checkpoint_json_contract, hardened_malformed_payload_diagnostic, hardened_non_object_payload_diagnostic, raptorq_sidecar_json_contract, decode_proof_artifact_json_contract, strict_unknown_field_diagnostic, version_mismatch_diagnostic) but the FT-native state-file error variants (TensorIOError::InvalidMagic, UnsupportedVersion, Corrupt-with-reason) are only verified via matches!() / contains() — their exact diagnostic strings are not pinned.

These strings are the contract that downstream tools (log-scrapers, alerting, checkpoint-corruption recovery scripts) rely on to identify what went wrong. Reformatting them silently is a contract break.

Acceptance: 3 insta snapshot tests pinning:
  1. TensorIOError::InvalidMagic — 'invalid magic bytes: not a FrankenTorch state file'
  2. TensorIOError::UnsupportedVersion {found: 99, max: 1} — 'unsupported format version 99 (max supported: 1)'
  3. TensorIOError::Corrupt {reason: truncated shape data} — produced via the existing native_format_rejects_truncated_shape_dims trigger.

ft-serialize lib tests +3, snapshots/ +3 .snap files.
close_reason: Shipped in 162c31b — 3 new insta snapshots pin the exact diagnostic strings for TensorIOError::InvalidMagic, UnsupportedVersion {99, 1}, and Corrupt-truncated-shape variants. All 7 ft-serialize *_diagnostic_snapshot tests pass.

title: Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp
description: These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.
notes: All children resolved across multiple commits: cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile/logit/xlogy/xlog1py/entr composed through autograd primitives in earlier rounds; gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma + multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6), logcumsumexp (ec75d35) wrapped in tensor_apply_function with analytical backwards. ft-api lib tests now at 1135 passing.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_transpose involution (transpose(transpose(x)) == x)
description:
transpose(transpose(M, i, j), i, j) == M bit-exactly for any 2-D tensor. Catches:
- transpose index off-by-one
- shape contract drift in the transpose backward

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_transpose_involution
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: transpose(transpose(M, 0, 1), 0, 1) == M bit-exact for 2-D tensors. Catches transpose index off-by-one and backward shape contract drift. Passes on first run.

title: Add E2E training test for GroupNorm + Conv2d block
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression that wires a Conv2d → GroupNorm block into a small image-regression task. Validates the GroupNorm composition (reshape + mean_dim + sub + mul + sqrt + div + affine) end-to-end through Adam.

Setup:
- 1x4x4x4 input (batch=1, channels=4, H=4, W=4)
- Conv2d(4, 4, 3x3, padding 1)
- GroupNorm(num_groups=2, num_channels=4)
- MSE against target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.5 (relaxed because GroupNorm normalizes)

title: [parity-gap] ConvTranspose1d module output_padding parity
close_reason: Implemented ConvTranspose1d output_padding parity

title: porting: add tensor_mv (matrix-vector multiply) for torch.mv parity
description: torch.mv(M, v) returns M @ v for a 2-D matrix M (shape [m, n]) and 1-D vector v (shape [n]); output is 1-D of shape [m]. PyTorch has it as a dedicated op even though matmul covers the same shape; user code calls it directly. Compose through autograd-aware primitives: reshape v to [n, 1] → tensor_matmul(M, v) → reshape to [m]. Add a few correctness and gradient regression tests.
close_reason: Added pub fn tensor_mv(M, v) at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs — matrix-vector product M @ v matching torch.mv. Composes through tensor_reshape + tensor_matmul + tensor_reshape; autograd-aware on both inputs. Added 3 unit tests: mv_known_values (forward correctness), mv_propagates_gradient_to_matrix_and_vector (analytic gradient regression for sum(M @ v)), mv_rejects_wrong_ranks (fail-loud for 1-D M, 2-D v, mismatched n). ft-api now at 1211 lib tests (up from 1208).

title: Numerical gradient check: tensor_linalg_det backward via finite differences
description: Lock the new autograd-aware tensor_linalg_det backward (added under frankentorch-pvfk) against numerical Jacobian computed by central finite differences. For each entry A[i,j], perturb ±eps, recompute det, and confirm (det(A+) - det(A-)) / (2*eps) matches the analytic backward to within finite-difference truncation tolerance. Catches any sign flip, transpose error, or scaling bug in the Jacobi-formula backward.
close_reason: Added linalg_det_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — exercises the new tensor_linalg_det backward (pvfk) on 4 well-conditioned 2x2/3x3 matrices (diag, general, rotation, perturbed-identity). Compares analytic ∂det/∂A from FrankenTorch's autograd against central FD with eps=1e-6 and abs_tol=5e-5. Locks the Jacobi-formula backward against any sign/transpose/scaling regression. ft-conformance now at 148 base lib tests, 194 with --features fuzz.

title: Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)
description:
Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward
close_reason: Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] no torch oracle for tensor_special_ndtri (inverse normal CDF)
description:
Just shipped tensor_special_ndtri (frankentorch-gf4d) via the closed-form ndtri(p) = sqrt(2) * erfinv(2p - 1). 3 lib tests cover known quantiles but no torch oracle pins it directly against torch.special.ndtri.

The oracle catches drift in:
  - erfinv's A&S polynomial vs torch's higher-order Cephes (loose ~1e-4 tolerance expected)
  - sqrt(2) constant arithmetic
  - boundary behavior at p=0 (-inf), p=1 (+inf), p<0 / p>1 (NaN)

Acceptance: torch_special_ndtri_subprocess_conformance test in ft-conformance with ≥10 cases covering median (0.5), 95% quantiles, near-boundary (1e-6, 1 - 1e-6), boundary (0, 1), and out-of-range (NaN); tagged-string wire encoding; ULP-bounded comparison with 1e-4 absolute tolerance for the smooth interior.
close_reason: Shipped — torch_special_ndtri_subprocess_conformance pins ndtri against torch.special.ndtri across 10 cases (median, 95%/80%/99.8% quantiles, far tails, NaN). 1e-4 absolute / 1e-3 relative tolerance covers erfinv's A&S polynomial accuracy.

title: porting: add causal_mask helper for masked attention
description: Mirror torch.nn.Transformer.generate_square_subsequent_mask. Returns a [seq_len, seq_len] tensor with 0.0 on and below the main diagonal and -inf above. Used to mask future tokens in transformer decoder self-attention. Currently every model that needs this builds the mask manually in its own forward; a shared helper makes for cleaner ports of HuggingFace / fairseq style code.
close_reason: Added FrankenTorchSession::causal_mask(size) helper mirroring torch.nn.Transformer.generate_square_subsequent_mask. Returns [size, size] tensor with -inf above diagonal and 0.0 on/below. Designed for use as additive mask in masked attention: softmax(scores + causal_mask). 3 unit tests cover the diagonal layout, zero-size edge case (returns [0, 0]), and the canonical softmax-zeros-future-positions usage pattern. ft-api now at 1260 lib tests.

title: Add ft-dispatch schema diagnostic snapshots
description: testing-golden-artifacts discovery: ft-dispatch has stable Display diagnostics for DispatchKeyError, OpSchemaError, SchemaRegistryError, and SchemaDispatchError, but no checked-in snapshot goldens. Acceptance: add insta dev-dependency and snapshot tests pinning representative schema/dispatch diagnostics, commit generated .snap artifacts, and validate with scoped rch fmt/test/check/clippy.
close_reason: Completed: added insta golden snapshot coverage for representative ft-dispatch dispatch-key, schema, registry, and schema-dispatch diagnostics; code shipped in 259020f.

title: [porting-to-rust] missing tensor_special_log_ndtr (numerically stable log normal CDF)
description:
Sister of ndtr (frankentorch-7qh9) — torch.special.log_ndtr returns log(ndtr(x)) but numerically stable for very negative x where ndtr(x) underflows to 0 in f64. Used heavily in VAE / Bayesian inference / importance sampling where log-probabilities matter.

Reference (scipy/torch): for x > 0, use log(ndtr(x)) directly; for x < ~-1, use the asymptotic series

  log_ndtr(x) ≈ -x²/2 - log(-x*sqrt(2π)) + log(1 - 1/x² + 3/x⁴ - 15/x⁶ + ...)

The series converges quickly for x << 0 because each term shrinks by ~x⁻². For -1 <= x <= 0 the direct log(ndtr(x)) form is fine.

Acceptance: tensor_special_log_ndtr method; piecewise composition (smooth interior via log(ndtr(x)), asymptotic for x < ~-7); ≥6 lib tests covering log_ndtr(0) = log(0.5), log_ndtr(-50) finite (asymptotic regime), agreement with log(ndtr(x)) in smooth interior, NaN propagation, gradient correctness.

Depends on: 7qh9 (ndtr port lands first to compose against).
close_reason: Shipped in b669a9d — tensor_special_log_ndtr via tensor_apply_function with piecewise-stable scalar forward (direct for x >= -1; asymptotic for x < -1) and backward (pdf/ndtr for x >= -7; Mills-ratio asymptotic for x < -7). 6 lib tests cover log_ndtr(0) = log(0.5), smooth-interior agreement, far-negative stability at x=-50, NaN propagation, gradient = sqrt(2/π) at x=0, and finite gradient at x=-10. All pass.

title: Severed-autograd loss umbrella: focal, poisson_nll, gaussian_nll
description:
Three more loss functions in ft-api that extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take tensor_mean — same severed-autograd pattern recently fixed for cross_entropy_with_smoothing / multilabel_soft_margin / soft_margin / multi_margin. The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.
notes: All children closed: poisson_nll (zl3n, 8d33f51), gaussian_nll (vjow, this commit), focal_loss (m6uk, this commit).

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] tighten torch_fft_numpy_subprocess_conformance tolerance from N^2 to N*log(N)*EPSILON after hpo6
description:
The 7z70 conformance harness currently uses bound N^2 * EPSILON to accommodate the previous O(N^2) naive DFT (which accumulated O(N) rounding per output cell).

After hpo6 replaced the implementation with Cooley-Tukey for power-of-2 N, the actual error growth is O(log N) per output cell — much tighter. The tolerance can be tightened proportionally:
- Power-of-2 N: bound = N * log2(N) * 32 * EPSILON
- Non-power-of-2 N: keep N^2 * EPSILON (fallback path is still naive)

Acceptance:
- ft-conformance/src/lib.rs torch_fft_numpy_subprocess_conformance updated with branched tolerance
- All 6 existing seed inputs (including 6-element non-power-of-2 case) still pass
- Commit message documents the math (Cooley-Tukey log2(N) butterfly error growth)
close_reason: Shipped: torch_fft_numpy_subprocess_conformance branched tolerance. Power-of-2 N now uses N*log2(N)*64*EPSILON (Cooley-Tukey path); non-power-of-2 retains N^2*EPSILON (naive fallback). At N=8 modest tightening (5.7e-15 → 5.3e-15); at N=4096 this would be ~2000x tighter. All 6 corpus cases still pass.

broadcast_to and expand should both produce the same shape and values for compatible inputs. Catches drift between the two broadcasting paths.

Acceptance:

- gated behind feature='fuzz'

title: Implement slogdet, pinverse, kthvalue, linalg_cross, linalg_vector_norm
description: Add numerically stable linalg and selection ops: slogdet (sign+log determinant), pinverse (SVD-based pseudoinverse), kthvalue (k-th smallest), linalg_cross (3D cross product), linalg_vector_norm (flexible vector norm). Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_roll(x, n) + tensor_roll(x, -n) roundtrip

roll(roll(x, n, dim), -n, dim) == x bit-exact. Rolling forward then backward returns to original.

Acceptance:
