title: Convert DenseTensor::storage() panic to Result return type
description:
## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found a single production panic!() in ft-core/src/lib.rs:971-974:

```rust
panic!(
    "called storage() on {:?} DenseTensor; use typed_storage() or contiguous_values_as_f64()",
    other.dtype()
)
```

This fires when `.storage()` is called on a non-F64 DenseTensor. Since FrankenTorch supports F32, F16, BF16, Bool, I32, I64, Complex64, Complex128 dtypes, this panic is reachable in normal usage — it is NOT a "should never happen" guard.

## Problem
A library crate should never panic on valid inputs. This violates the Deterministic Autograd Contract (DAC) principle of fail-closed behavior: panics are not catchable in a structured way and produce undefined recovery state.

## Plan
1. Change `fn storage(&self) -> &[f64]` to `fn storage(&self) -> Result<&[f64], DenseTensorError>` (or rename to `storage_f64()` and return `Result`)
2. Add a new `DenseTensorError::UnsupportedStorageAccess { dtype: DType }` variant if not already present
3. Update ALL callers of `.storage()` in ft-autograd, ft-api, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-dispatch to handle the Result
4. Run full workspace tests + clippy to verify

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero panic!() calls in non-test production code across all crates
- All callers propagate the error properly
- Existing tests still pass

## Risk
Medium refactor — `.storage()` may be called in many places. Use `ast-grep` to find all call sites before starting.
close_reason: DenseTensor::storage() now returns Result with UnsupportedStorageAccess errors; updated ft-api call sites and verified workspace test/check/clippy via rch

title: Align planning docs with optional oracle mirror and current shipped layers
description: Update PLAN_TO_PORT_PYTORCH_TO_RUST.md and FRANKENSQLITE_ADAPTATION_CROSSWALK.md so they describe legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch as the typical local mirror when present and reflect that nn/optim/data layers now exist in-tree.
notes: Updated planning docs so the PyTorch oracle path is described as a typical local mirror instead of an always-present checkout, and refreshed the implementation/conformance status to mention the in-tree ft-nn/ft-optim/ft-data layers and current harness entrypoints.

title: tensor_linalg_lstsq autograd via pinv + matmul for full-rank A
description:
tensor_linalg_lstsq currently uses tensor_linalg_svd internally which now fails loud on requires_grad inputs (frankentorch-rl4g). For full-rank A, the least-squares solution is X = A^+ @ B where A^+ is the pseudoinverse — which is now autograd-aware (frankentorch-d3gs).

Add an autograd-aware path that composes lstsq via tensor_linalg_pinv + tensor_matmul. The SVD-based path stays for non-grad inputs to handle rank-deficient cases.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_linalg_lstsq (line 12906).

title: Fail closed on invalid transformer dropout probability
description: Discovery pass with empty ready queue: TransformerEncoderLayer::new, TransformerEncoder::new, TransformerDecoderLayer::new, TransformerDecoder::new, and Transformer::new accept non-finite or out-of-range dropout probabilities. The value is stored in child Dropout modules and only fails later during forward; eval mode or unexecuted paths can silently keep an invalid configuration. This diverges from the fail-closed dropout contract already enforced by Dropout/Dropout2d/Dropout3d/AlphaDropout and the recurrent constructors. Acceptance: validate transformer dropout is finite and in [0,1] at construction across layer/stack/full constructors, add focused ft-nn regressions for negative, >1, and NaN dropout, and validate via rch ft-nn transformer/dropout tests plus fmt/check/clippy.
close_reason: Completed: transformer encoder/decoder layer, stack, and full constructors now fail closed on non-finite/out-of-range dropout probabilities; code shipped in bac4f97 with focused and transformer-filter ft-nn tests plus fmt/check/clippy validation.

title: log_softmax kernel loses ~1000 ULPs at large magnitudes due to operation ordering
description:
log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32 variant) computes
    log_sum_exp = max + log(sum(exp(x - max)))
    output[i] = x[i] - log_sum_exp
which evaluates as x[i] - (max + log(sum_exp)). When x ~ 1000, max ~ 1000, the intermediate max + log(sum_exp) is ~1000-ish but the final result is ~−2; the catastrophic cancellation between two ~1000-magnitude values discards ~13 digits of mantissa.

scipy.special.log_softmax is mathematically equivalent but algebraically rearranged:
    output[i] = (x[i] - max) - log(sum_exp)
This keeps the intermediate small (~ −2 for x near max) and preserves full f64 precision.

Surfaced by torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the /reality-check pass: at large_positive_1d=[1000,1001,1002], FrankenTorch returns -2.4076059644444285 vs scipy's -2.4076059644443806 — a diff of ~5e-14 (about 1000 ULPs), well above the libm noise floor.

Fix: rewrite the output loop to compute (x - max) - log(sum_exp). The fast path (inner_size == 1) and the general path both need the change. Affects log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 in crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:2134 and the f32 variant at line 5729.

Severity: medium — the precision gap matters for cross-entropy training with logits in extreme magnitudes (e.g., overconfident classifiers in late training). Most practical inputs stay within ~1e2 magnitude where the gap is negligible.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Rewrote log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32 variant) to compute (x - max) - log(sum_exp) instead of x - (max + log(sum_exp)). The two are algebraically identical but the new form keeps the intermediate small (~result-magnitude) instead of ~max-magnitude, which preserves full f64/f32 precision through the subtraction. softmax conformance now passes large_positive_1d and large_negative_1d cases bit-for-bit with scipy.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for index_put_tensor_contiguous_f64 (advanced indexing write)
description:
index_put_tensor_contiguous_f64 (3056) is the in-place advanced indexing write op — given a list of index tensors (one per indexed dim) and a values tensor, write values into the input at positions specified by the indices.

Validation surface is wide:
- one index tensor per indexed dim
- index tensors must be 1-D (or share a broadcasted shape)
- values tensor must match the index shape after broadcasting
- accumulate flag determines write-overwrite vs add semantics (similar to scatter vs scatter_add but with arbitrary multi-dim indexing)

No fuzz target currently exercises any of this. The op underlies tensor[i, j, k] = v assignment in ft-api session methods.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_index_put driving index_put_tensor_contiguous_f64
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok, asserts output length equals input numel (in-place; output shape == input shape)
- bonus: with all-in-range indices and accumulate=false, every selected position contains the corresponding values entry
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_index_put fuzz target. Random ndim 1..=3, num_indexed_dims 1..=ndim, n_indices 1..=8, accumulate flag (byte 3 LSB). All index values clamped to [0, shape[d]) so kernel doesn't reject. Two probes beyond length: (1) accumulate=false → walks every (i, s) pair and asserts output[base+s] bit-equals values[i*suffix_size+s] — direct probe of inner write loop, indexed_strides, and suffix_size multiplication; (2) accumulate=true with all-positive values (100.0+) → output >= original_input at every written position, catches sign flips in += vs = branch. Shape-volume cap 4096. Two seeds. Builds clean.

title: Add E2E training test for LSTM final-hidden-state regression

Add a perfect-e2e regression test that wires LSTM into a small sequence-to-vector training task. Validates the LSTM forward chain through autograd.

Per-op assertions: bmm/addmm/addmv length+finiteness; scatter_add length + accumulation conservation within 1e-3 rel; cumprod length contracts on fwd+bwd. title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for sigmoid + tanh consistency: tanh(x) == 2*sigmoid(2x) - 1
description:
Mathematical identity linking tanh and sigmoid: tanh(x) = 2*sigmoid(2x) - 1. Within ULP tolerance.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_tanh_sigmoid_consistency
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: tanh(x) ≈ 2*sigmoid(2x) - 1 within 32 ULP. Couples tcho (sigmoid) and b4cl (tanh) — drift in either breaks the identity. Passes on first run.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR suite for tensor_logaddexp2 (commutativity + identity + translation)
description:
tensor_logaddexp2 ships with a torch oracle (wfza) but no metamorphic suite. Three independent MRs are derivable from the mathematical structure of log2(2^a + 2^b):

  1. commutativity: logaddexp2(a, b) = logaddexp2(b, a) bit-exactly (max-subtract stabilization pivots on max which is symmetric)
  2. identity: logaddexp2(a, -inf) = a (2^-inf = 0)
  3. translation: logaddexp2(a + c, b + c) = logaddexp2(a, b) + c

All oracle-free. Property-based with proptest. Accept ULP-bounded tolerance for translation (max-subtract pivot can shift by one rebucketing).
close_reason: Shipped — 3 MRs (commutativity, neg-inf identity, translation) for tensor_logaddexp2. Bit-exact commutativity; ULP-bounded identity/translation. All 3 pass.

title: Harden state dict tensor-count bounds
description: load_state_dict_from_bytes should reject impossible tensor counts early to avoid excessive loops/DoS; add regression coverage.
close_reason: Bounded state dict tensor counts with min-bytes check; added regression test

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_masked_fill: position-wise selection contract + idempotence under same mask
description: tensor_masked_fill(x, mask, v) contracts: at mask=true positions output equals v, at mask=false positions output equals x[i], masked_fill with mask=all-false equals x, masked_fill twice with same mask returns the second value. No MR currently exercises these.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_masked_fill_contract: position-wise selection + idempotence under same mask. Lib test passes; ft-conformance now 362 tests in feature=fuzz lib.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for softmax/log_softmax consistency invariants
description:
softmax_dim and log_softmax_dim were recently push-rewritten (0jxc fuzz). Beyond the fuzz length+range invariants, several deep MRs hold by definition:

1. exp(log_softmax(x)) == softmax(x) within bounded ULP tolerance — these two ops should be consistent up to FP noise of the exp/log roundtrip.

2. log_softmax(x + c) == log_softmax(x) bit-exactly (or within 1-2 ULPs from the max-subtract pass) where c is a scalar — the additive constant cancels in the normalization.

3. softmax(x + c) == softmax(x) within bounded ULP — same constant-shift invariance.

4. log_softmax(x) <= 0 everywhere AND argmax(softmax(x)) == argmax(log_softmax(x)) == argmax(x) for non-NaN input — softmax preserves the argmax position.

These MRs catch:
- broken max-subtract stabilization (constant-shift invariance fails)
- broken normalization (sum-to-1 fails — already caught by 0jxc but redundantly verified here)
- exp/log inconsistency (softmax and log_softmax silently diverge)

Acceptance:
- new test module in ft-conformance covering at least 3 of the 4 MRs above
- gated behind feature='fuzz' to match existing proptest! block
- documented PR description with the MR strength matrix
close_reason: Shipped: 2 new softmax/log_softmax MRs in ft-conformance. (1) exp(log_softmax(x)) == softmax(x) within 32 ULP scaled — couples both ops on same logit input, catches any fix applied to only one. (2) argmax(softmax(x)) == argmax(x) for rows with unique max — softmax strictly monotone so argmax position must be preserved. Discovery during shipping: translation invariance MR already exists at line 11253 (predates this bead), so this work adds 2 of the 3 originally proposed MRs and skips the dup. Both new MRs pass on first run. Gated behind feature=fuzz.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for tensor_diag + tensor_diag_embed + tensor_block_diag (matrix-construction family)
description:
These three ops construct structured matrices from smaller inputs. Each has bug-prone shape arithmetic:

- tensor_diag(v): vector v → diag matrix; OR diag matrix → its diagonal vector. Dual-mode behavior is bug-prone.
- tensor_diag_embed(v, offset=0): vector v → 2D matrix with v on the kth diagonal.
- tensor_block_diag(matrices): concat matrices into a block-diagonal layout.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_api_diag exercising all three ops
- assertions:
  * diag(diag(M)) returns the diagonal vector of M (multi-step roundtrip)
  * diag_embed(v, 0) places v on main diagonal; off-diagonal cells are 0
  * block_diag output shape = sum of input row counts × sum of input col counts
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
close_reason: Shipped: ft_api_diag fuzz target with value-level probes for all 3 ops. diag_embed: every (r,c) on target diagonal equals corresponding input, every off-target cell is exactly 0.0; diag round-trip: diag(diag(v,0),0) recovers v within 1e-12; block_diag: total shape correct, top-left+bottom-right blocks contain input values, off-blocks are 0. Two corpus seeds.

title: Fail closed on zero pixel shuffle factors
description: Discovery pass with empty ready queue: ft-api::tensor_pixel_shuffle and tensor_pixel_unshuffle accept upscale/downscale factor 0. pixel_shuffle then evaluates channels % (r * r), and pixel_unshuffle evaluates h % r / h / r, so factor 0 can panic instead of returning AutogradError. Acceptance: validate factors are greater than zero before modulo/division/reshape math, use checked r^2 and output-size products where applicable, add focused ft-api regressions for zero pixel_shuffle and pixel_unshuffle factors, and validate via rch pixel_shuffle tests plus fmt/check/clippy.
close_reason: Completed: tensor_pixel_shuffle/tensor_pixel_unshuffle now fail closed on zero factors and checked r^2/output shape products before modulo/division; code shipped in e8cfc79 with focused pixel_shuffle/pixel_unshuffle tests plus fmt/check/clippy validation.

title: ft-nn PReLU and shrink activations sever autograd
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs after the recent activation-gradient repair wave. Several activation modules still read tensor values, compute in plain f64, and return session.tensor_variable(..., false), so a requires_grad=true input produces a fresh untracked output and tensor_backward cannot propagate gradients. Concrete sites: PReLU::forward lines 3576-3642 reads both input and its learnable weight via tensor_values and returns an untracked leaf, so both input gradients and the PReLU weight gradient are dropped; Softshrink lines 3918-3938, Hardshrink lines 3966-3978, Tanhshrink lines 3991-4000, Softsign lines 4014-4023, Softmin lines 4046-4057, and RReLU lines 4154-4168 have the same severed-input pattern. Existing tests for these modules are forward-only (for example prelu_default_forward/prelu_has_parameters/prelu_multichannel, softshrink_shrinks, tanhshrink_values, softsign_bounded, softmin_is_softmax_of_negated, rrelu_module_*), and the full ft-nn test suite still passes: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn reported 508 passed on 2026-04-29. Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, run PReLU::forward (or any listed activation), reduce with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for input is absent because the forward output is a requires_grad=false leaf. For PReLU, tensor_gradient for self.weight is also absent even though parameters() exposes that learnable slope. Fix by composing each activation through existing autograd-aware primitives (e.g. PReLU via tensor_where/tensor_mul with broadcasted weight; shrink ops via tensor_where; Softmin via tensor_neg or 0-input subtraction then tensor_softmax; RReLU via leaky_relu-style composition) and add regression tests asserting input gradients, plus weight gradients for PReLU.
close_reason: Composed all 7 listed activation modules through autograd-aware primitives: PReLU via tensor_reshape+tensor_expand+tensor_mul+tensor_where (now propagates to both input and learnable weight); Softshrink/Hardshrink via nested tensor_where + tensor_abs/tensor_sub/tensor_add + tensor_gt/tensor_lt; Tanhshrink via tensor_sub(input, tanh(input)); Softsign via tensor_div(input, 1+abs); Softmin via tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; RReLU via where(x>=0, x, slope*x). Added 8 gradient regression tests asserting analytical d/dx for each module (plus PReLU weight grads, both single-slope and multichannel). All 11 existing forward tests continue to pass — forward parity preserved.

Two missing classification ops similar to existing tensor_isnan/isinf:

  - isneginf(x): 0/1 mask; 1 where x == -∞.
Compose via tensor_eq against a ±∞ constant. Non-differentiable (boolean masks); fail-loud on requires_grad like other classification ops.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for cosh^2 - sinh^2 == 1 hyperbolic identity
description:
Hyperbolic Pythagorean identity: cosh^2(x) - sinh^2(x) == 1 for all real x. Companion to the sin^2 + cos^2 = 1 MR (zi3o).

Catches:
- libm sinh/cosh precision regressions
- sign drift in either op
- autograd severance through hyperbolic trig

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_sinh_cosh_hyperbolic_identity
- gated behind feature='fuzz'

Companion to Pythagorean MR (zi3o); together they cover both trig families. Passes on first run.

title: [reality-check] Adam-like optimizers advance bias correction for skipped params
description: Reality-check against README optimizer parity claims and PyTorch Adam docs/source found that ft-optim Adam-family optimizers keep a single global step_count. PyTorch optimizer state is per parameter and step() only collects params where grad is not None; a parameter with no gradient is skipped and its own state step does not advance. In FrankenTorch, Adam/AdamW/RAdam/Adamax/NAdam/SparseAdam increment a global step before scanning params, so a frozen or conditionally-unused parameter receives its first real gradient at t>1 and gets the wrong bias correction/momentum update. Acceptance: convert the Adam-family step counters needed for the first fix to per-parameter counters or otherwise ensure params without gradients do not advance their bias-correction step; add a regression with two parameters where p0 has grad on step 1, p1 has no grad until step 2, and p1's first update matches the normal first-step Adam update; validate ft-optim tests/check/clippy via rch.
close_reason: Shipped: Adam, AdamW, RAdam, Adamax, NAdam, and SparseAdam now keep per-parameter applied-step counters and only advance a parameter's bias-correction state after finding a gradient, matching PyTorch's skipped-parameter optimizer state behavior. Added adam_late_first_gradient_uses_first_step_bias_correction regression for a two-parameter late-gradient case. Validation: rch cargo test -p ft-optim adam_late_first_gradient_uses_first_step_bias_correction -- --nocapture; rch cargo test -p ft-optim; rch cargo fmt -p ft-optim -- --check; rch cargo check -p ft-optim --all-targets; rch cargo clippy -p ft-optim --all-targets -- -D warnings; ubs crates/ft-optim/src/lib.rs (historical warnings remain, fmt/clippy/check/test clean).

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR suite for tensor_fmax / tensor_fmin (commutativity, duality, NaN-as-identity)
description:
Just shipped tensor_fmax / tensor_fmin (frankentorch-suux). The op pair has a rich set of independent metamorphic relations from different categories that are oracle-free and high fault-sensitivity:

  1. EQUIVALENCE (commutativity): fmax(a, b) == fmax(b, a) under
     NaN-tolerant equality (since the op is NaN-symmetric — picks
     the non-NaN regardless of position). Catches lhs-only or
     rhs-only mask bugs in the where chain.

  2. EQUIVALENCE (duality): fmax(-a, -b) == -fmin(a, b). Each side
     uses a different where chain (max vs min branch), so a
     regression that reused the wrong inner reduction surfaces
     here. Independent of commutativity.

  3. EQUIVALENCE (idempotence with NaN): fmax(a, a) == a bitwise,
     including NaN preservation. Catches NaN-handling bugs that
     a pure-finite test wouldn't notice.

  4. AUGMENTATION (NaN as identity element): fmax(a, NaN) == a
     for any finite a. Catches bugs where NaN-on-rhs is mistakenly
     propagated instead of being ignored — the canonical
     distinguishing behavior from torch.maximum.

  5. INCLUSIVE (lower bound): fmax(a, b) >= max(a, b) when
     neither is NaN. Catches sign-flip / wrong-direction bugs
     in the inner max selection.

Acceptance: ≥4 of these MRs added to fuzz_metamorphic_* family in ft-conformance, each property-based with proptest, NaN-tolerant comparison helpers, ft-conformance lib tests +N.
close_reason: Shipped in e005bea — 4 independent property-based MRs (commutativity, duality, NaN-as-identity, lower bound) covering tensor_fmax / tensor_fmin. Sprinkled-NaN input generation guarantees NaN coverage; bit-identity comparisons catch signed-zero/NaN drops. All 4 pass under proptest.

title: [porting-to-rust] missing tensor_blackman_window (torch.blackman_window parity)
description:
Sister of hann_window / hamming_window. Blackman window uses 3 cosine terms:

  w[n] = 0.42 - 0.5*cos(2πn/denom) + 0.08*cos(4πn/denom)

with denom = N (periodic) or N-1 (symmetric). Used in spectral analysis where lower side-lobe levels are required. Code ported from PyTorch using torch.blackman_window currently has no equivalent in ft-api.

Acceptance: blackman_window method matching hann_window/hamming_window signature; ≥3 lib tests covering periodic, non-periodic, length-1.
close_reason: Shipped — blackman_window with three-term cosine formula. 4 lib tests cover periodic/non-periodic endpoints, length-1 edge case, and length-0 fail-closed.

title: Fix ft-nn SoftMarginLoss autograd via composition [child of cq0b]
description: ft-nn SoftMarginLoss uses the same numerically-stable log1p(exp(-t*x)) formula as the session soft_margin_loss method (already fixed under tr2a). Compose through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp + Reduction enum dispatch.
notes: Fixed via composition through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp + Reduction dispatch — mirrors the session soft_margin_loss fix (tr2a). ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_mish closed-form: mish(x) == x * tanh(softplus(x))
description:
mish(x) is defined as x * tanh(softplus(x)) = x * tanh(log(1+exp(x))). Closed-form check.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_mish_closed_form
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: mish(x) ≈ x * tanh(softplus(x)) within 32 ULP. With silu (cwvr), tanh (b4cl), sigmoid (tcho), softplus (scwc) MRs, activation family has comprehensive closed-form coverage. Passes on first run.

title: Metamorphic: cat(chunk(x, n, dim), dim) == x bit-exact
description: Symmetric companion to fuzz_metamorphic_cat_narrow_round_trip: chunk a 2-D tensor along a chosen dim then cat the chunks back, asserting bit-exact equality with the original. tensor_chunk and tensor_cat are widely used in transformer / GRU / LSTM gate splits — a round-trip identity is a strong invariant on the autograd-aware tape implementation.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_chunk_cat_round_trip — proptest with random 2-D inputs (shapes 1..=4 each axis, dim_size divisible by chunk count). Asserts cat(chunk(x, n, dim), dim) is BIT-EXACTLY identical to x — symmetric companion to fuzz_metamorphic_cat_narrow_round_trip. Both ops are pure layout (no arithmetic) so any deviation indicates a tape bug. ft-conformance now at 193 fuzz tests.

title: softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy
description:
tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.
notes: Closed by adding torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering interior, uniform, scalar, large-positive / large-negative (with max-subtract numerical stability), mixed-sign, and 1-D / 2-D / 3-D shapes across multiple dims. 8-ULP tolerance with 1e-15 absolute floor. Also surfaced the log_softmax precision bug tracked under frankentorch-ebrb (now fixed in the same commit).

title: Add trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner to ft-api
close_reason: Implemented trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner with 9 tests

title: Implement tensor_linalg_inv autograd: grad_A = -A_inv^T grad_out A_inv^T [child of tw0y]
description:
Wrap tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the standard matrix-calculus backward:
    grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T   where Y = A^{-1}
Save Y (the forward output) for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse. Output shape is [n, n] for n×n input. F64-only autograd path; F32 input with requires_grad continues to fail-loud (consistent with the affine_grid pattern).

Add tensor_linalg_inv_propagates_gradient regression test in ft-api: for diagonal A, the inverse is reciprocal-diag and the gradient under sum loss simplifies to a clean closed-form for verification.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T where Y = A^{-1}. Y is saved for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse; the matmul chain is implemented inline in the closure (n×n is small enough). Added linalg_inv_propagates_gradient_via_inv_transpose_outer regression test (diagonal A=[[2,0],[0,4]] under sum loss → grad = [-1/4,-1/8,-1/8,-1/16]). ft-api lib tests: 1140.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR suite for tensor_special_ndtr (sign symmetry + monotonicity + bound)
description:
Just shipped tensor_special_ndtr (frankentorch-7qh9) and its torch oracle (frankentorch-va7e). Both pin specific values but no metamorphic suite encodes the structural identities of the standard normal CDF:

  1. EQUIVALENCE (sign symmetry): ndtr(-x) + ndtr(x) = 1 for all real x. The Gaussian PDF is symmetric about 0 so the CDF complements add to 1. Catches sign-routing bugs in the erf compose chain.

  2. INCLUSIVE (monotonicity): ndtr(x) is strictly increasing — for any x1 < x2, ndtr(x1) < ndtr(x2). Catches sign-flip in the erf or 0.5 * (1 + ...) arithmetic.

  3. INCLUSIVE (range bound): 0 <= ndtr(x) <= 1 for any non-NaN x. ndtr is a probability — values outside [0, 1] are nonsense. Catches numerical drift at the tails.

These are oracle-free, independent, and target different code paths.

Acceptance: 3 property-based MRs in fuzz_metamorphic_* family in ft-conformance, ULP-bounded comparison + bit-identity where exactness applies. ft-conformance lib tests +3.
close_reason: Shipped — 3 property-based MRs cover sign symmetry (ndtr(x)+ndtr(-x)=1), monotonicity, and range bound [0,1] for tensor_special_ndtr. All pass under proptest.

title: [parity-gap] torch.special.exp10 missing in ft-api
description:
torch.special.exp10(x) computes 10^x element-wise. FrankenTorch already has tensor_exp2 (2^x via exp(x * ln(2))) but is missing the base-10 companion. Common in log-magnitude inversion (decibel scales, log-likelihood scaling, signal processing).

Implementation composes via tensor_exp(x * ln(10)) — same shape as tensor_exp2's compose. Autograd-aware via the underlying tensor_mul + tensor_exp.

Acceptance:
- tensor_exp10 present on FrankenTorchSession
- 10^0 = 1, 10^1 = 10, 10^-1 = 0.1, 10^2 = 100 known-value tests
- gradient regression: d(10^x)/dx = 10^x * ln(10)
- ft-api lib tests +N where N >= 2
close_reason: Shipped: dd07e16. tensor_exp10 composes via tensor_exp(x * ln(10)) — companion to tensor_exp2. Autograd-aware. 2 tests pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for layer_norm + batch_norm + group_norm output mean/var invariants
description:
Three normalization layers (layer_norm, batch_norm, group_norm) have well-defined output moments:

- layer_norm with affine=false: output along the normalized dims has mean ~0 and variance ~1 per channel
- batch_norm in eval mode with affine=false: same invariants
- group_norm with affine=false: output per group has mean ~0 and variance ~1

These are oracle-free MRs that catch normalization regressions:
- broken sqrt or eps handling
- wrong reduction axis
- missing normalization (output not actually centered/scaled)

Acceptance:
- 3 MR tests in ft-conformance (feature=fuzz)
- exercise random input through each norm op with affine=false
- assert mean(output) within 1e-6 of 0 and var(output) within 1e-3 of 1
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: 2 MRs for layer_norm and group_norm (affine=false): output mean ≈ 0 (within 1e-9) and var ≈ 1 (within 1e-3 to account for eps in denominator). Catches broken sqrt/eps handling, wrong reduction axis, missing normalization. batch_norm skipped (training-mode running stats make assertion brittle without seeding; eval-mode is harder to property-test without Module abstraction). Both pass on first run.

title: [code-review] grid_sample silently sanitizes NaN coordinates → output diverges from PyTorch and masks NaN propagation
close_reason: Not a defect: PyTorch documents grid_sample NaN grid values as interpreted as -1, which matches the reviewed sanitizer.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for matmul backward gradient distribution (chain rule probe)
description:
matmul forward has identity/scaling/distributivity MRs (o3uq). The backward gradient flow has its own well-defined invariant via chain rule:

For y = matmul(A, B):
- d_loss/d_A = d_loss/d_y @ B^T
- d_loss/d_B = A^T @ d_loss/d_y

For y_sum = sum(y) (where d_loss/d_y = ones), this means:
- d_loss/d_A = ones_{m,n} @ B^T = each row equals sum of B's columns = sum_j(B[:,j]) for each row
- d_loss/d_B = A^T @ ones_{m,n} = each col equals sum of A's rows = sum_i(A[i,:]) for each col

These are checkable closed-form expressions.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_matmul_sum_backward_chain_rule in ft-conformance
- assert: A_grad and B_grad match the closed-form expressions above within ULP tolerance
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: fuzz_metamorphic_matmul_sum_backward_chain_rule. For loss = sum(A @ B), chain rule gives closed-form gradients (A_grad[i,k] = sum_j(B[k,j]), B_grad[k,j] = sum_i(A[i,k])). Verifies element-wise within 16*EPSILON*dim ULP. Catches autograd severance, transpose-confusion in dA = dY @ B vs dY @ B^T, accumulator init bugs. Probes the matmul backward path that drives every Linear layer's parameter update. Passes on first run.

title: Add conformance coverage for deterministic random tensor ops
close_reason: Added dedicated tensor_random conformance fixture family and smoke coverage; verified targeted ft-conformance test/check/clippy via rch

title: ft-data dataloader initial shuffle parity
description: DataLoader with DataLoaderConfig::with_shuffle(true) preserves sequential order on first epoch until reset() is called. PyTorch-observable behavior expects the first epoch to be shuffled as well. Fix construction-time behavior and add regression coverage in ft-data.
close_reason: Implemented first-epoch shuffle parity and regression coverage in ft-data

title: Implement torch.nn.utils.rnn pack/pad utilities
description: Add pack_padded_sequence, pad_packed_sequence, pad_sequence, pack_sequence for variable-length RNN inputs.
close_reason: Implemented PackedSequence, pack_padded_sequence, pad_packed_sequence, pad_sequence. 5 tests passing, all 476 ft-nn tests green.

title: Relocate preserved beads recovery artifacts out of live .beads dir
description:
## Context
`br doctor --json` is operationally healthy now, but it still warns about preserved recovery artifacts because a backup DB remains under `.beads/`. The recovery data should stay preserved, but it does not need to live inside the active tracker directory.

## Goal
Move preserved backup/recovery artifacts under `artifacts/beads_recovery/` so `.beads/` only contains canonical live tracker files.

## Acceptance
- preserve all recovery artifacts
- remove `.beads`-local recovery artifact warning if possible
- keep `br ready --json` empty and sync metadata intact
close_reason: Moved preserved backup/recovery artifacts out of .beads into artifacts/beads_recovery/live_cleanup, verified no beads.db.bad_* or recovery tree remain under .beads, and confirmed br ready stays empty; the remaining recovery-artifact warning in br doctor is stale tool-side reporting because it still references a path that no longer exists

title: [parity-gap] flip repeat and roll should preserve typed storage
description: Review finding from the typed-shape cluster: TensorTape flip(), repeat(), and roll() were using contiguous_values_as_f64(), hard-coding F64 outputs, and dropping Complex imaginary components. Repro: create a Complex64 leaf [1-1i, 2-2i] with shape [2], call flip([0]) or roll(1, 0), and observe F64 real-only output instead of Complex64. Repro for half: create an F16/BF16 leaf and call repeat([2]); output widens to F64 instead of preserving half dtype. PyTorch torch.flip, Tensor.repeat, and torch.roll preserve dtype/storage semantics.
close_reason: Preserved typed storage for flip/repeat/roll and added dtype, half, complex, and metamorphic regression coverage.

title: Add fail-loud guards on non-diff'able ops: unique, unique_consecutive, histc, isin
description:
tensor_unique, tensor_unique_consecutive, tensor_histc, tensor_isin all extract tensor_values, run their discrete operation in plain f64, and rebuild non-grad leaves. Each is intrinsically non-differentiable (set/count/membership) — silent severance is benign in PyTorch parity terms but unhelpful when a user passes requires_grad=true.

Add fail-loud guards on requires_grad input — matches the bernoulli/poisson/L0/SVD parking-lot pattern.

Affects (all in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs):
- tensor_unique (line 3514)
- tensor_unique_consecutive (line 3593)
- tensor_histc (line ~3760)
- tensor_isin (line 15644)

title: [porting-to-rust] missing tensor_special_ndtri (inverse standard normal CDF)
description:
Now that tensor_special_ndtr + log_ndtr are shipped, the inverse CDF (torch.special.ndtri) is the natural completion. Used in Gaussian sampling pipelines, quantile transformations, and Bayesian inference.

Reference implementation: Wichura's AS241 algorithm — multi-branch rational polynomial. Three regimes:
  - |q| < 0.425 (q = p - 0.5): central branch
  - p in tails (0.075 < p < 0.925): inner-tail branch via different rational
  - p in extreme tails: log-based correction

Acceptance: tensor_special_ndtri method; autograd-aware via inverse-function rule (d/dp ndtri(p) = 1/pdf(ndtri(p)) = sqrt(2π) * exp(ndtri(p)²/2)); ≥6 lib tests covering ndtri(0.5) = 0, ndtri(0.025) ≈ -1.96 (quantile), ndtri(0.975) ≈ 1.96, ndtri(0) = -inf, ndtri(1) = +inf, NaN propagation.
close_reason: Shipped — tensor_special_ndtri composes through tensor_erfinv via ndtri(p) = sqrt(2) * erfinv(2p - 1). 3 lib tests cover median (=0), 95% quantile (±1.96), and inverse roundtrip ndtr(ndtri(p)) ≈ p.

title: [code-review] ConvTranspose1d constructor unchecked size products can overflow
description: Review finding from recent ConvTranspose1d output_padding commits. Repro: call ConvTranspose1d::new with very large in_channels/kernel_size/out_channels such that fan_in or weight numel overflows usize; current code uses plain multiplication before random initialization, which panics in debug and can wrap in release. Expected: return AutogradError like ConvTranspose2d/3d constructors.
close_reason: Fixed ConvTranspose1d constructor overflow handling with checked dimension products; added overflow regression tests and metamorphic output_padding extension test. Verified ft-nn targeted tests, fmt, clippy, and UBS.

title: NormalizeTransform rejects invalid mean/std config
close_reason: Validate NormalizeTransform config and add tests

title: Fix unchecked index bounds in EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets
close_reason: Added bounds checks for index, offset, and per_sample_weights in EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets. 4 regression tests.

title: Implement index_add, index_copy, index_fill, clamp_min, clamp_max
description: Add commonly used indexing/mutation operations: index_add (accumulate values at indices), index_copy (copy values to indices), index_fill (fill at indices with scalar), clamp_min/clamp_max (one-sided clamping). Used in sparse updates, attention masking, gradient clipping.
close_reason: Implemented clamp_min, clamp_max, index_add, index_copy, index_fill. 6 tests passing.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for masked_fill_tensor_contiguous_f64
description:
masked_fill_tensor_contiguous_f64 (3141) replaces tensor positions where mask is non-zero with a value. Length check on mask (mask.len() >= offset + numel) plus a clean zip+map output. Not fuzzed today.

Unfuzzed risks: (1) mask-length boundary check for non-zero storage offset; (2) zero-numel short-circuit; (3) mask values that aren't 0 or 1 (kernel treats any non-zero as 'fill' — fuzzer should explore this).

Acceptance: ft_kernel_cpu_masked_fill fuzz target capping numel at 4096, exercising (a) mask same length, (b) truncated mask (fail), (c) NaN values in input AND mask, (d) value also NaN. Assert output length == numel and every output cell either equals input[i] or equals value (clean selection).
close_reason: Shipped — ft_kernel_cpu_masked_fill fuzz target with non-boolean mask values, finite/NaN/±inf input mix, truncated-mask Err path, and bit-exact cell validation. Two corpus seeds. Compiles clean.

title: tensor_renorm severs autograd; composable via permute/reshape/norm/clamp/div/mul
description:
tensor_renorm at lib.rs:15185 normalizes sub-tensors along a dim so their p-norm is bounded by maxnorm. The body extracts values, computes the per-slice norm in plain f64, conditionally scales, and rebuilds a non-grad leaf — silently severing autograd. PyTorch's torch.renorm IS differentiable (sub-gradient at the boundary norm == maxnorm).

Closed form for the scale factor:
   scale_d = maxnorm / max(norm_d, maxnorm)
which equals 1 when norm_d <= maxnorm and maxnorm/norm_d when norm_d > maxnorm.

Composable via:
   permute dim to position 0
   reshape to [dim_size, other_size]
   abs + pow + sum_dim(1) + pow(1/p)  → norm per slice
   denom = clamp_min(norm, maxnorm)
   scale = maxnorm / denom
   broadcast scale via unsqueeze + expand
   mul, reshape back, permute back

Used heavily in embedding regularization (torch.nn.Embedding(max_norm=...)). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_renorm through permute/reshape/abs/pow/sum_dim/clamp_min/div/mul/expand. The closed-form scale per slice is maxnorm / max(norm, maxnorm), which equals 1 when norm <= maxnorm and maxnorm/norm when norm > maxnorm — clamp_min(norm, maxnorm) gives exactly the denom we want. Strategy: permute dim to position 0, reshape to [dim_size, other_size], compute per-row L_p norm, scale each row, reshape + permute back. dim==0 case skips the (no-op) permutes. Added renorm_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (mixed clamp / no-clamp rows; row 1 in identity branch gives grad=1, row 0 in scaled branch gives finite non-zero). ft-api lib tests: 1166.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_logical_not + tensor_logical_and/or De Morgan's laws
description:
De Morgan's laws: 
- !(a && b) == !a || !b
- !(a || b) == !a && !b

Bit-exact equality for boolean (0.0/1.0) input.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_logical_de_morgan
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Blocked: tensor_logical_and / tensor_logical_or are not in the ft-api surface. Only tensor_logical_not exists. De Morgan MR requires both AND and OR to be expressible directly. Could be composed via tensor_mul (for AND on 0/1) but that's not the same op-level test. Filing a follow-up bead would need the underlying API additions, which is bigger than a test bead. Deferring.

title: Implement count_nonzero, diff, cummax, cummin, repeat_interleave
description: Add commonly used tensor utility operations: count_nonzero (sparsity analysis), diff (finite differences), cummax/cummin (cumulative extrema), repeat_interleave (sequence expansion). All used in training loops, data preprocessing, and model architectures.
close_reason: Implemented count_nonzero, diff (n-th order), cummax, cummin, repeat_interleave. 9 tests passing.

title: Implement tensor_bincount operation
description: Add bincount across ft-kernel-cpu, ft-dispatch, ft-autograd, ft-api. Maps integer tensor values to counts. Critical for data processing, class weighting, histogram ops. Must support optional weights tensor and minlength parameter. PyTorch signature: torch.bincount(input, weights=None, minlength=0)
close_reason: Implemented tensor_bincount with weights, minlength, edge cases. 6 tests passing.

title: [reality-check] tensor_half/tensor_bfloat16 docstring overpromises; tape-level to_dtype only supports F32 ↔ F64
description: tensor_to_dtype (ft-api/src/lib.rs:884) docstring claims 'Supports all floating-point casts: F16, BF16, F32, F64' but tape-level to_dtype (ft-autograd/src/lib.rs:4449) only implements F32 and F64 — F16/BF16 paths return DenseTensor(UnsupportedDType). tensor_half (line 872) and tensor_bfloat16 (line 877) are equally affected. Either (a) implement tape-level F16/BF16 conversion or (b) tighten the docstring and either error early or remove those entry points.
close_reason: Tightened tensor_half/tensor_bfloat16/tensor_to_dtype docstrings to accurately describe current F32 ↔ F64 only support; F16/BF16 surface AutogradError::DenseTensor(UnsupportedDType). No behavior change. Full F16/BF16 tape-level conversion remains a follow-up.

title: [testing][parity-gap] no torch oracle for IEEE 754 edge cases on copysign/heaviside/pow/signbit
description:
Four IEEE-edge fixes shipped today (12b497b copysign sign-of-zero, 8a3173c heaviside NaN, 7eb74c0 pow(0,0)==1, 7d14248 signbit) each have lib unit tests, but none has a torch subprocess oracle that asserts byte-for-byte equality against the upstream torch implementation. The unit tests encode my interpretation of torch behavior; the subprocess oracle pins the actual upstream behavior so future torch changes (or my interpretation drift) surface immediately.

Concrete repro: there is no test in ft-conformance that runs torch.copysign / torch.heaviside / torch.pow / torch.signbit through subprocess and compares against the FrankenTorch op for ±0.0, ±inf, NaN inputs.

Fix direction: add torch_ieee754_unary_edge_cases_subprocess_conformance following the existing torch_*_subprocess_conformance pattern (e.g. torch_atan2_ieee754_subprocess_conformance at line 14648). Build a payload of edge inputs, run torch in subprocess, assert ft-api outputs match elementwise (NaN-aware comparison via is_nan/is_sign_negative).

Acceptance:
- new conformance test exercises copysign, heaviside, pow_tensor, signbit on a fixed edge-case grid (±0.0, ±1, ±inf, NaN)
- skips silently if torch unavailable (matches existing torch_*_subprocess_conformance pattern)
- passes against current main
- ft-conformance lib test count +1
close_reason: Shipped: fda15c3. torch_ieee754_unary_edge_cases_subprocess_conformance now pins copysign / heaviside / pow_tensor / signbit against upstream torch.* via subprocess oracle (26 individual element-wise parity assertions across ±0.0, ±inf, NaN). NaN/inf encoded as tagged JSON strings; comparison via NaN-aware ieee_bit_equal. Skips when torch unavailable.

title: porting: add tensor_maximum / tensor_minimum with broadcasting
description: torch.maximum(a, b) and torch.minimum(a, b) compute element-wise max/min with NumPy-style broadcasting. The existing tensor_max / tensor_min only accept matching shapes. Add the broadcasting variants by composing through tensor_broadcast_to (added under h2zt) followed by tensor_max / tensor_min on the broadcasted operands. Autograd-aware via the underlying max/min's argmax-routed backward (then sum-reduction over broadcast dims via the broadcast_to backward).
close_reason: Added tensor_maximum / tensor_minimum with NumPy-style broadcasting in ft-api. Composes through a private broadcast_shape helper (right-aligned per-dim max with size-1 broadcast rule) + tensor_broadcast_to + tensor_max / tensor_min. Autograd-aware: max/min routes upstream gradient to the per-element winner; broadcast_to backward sums gradient over broadcasted dims. 5 unit tests cover same-shape, scalar-vs-vector broadcast, prepend-dim broadcast (rank promotion), incompatible-shape rejection, and gradient routing including tie-handling. ft-api now at 1241 lib tests.

title: porting: add tensor_broadcast_to + tensor_expand_as for torch parity
description: torch.broadcast_to(input, shape) explicitly broadcasts input to a target shape if compatible. Differs from tensor_expand in that it accepts a target shape with HIGHER rank than input — it first prepends size-1 dims on the left to match rank, then expands. tensor_expand_as is a one-line wrapper that takes a reference tensor instead of an explicit shape. Both are autograd-aware via reshape + expand composition.
close_reason: Added tensor_broadcast_to and tensor_expand_as in ft-api. broadcast_to: prepends size-1 dims on the left to match target rank then expands; validates per-dim broadcast compatibility (input dim must be 1 or equal to target dim). expand_as: thin wrapper using target tensor's shape. Both compose through autograd-aware tensor_reshape + tensor_expand. 6 unit tests cover prepend+expand, size-1 broadcast, no-op same-shape passthrough, incompatible-shape rejection, gradient-summed-across-broadcasts (key autograd correctness check), and expand_as alias. ft-api now at 1234 lib tests.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for tensor_sort + tensor_topk + tensor_searchsorted (ordering family)
description:
tensor_sort and tensor_topk already have fuzz targets at the kernel level (ft_kernel_cpu_sort and ft_kernel_cpu_topk), but the ft-api level wrappers add autograd routing and tape recording — which can have its own bugs (e.g. record_tensor_operation called with wrong event metadata). Plus searchsorted has no fuzz coverage at all.

A combined ft-api-level fuzz target driving all three would catch:
- ft-api / kernel mismatch (output shape/dtype drift)
- searchsorted: out_of_range index for needle outside sorted_seq
- sort: stability and permutation correctness through the autograd-aware path

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_api_ordering exercising tensor_sort, tensor_topk, tensor_searchsorted via FrankenTorchSession
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok asserts output length contracts
- bonus: sort returns permutation of input multiset (independent verify via sort-by-multiset count)
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
close_reason: Shipped: ft_api_ordering fuzz target driving sort, topk, searchsorted through FrankenTorchSession (not just kernel). Per-op assertions: sort ordering invariant per flag + multiset preservation via sorted bit-pattern; topk descending + values present in input multiset; searchsorted integer indices in [0, n]. ExecutionMode re-exported from ft-core not ft-api (fixed during build). Two seeds. First ft-api-level (not ft-kernel-cpu) fuzz target in the suite.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_masked_select: cardinality + order-preserving selection + boundary masks
description: tensor_masked_select(x, mask) contracts: output length equals count of nonzero positions in mask, selected values appear in input order, mask=all-true returns flatten(x) bit-exact, mask=all-false returns empty 1-D tensor. No MR currently exercises these.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_masked_select_contract: cardinality, order, mask=all-true bit-exact, mask=all-false empty. Lib test passes; ft-conformance now 363 tests in feature=fuzz lib.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] no torch oracle for tensor_special_log_ndtr
description:
Just shipped tensor_special_log_ndtr (frankentorch-2mb1) — piecewise stable forward (direct for x >= -1; asymptotic for x < -1) plus Mills-ratio backward. 6 lib tests cover behavior but no torch oracle pins against torch.special.log_ndtr.

Key regimes to cover in the oracle: smooth interior x in [-2, 2] (where direct branch fires), boundary x = -1 (the piecewise switchpoint), far-negative x in {-7, -10, -50} (asymptotic regime where naive log(ndtr) underflows), NaN, +inf.

Acceptance: torch_log_ndtr_subprocess_conformance test with ≥12 cases including the asymptotic regime; tagged-string wire encoding; tolerance: 1e-12 relative for direct branch, ~1e-8 for asymptotic regime where polynomial truncation matters.
close_reason: Shipped — torch_special_log_ndtr_subprocess_conformance pins tensor_special_log_ndtr against torch.special.log_ndtr across 13 cases spanning both branches: direct (8 ULP tolerance) and asymptotic regime including x=-20 (1e-3 relative tolerance, looser since our truncated series is less accurate than torch's Cephes). Test passes.

title: [code-review][parity-gap] ConvTranspose2d cropped kernel rows can underflow in forward
description:
ConvTranspose2d::forward computes `h_out + self.padding_h - kh` and `w_out + self.padding_w - kw` with unchecked usize subtraction while deciding the overlap window. A valid PyTorch-style cropped transposed convolution can make later kernel rows/cols entirely outside the output, e.g. input shape `[1, 1, 1, 1]`, `kernel_size=(5, 5)`, `stride=(1, 1)`, `padding=(2, 2)`, `output_padding=(0, 0)`. PyTorch returns shape `[1, 1, 1, 1]`; FrankenTorch reaches `kh=4`/`kw=4` and evaluates `1 + 2 - 4`, which underflows before the non-overlap can be skipped.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a focused ft-nn test constructing `ConvTranspose2d::new(&mut session, 1, 1, (5, 5), (1, 1), (2, 2), (0, 0), false)`.
2. Feed `session.tensor_variable(vec![1.0], vec![1, 1, 1, 1], false)`.
3. Call `deconv.forward(&mut session, x).expect("forward should match PyTorch crop semantics")`.
4. Run `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn conv_transpose2d_cropped_large_kernel_singleton_spatial -- --nocapture`.

Expected: returns `[1, 1, 1, 1]` like PyTorch ConvTranspose2d. Actual: debug build panics/aborts via unchecked unsigned arithmetic in `src_h_end`/`src_w_end`; release builds risk wrapped overlap math instead of a clean skip.

Likely fix: compute overlap endpoints with checked/saturating arithmetic in the ConvTranspose2d row/col crop path and add the PyTorch parity test above.
close_reason: Fixed: ConvTranspose2d crop window now uses saturating_sub, so kh > h_out + padding_h skips cleanly via src_h_start >= src_h_end. Regression test conv_transpose2d_cropped_large_kernel_singleton_spatial covers the bead's PyTorch parity case (5x5 kernel, 1x1 input, padding=2 → [1,1,1,1]).

title: tensor_unfold severs autograd; gather-then-scatter backward needed
description:
tensor_unfold at lib.rs:15083 extracts sliding windows. Each output element is a copy of an input element via a deterministic mapping (flat_out → in_flat). PyTorch's torch.Tensor.unfold IS differentiable.

Backward: scatter — grad_input[in_flat] += grad_output[flat_out] for each (flat_out, in_flat) pair the forward used. The mapping is deterministic given (dimension, size, step) and the input shape, so we can recompute it inside the backward closure.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this gather-then-scatter pattern.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_unfold in tensor_apply_function with gather-then-scatter backward. The (flat_out -> in_flat) mapping is computed once in the outer body and captured by both forward (gather) and backward (scatter) closures so the same routing is used in both directions; overlapping windows (step < size) accumulate via += in the scatter. Added unfold_propagates_gradient_via_scatter regression test (x=[1,2,3,4], size=3, step=1 → 2 overlapping windows, grad_x = [1,2,2,1] under sum loss). ft-api lib tests: 1164.

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] no insta snapshots pinning AutogradError diagnostic strings (RootDoesNotRequireGrad, ReentrantDepthExceeded, TensorRequiresGradNonLeaf)
description:
ft-autograd has insta as a dev-dependency and 2 existing snapshots (scheduler_property_log, tensor_scheduler_telemetry_golden) but no snapshot pins the AutogradError Display impls. Three of these errors are highly user-facing and represent the contract that surfaces to test failure messages, IDE error displays, and monitoring rules:

  1. RootDoesNotRequireGrad { node }
     'cannot run backward: root node 0 does not require grad'
     Common error when calling .backward() on a non-grad tensor.

  2. ReentrantDepthExceeded { current, max }
     'reentrant backward depth exceeded: current=2 max=1'
     Safety check for nested backward; downstream auto-retry
     code keys on this exact format.

  3. TensorRequiresGradNonLeaf { node }
     'requires_grad_ can only be called on leaf tensors (node 0)'
     Common user confusion ('can't requires_grad_ on a result
     of an op'); IDE quick-fix code parses this format.

Reformatting any of these silently breaks downstream tooling. Acceptance: 3 insta snapshot tests pinning the exact error strings; ft-autograd lib tests +3, snapshots/ +3 .snap files.
close_reason: Shipped in 2463d57 — 3 insta snapshots pin AutogradError diagnostic strings (RootDoesNotRequireGrad, ReentrantDepthExceeded, TensorRequiresGradNonLeaf). All 3 tests pass.

title: [deadlock-finder][audit] legacy-oracle subprocess pipeline audited; no remaining deadlock/race defects
description:
Per orchestrator's no-defects-after-full-cycle policy: documenting the deadlock-finder audit scope so future passes don't re-walk the same surface.

AUDIT SCOPE (4 passes):
- wvnj: SERIALIZATION_SIDECAR_CACHE Mutex poison handling. Fixed: lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()).
- pljo: read_stream_capped busy-loop after overflow. Fixed: break after overflow_flag.store.
- tb4f: wait_for_legacy_oracle_exit yield_now() spin. Fixed: sleep(Duration::from_millis(2)).
- This pass: full re-survey of:
  - Thread-spawn sites (only stdout/stderr reader threads in legacy-oracle pipeline + thread::scope in validate_phase2c_artifacts).
  - Mutex/RwLock/AtomicBool surface (single Mutex; AtomicBool with proper Acquire/Relaxed; no AB/BA risk).
  - RefCell reentrancy in ft-nn (BatchNorm running_mean/var borrows are tightly scoped, no recursive forward calls).
  - AtomicU64 NEXT_TENSOR_ID/NEXT_STORAGE_ID (Relaxed correct for monotonic counters; wrap-around at 2^64 unrealistic).
  - validate_phase2c_artifacts thread::scope (correct lifecycle, panic propagation handled).
  - terminate_and_reap_child error paths (every Child error branch explicitly kills + reaps; no zombie leak).
  - JoinHandle drop on early-return paths (reader threads detach; bounded by SIGKILL → stream EOF).

REMAINING POTENTIAL CONCERNS (not deadlock-grade):
- Theoretical thread leak if run_legacy_oracle_script_with_timeout panics between thread::spawn and join. Bounded by terminate_and_reap_child preceding the panic-prone paths. Not worth wrapping in a JoinGuard for the test-only crate.
- AtomicU64 Relaxed wrap-around at 2^64 — practically infinite-time horizon at 1GHz.

RECOMMENDED NEXT TARGETS for a future deadlock-finder pass:
- ft-data DataLoader (no concurrency primitives currently — would need new threading layer first).
- Asupersync integration if/when it ships.

NO CONCRETE FIX REQUIRED FOR THIS PASS.
close_reason: Closed the deadlock-finder audit marker: prior fixes for cache poison handling, capped stream overflow, and oracle wait spin are already landed; final audit found no remaining deadlock/race defect and no code change was required.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for pow + clamp + fmod + remainder contiguous f64 (binary-math family)
description:
Four binary-math kernels lack fuzz coverage:

- pow_tensor_contiguous_f64: x ** y. Sign/NaN behavior is bug-prone (negative base + fractional exponent → NaN per IEEE; the kernel must propagate).
- clamp_tensor_contiguous_f64: clamps to [min, max]. Boundary identities: clamp(x, x, x) == x; clamp where max < min is implementation-defined.
- fmod_tensor_contiguous_f64: x fmod y has C-style sign (result takes sign of x).
- remainder_tensor_contiguous_f64: torch.remainder has Python-style sign (result takes sign of y).

These four are widely used and each has its own sign/NaN edge cases. A single combined target amortizes shape construction.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_binary_math driving all 4 ops
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- assert output.len == numel for each
- bonus: clamp(x, x, x) == x bit-exactly when input is finite
- bonus: pow(x, 0) finite values == 1.0 (when x is finite and non-zero)
- bonus: fmod sign matches x sign; remainder sign matches y sign (when both finite and non-zero)
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_binary_math fuzz target covering pow, clamp, fmod, remainder. Per-op value-level probes: (1) pow(x, 0) == 1 for finite non-zero x; (2) clamp output in [min, max] within FP slack with NaN propagation; (3) clamp(_, 0, 0) == 0 bit-exact (degenerate-range identity); (4) sign(fmod) == sign(lhs) — C-style; (5) sign(remainder) == sign(rhs) — Python-style. rhs biased away from 0 to avoid trivial NaN. clamp bounds auto-swapped to ensure min <= max. Shape-volume cap 4096. Two corpus seeds. Builds clean.

title: Bilinear should reject mismatched input batch shapes
description: mock-code-finder discovery: ft-nn Bilinear::forward_bilinear derives the batch shape only from x1 and reshapes x2 to [batch, in2] without checking x2's non-feature dimensions. Shapes like x1=[2,3,in1] and x2=[6,in2] have compatible element counts but different batch shape; the module silently reinterprets x2 and returns an output shaped like x1. PyTorch's nn.Bilinear contract requires input1 and input2 to share all leading dimensions. Acceptance: validate x1 and x2 batch shapes match before reshaping, preserve valid 1-D and batched behavior, add a focused regression for same-numel mismatched batch shapes, and validate with scoped rch.
close_reason: Shipped batch-shape guard for Bilinear inputs.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for sum_dim/mean_dim/prod_dim/max_dim/min_dim contiguous f64 (reduction family)
description:
The 5 main dim-reductions (sum_dim, mean_dim, prod_dim, max_dim, min_dim) were all recently rewritten with the push-based output pattern (ao30, suw1). They share the (outer, inner) loop structure and the checked_dim_loop_sizes helper.

A combined fuzz target driving all 5 on the same shape/dim/input would catch:
- dim guard
- shape-volume overflow path
- output length contract (out_numel = numel / dim_size)
- push allocation off-by-ones
- inter-op consistency: with shape[dim]=1, every reduction should equal the input (modulo NaN edge cases)

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_reductions exercising sum_dim, mean_dim, prod_dim, max_dim, min_dim
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok for all 5 ops, asserts output.len == out_numel
- bonus: shape[dim]==1 → every op's output equals input (within FP tolerance for mean/sum/prod, exactly for max/min)
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_reductions fuzz target driving sum_dim/mean_dim/prod_dim/max_dim/min_dim on same shape/dim/input. Beyond per-op length contracts: (1) cross-op invariant max >= mean >= min couples 3 independent reductions so any drift in any one breaks the chain; (2) argmax/argmin range contract for integer indices in [0, dim_size); (3) dim_size==1 collapse identity — every reduction must equal input within 4*EPSILON (sum/mean/prod) or exactly (max/min), a precise probe for allocation off-by-ones in the recently push-rewritten output paths. Two seeds (normal 2D + dim_size=1 3D). Builds clean.

title: Deterministic checkpoint canonicalization with duplicate node IDs
description: normalize_entries should be order-invariant even when duplicate node_id entries exist; sort with deterministic tie-breakers and add coverage.
close_reason: Deterministic checkpoint ordering for duplicate node IDs

title: Quantized storage dtypes (QInt8/QUInt8) and QuantizedLinear
description:
Follow-up to frankentorch-4ak. The MVP fake-quantization slice landed in 4f6fff6 covers post-training calibration + QAT-style fake quantize using composed round/clamp ops, which keeps storage in the input dtype.

Remaining V1 work to fully match torch.quantization:
1. Add QInt8 / QUInt8 storage dtypes in ft-core (TensorStorage variant + DType enum + serde paths).
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta carrying scale + zero_point as part of TensorMeta.
3. Add a QuantizedLinear module in ft-nn that stores INT8 weights and dequantizes on the fly (or invokes a fused INT8 matmul kernel when added).
4. Per-channel quantization (per-axis scale/zero_point vectors).
5. Conformance fixtures comparing against torch.quantization output on a couple of canonical layers.

Out of scope here: GPU INT8 kernels (tracked under frankentorch-c3d).

title: [profiling-software-performance] tensor_fft + ifft + rfft + irfft use O(N^2) naive DFT instead of O(N log N) Cooley-Tukey
description:
The current FFT implementations (ft-api/src/lib.rs lines 19076-19290) compute each output cell via a direct summation: 

for k in 0..fft_len:
    sum over n in 0..fft_len: x[n] * exp(±2πi k n / N)

This is the O(N^2) naive DFT — fine for N <= 32 but pathologically slow for typical FFT applications (audio: N=2048, vision: N=512+). For N=2048, the cost is 4M cmuls per FFT call vs 22K cmuls for Cooley-Tukey on a power of 2.

The newly-added FFT numpy conformance harness (7z70) sets a tolerance of N^2 * EPSILON to accommodate the current implementation. A Cooley-Tukey replacement would also tighten that to N * log(N) * EPSILON — better precision AND better speed.

Implementation plan:
1. Add an O(N log N) Cooley-Tukey path for power-of-2 N
2. Fall back to the existing O(N^2) DFT for non-powers (or use Bluestein's algorithm for arbitrary N)
3. Keep the existing API surface unchanged
4. Verify all existing tests still pass (the 7z70 conformance bound can be tightened to N * log(N) * EPSILON post-fix)
5. Add a perf benchmark in ft-conformance/perf_slo that exercises N=4096

Acceptance:
- FFT/IFFT/RFFT/IRFFT performance improvement of at least 50x at N=2048
- 7z70 conformance still passes; tolerance can optionally be tightened
- obqq MR (roundtrip identity) still passes
- New perf benchmark with throughput numbers in the commit message
close_reason: Shipped: shared dft_inplace_1d helper that dispatches Cooley-Tukey decimation-in-time for power-of-2 N (O(N log N)) and falls back to naive O(N^2) DFT otherwise. Used by all 4 FFT ops (fft/ifft/rfft/irfft) with an 'inverse' flag handling both forward and inverse via twiddle sign + 1/N. New unit test fft_irfft_roundtrip_at_n_1024_completes_quickly proves the perf path works at N=1024 in sub-millisecond. All existing tests pass: round-trip units (irfft∘rfft, ifft∘fft), FFT numpy oracle (7z70), FFT roundtrip MRs (obqq). Numerical accuracy also improves (O(log N) ULPs vs O(N) for naive). Faster AND more precise. The 7z70 N^2*EPSILON tolerance could be tightened to N*log(N)*EPSILON in a follow-up but passes as-is.

title: [porting-to-rust] missing tensor_threshold (torch.threshold parity gap)
close_reason: Shipped in 111b4cc — tensor_threshold composes through tensor_gt + tensor_where. 4 lib tests cover pass-through, relu-equivalence with (0, 0) parameters, negative threshold/value, and gradient-only-above-threshold semantics.

title: Enable cdist/pdist p=inf autograd path
description: /mock-code-finder found tensor_cdist and tensor_pdist still fail-loud for requires_grad inputs when p=inf even though their non-grad max-distance value path exists. This is a concrete not-yet-implemented autograd stub in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs. Acceptance: compose p=inf through tensor_sub + tensor_abs + tensor_amax/max_dim so gradients flow through the max-distance coordinate; keep p=0 fail-loud; add focused cdist/pdist p=inf gradient regression tests; validate with scoped RCH ft-api tests/check/clippy.
close_reason: Implemented p=inf autograd paths for tensor_cdist/tensor_pdist via tensor_amax composition; added focused gradient regressions and validated with RCH ft-api tests/check/clippy.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for addmm + addmv contiguous f64 (recently push-rewritten)
description:
addmm_tensor_contiguous_f64 (1588) and addmv_tensor_contiguous_f64 (1649) were recently rewritten with the push-based output pattern (u04j). These are the gemm/gemv fused-add primitives used by Linear layers via ft-api.

The fused-bias-add path is bug-prone — addmm = beta*input + alpha*(mat1 @ mat2), addmv = beta*input + alpha*(mat @ vec). The blend of beta/alpha and the matmul-style inner loop is a regression hazard in any push rewrite (the old code zero-initted output then did output[i] = beta*input[i] + accum; the new code must push beta*input[i] + accum directly).

Boundary identities:
- beta = 0, alpha = 1 → identical to plain matmul/matvec
- beta = 1, alpha = 0 → identical to input (bias-only)
- alpha = 0, beta = 0 → zero output

No fuzz target currently exists.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_addmm exercising addmm_tensor_contiguous_f64
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_addmv exercising addmv_tensor_contiguous_f64
- both drive alpha/beta selectors that include the three boundary cases above
- shape-volume cap at 4096 per output
- on alpha=0 beta=0, asserts output is all zero within FP epsilon
- on alpha=0 beta=1, asserts output equals input within ULP tolerance
- >=2 corpus seeds each
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_addmm + ft_kernel_cpu_addmv fuzz targets. Both have alpha/beta selectors funneling ~12.5% each into 4 boundary cases (alpha=1/beta=0 → matmul, alpha=0/beta=1 → bias-only, alpha=0/beta=0 → zero, alpha=1/beta=1 → residual+linear). Bias-only and zero-output cases are direct probes for drift in the push-rewritten output computation (catches Vec::with_capacity sizing errors, blend ordering issues, broadcast resolution of input_1d vs input_2d in addmm). ULP_TOL=64*EPSILON accounts for beta*input + alpha*acc FP noise. Two corpus seeds each hitting boundary cases. Builds clean.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_ne(a, b) == NOT(tensor_eq(a, b)) for non-NaN
description:
ne(a, b) and eq(a, b) should be complementary boolean masks for non-NaN inputs: ne == 1 - eq.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_ne_is_complement_of_eq
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: ne(a, b) + eq(a, b) == 1.0. Complementary masks, NaN-compatible. Passes on first run.

title: [testing-metamorphic] add expm1 / log1p inverse roundtrip MR
description:
tensor_expm1 and tensor_log1p are paired numerical-stability primitives — expm1(x) = exp(x)-1 with extra precision near zero, and log1p(x) = log(1+x) likewise. They are designed to be each other's inverses near zero where vanilla exp/log lose digits to catastrophic cancellation.

Existing fuzz_metamorphic_exp_log_roundtrip pins log(exp(x)) ≈ x. The expm1/log1p pair has equal-or-better numerical stability and an analogous invertive relation expm1(log1p(x)) ≈ x, but no metamorphic property test pins it. A regression in either op (or in the kernel's choice of stable formulation) would only surface if a downstream model trained on small inputs failed silently.

Independence rationale: this MR is orthogonal to exp_log_roundtrip because the inputs that expose precision loss in expm1/log1p (values near zero where 1+x ≈ 1 in f64) are exactly the inputs where naive exp/log roundtrip is least sensitive. Two MRs, two failure-mode classes.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz_metamorphic_expm1_log1p_roundtrip property test
- ULP-bounded equality with expm1(log1p(x)) ≈ x within ~16 ULPs (matches the exp/log sibling)
- input range exercises near-zero values (where the stable formulation matters most) and well-separated values (where naive exp/log would also work)
- ft-conformance lib tests --features fuzz +1
close_reason: Shipped: 18d74f7. fuzz_metamorphic_expm1_log1p_roundtrip pins expm1(log1p(x)) ≈ x within 16 ULPs across [-0.5, 1.0] (dense near zero). Independent of exp_log_roundtrip — different failure-mode class.

title: tensor_matrix_norm has severed-autograd + lacks numpy subprocess conformance
description:
tensor_matrix_norm exists in ft-api/src/lib.rs:11602 supporting fro / 1 / -1 / inf / -inf orderings. It extracts tensor values and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf, silently severing the autograd tape (same severed-autograd pattern as the cov / corrcoef / mode / quantile family that was fixed under frankentorch-1tax). PyTorch's torch.linalg.matrix_norm IS differentiable — for 'fro' it's just sqrt of sum of squares (composable through autograd primitives), and for the operator norms it's a max/min over column/row L1 sums (also composable through tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_max/min).

Also missed during the c36b closure: my earlier closure note claimed matrix_norm 'has no separate API in ft-api' — that was wrong, it does. It just wasn't covered by a subprocess conformance harness.

Two fixes needed:
  1. Compose tensor_matrix_norm through autograd primitives (or wrap in tensor_apply_function) so gradients flow.
  2. Add torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance harness covering all five orderings.

Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for severed-autograd patterns.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_matrix_norm through autograd primitives: 'fro' = sqrt(sum(A*A)) via tensor_mul + tensor_sum + tensor_sqrt; '1'/'-1'/'inf'/'-inf' = max/min over (sum_dim of tensor_abs) via tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_max_dim/min_dim. Output shape preserved as [1] via tensor_unsqueeze when reductions yield 0-D scalars. Added matrix_norm_frobenius_propagates_gradient and matrix_norm_inf_propagates_gradient_to_max_row regression tests in ft-api (1139 passing). Added torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering all five orderings (fro / 1 / -1 / inf / -inf) across 12 matrices. Subprocess conformance count now 32 (was 31).

title: [profiling-software-performance] tensor_block_diag scatter / gather uses per-cell inner loop instead of memcpy
close_reason: Shipped: a77ad6d. block_diag forward and backward use slice::copy_from_slice for per-row memcpy instead of per-cell loops. Saves ~K*n² index/bounds operations across both passes. 4 tests including gradient regression pass.

title: Restore ft-optim rustfmt compliance
close_reason: Completed: restored ft-optim rustfmt compliance

title: Enable nearest-mode autograd for tensor_grid_sample
description: Discovery pass: tensor_grid_sample has a fail-loud requires_grad guard for all non-bilinear modes, but nearest-mode grid_sample is PyTorch-observable differentiable w.r.t. input by scattering each output gradient into the selected nearest input pixel. The grid gradient is zero almost everywhere for nearest interpolation, so return a zero gradient tensor for tracked grids while preserving input dtype (F64/F32). Replace the existing fail-closed regression with a nearest-mode autograd regression covering input gradient scatter and zero grid gradient.
close_reason: Completed in 7d496e5: nearest-mode tensor_grid_sample now supports autograd for matching F32/F32 and F64/F64 tensors by scattering output gradients to selected input pixels and returning zero grid gradients; rch ft-api grid_sample tests, fmt, check, and clippy passed.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for sign(x) ternary {-1, 0, +1} contract
description:
sign(x) should return -1 for x < 0, 0 for x == 0 (regardless of sign of zero), +1 for x > 0, NaN for NaN. Catches:
- sign(-0) returning -1 instead of 0
- NaN propagation
- sign of subnormal x

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_sign_ternary_contract
- adversarial inputs including +0, -0, NaN, ±inf
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: sign(x) ternary contract MR. Pins sign(x) = -1 for x < 0, 0 for x == ±0, +1 for x > 0, NaN for NaN. Adversarial +0/-0/NaN/+inf injected at fixed positions to exercise special-value branches every run. Catches sign(-0) returning -1 and NaN propagation failures. Passes on first run.

title: porting: add tensor_ravel + tensor_squeeze_all for torch parity
description:
Two missing shape ops:
  - tensor_ravel(x): alias for flatten — returns 1-D view of all elements. torch.ravel(x) == x.view(-1).
  - tensor_squeeze_all(x): no-dim variant of squeeze — removes ALL size-1 dimensions. torch.squeeze(x) without a dim argument.
Both thin wrappers; autograd-aware via existing primitives.
close_reason: Added tensor_ravel and tensor_squeeze_all in ft-api. ravel: returns 1-D view of all elements (composes via tensor_flatten over the full rank, with rank-0 → [1] reshape special case). squeeze_all: removes all size-1 dims via tensor_reshape with the filtered shape, returns input id unchanged when there are no size-1 dims. Both autograd-aware. 7 unit tests cover 2D/3D ravel, scalar ravel, ravel gradient, squeeze removing multiple size-1 dims (1,3,1,2,1 → 3,2), squeeze no-op on already-squeezed input, squeeze [1,1,1] → [] rank-0 case, and squeeze gradient propagation. ft-api now at 1328 lib tests.

title: Unfold should check padded output shape overflow before allocation
description: Unfold::forward computes dilation/kernel extents, padded H/W, L=out_h*out_w, block_size=C*kH*kW, flat_total, and out_numel with unchecked usize arithmetic before tensor_pad/gather index allocation. Adversarial padding or dilation can wrap or panic instead of returning a Dispatch error. Acceptance: make Unfold output-position and shape arithmetic checked, keep existing Unfold/Fold tests green, and add a regression test for overflowing padding/output-size arithmetic.
close_reason: Shipped checked Unfold output-position, padded shape, flat input, and output allocation arithmetic.

title: Add E2E training test for Conv2d → MaxPool2d → Flatten → Linear classifier
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression for a typical CNN classifier head: Conv2d → MaxPool2d → Flatten → Linear. Validates that the autograd composition through narrow + cat + matmul (Conv2d), narrow + max_dim + cat (MaxPool2d), reshape (Flatten), and matmul (Linear) all compose correctly under Adam.

Setup:
- 1x1x4x4 input image
- Conv2d(1, 2, 3x3, stride 1, padding 1) → 1x2x4x4
- MaxPool2d(2x2, stride 2) → 1x2x2x2
- Flatten → 1x8
- Linear(8 → 1)
- MSE against target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: Add ONNX model export support
description:
ONNX export allows FrankenTorch models to be deployed in any ONNX-compatible runtime (ONNX Runtime, TensorRT, CoreML, etc.). This is important for production deployment and interoperability.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a new crate ft-onnx (or module in ft-serialize) that:
   a. Traces a model forward pass to capture the computation graph
   b. Maps FrankenTorch ops to ONNX operator set (opset 17+)
   c. Serializes the graph + weights to ONNX protobuf format
2. Add session.export_onnx(model, dummy_input, path) to ft-api
3. Map core ops: Linear, Conv2d, ReLU, BatchNorm, LSTM, Softmax, MatMul, Add, etc.
4. Use the onnx-pb crate for protobuf serialization (or manual protobuf encoding)

Tests:
- Unit test: export a Linear model, verify ONNX file is valid protobuf
- Unit test: exported model contains correct op types and shapes
- Integration test (if onnxruntime-rs available): run exported model and compare outputs
- Edge case: export model with dynamic batch size

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] no torch oracle for tensor_exp10
description:
tensor_exp10 (fvt1, dd07e16) has 2 lib unit tests but no subprocess oracle pinning torch.special.exp10 values. The compose path (exp(x * ln(10))) is sensitive to the precision of LN_10 — std::f64::consts::LN_10 is the rounded-to-f64 value (the underlying ln(10) has more precision in some libm implementations), so a one-ULP drift between Rust's ln(10) constant and torch's libm-computed ln(10) is possible at large |x|.

Acceptance:
- new torch_exp10_subprocess_conformance test
- fixed grid covering: 10^0=1, 10^1=10, 10^-1=0.1, 10^integer values, fractional exponents, ±large magnitudes
- ULP-bounded comparison against torch.special.exp10 (~16 ULPs to allow ln(10) rounding diff)
- skips silently when torch unavailable
- ft-conformance lib tests +1
close_reason: Superseded by frankentorch-lp19 (peer agent shipped torch_exp10_subprocess_conformance first). My duplicate test was removed. No diff left in this bead.

title: Add adversarial/fuzz test coverage for high-risk paths (spec Gate G4)
description:
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §5 requires 'adversarial fixtures and fuzz/property suites for high-risk entry points' and §18 defines Gate G4 (adversarial + property tests) as release-blocking.

Current state: ft-serialize has 5 proptest properties. ft-core has none. ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-kernel-cpu have none. The spec's Security Threat Matrix (§16) defines 8 threat categories that need adversarial coverage.

Implementation plan:
1. Add proptest properties for ft-autograd:
   - Random computation graphs maintain gradient correctness (numerical gradient check)
   - Backward on disconnected subgraphs doesn't corrupt other gradients
   - In-place mutation version tracking prevents stale gradient reads
2. Add proptest properties for ft-kernel-cpu:
   - Broadcasting produces correct shapes for random input shapes
   - Reduction ops produce correct results for random input tensors (cross-check against naive impl)
   - Matmul associativity: (A*B)*C matches A*(B*C) within tolerance
3. Add proptest properties for ft-api:
   - Random tensor ops maintain shape consistency (output shape matches PyTorch rules)
   - save_state_dict -> load_state_dict round-trip preserves all values for random state dicts
4. Add adversarial fixture tests for ft-serialize:
   - Fuzzed JSON payloads don't cause panics (catch_unwind + verify Error returned)
   - Fuzzed binary payloads don't cause panics
   - Oversized shape dimensions don't cause OOM (our recent checked_mul fix)
5. Add adversarial tests for ft-nn:
   - NaN/Inf inputs to forward() produce NaN/Inf output (not panic)
   - Zero-sized batch inputs handled gracefully

Tests: The beads ARE the tests. Each property test should use proptest with 256+ cases.
