Strict-mode envelope contracts already have insta snapshots (strict_checkpoint_json_contract_snapshot, hardened_checkpoint_json_contract_snapshot). Diagnostic-message contracts are the symmetric gap.

Acceptance:
- new test hardened_malformed_payload_diagnostic_snapshot uses insta::assert_snapshot! to pin the exact error message for a fixed malformed input (the same '{ not json' fixture as the existing test)
- snap file checked into src/snapshots/
- existing hardened_malformed_payload_returns_bounded_diagnostic test left intact (the bounded-length contract is independent of the wording contract)
- ft-serialize lib tests +1
close_reason: Shipped: 92c280b. hardened_malformed_payload_diagnostic_snapshot pins exact diagnostic 'invalid json: key must be a string at line 1 column 3; payload_prefix={ not json'. Companion to existing checkpoint envelope snapshots; bounded-length contract preserved as orthogonal assertion.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_topk first element == max
description:
For tensor_topk(x, k=1, largest=true) on a 1-D tensor, the returned value equals max(x). Catches drift between topk and max on the simplest case.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_topk_1_equals_max
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: topk(x, 1, largest=true).value == max_dim(x, 0).value bit-exact. NaN inputs skipped. Catches drift between topk and max_dim on the simplest k=1 case. Passes on first run.

title: [code-review] Roll normalization lacks inverse metamorphic coverage
description:
Review source: six-hour audit of ef8cbec roll-shift normalization and backward changes.

Finding: the fix added point coverage for isize::MAX / isize::MIN on a two-element vector, but no metamorphic inverse relation asserting roll(roll(x, s), -s) == x and that backward through the composed rolls routes gradients identically. That leaves a realistic regression class uncovered: sign errors in inverse_normalized_roll_shift/add_normalized_roll_shift can pass single-shift value tests while failing composed forward/backward invariants.

Concrete repro steps:
1. In crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs, mutate inverse_normalized_roll_shift to return shift_mod instead of dim_size - shift_mod.
2. Existing point tests still cover only selected cases and can miss the composed inverse relation.
3. Add a test that rolls a length-3 tracked tensor by isize::MAX, rolls back by -isize::MAX, asserts values equal the original, then applies a weighted sum and asserts gradients equal the weights.

Expected closure: add a metamorphic inverse test for forward identity and backward gradient routing.
close_reason: Closed in review mode: added roll_inverse_metamorphic_preserves_values_and_gradients to lock roll(roll(x, s), -s) value identity and backward gradient routing for extreme shifts.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for atan(tan(x)) periodic identity (x in (-π/2, π/2))
description:
atan(tan(x)) == x for x in the principal branch (-π/2, π/2). Beyond this range, atan returns the equivalent angle mod π. Restricting to the principal branch gives a clean round-trip MR.

Catches:
- libm atan/tan precision drift
- autograd severance through inverse trig

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_atan_of_tan_principal_branch
- inputs restricted to roughly [-1.5, 1.5] (within principal branch)
- 16 ULP tolerance
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: atan(tan(x)) ≈ x for x in (-1.5, 1.5) — principal branch, clear of tan poles. 16 ULP scaled tolerance. With zi3o (Pythagorean) and fc2a (hyperbolic) the trig-family MR coverage is now thorough. Passes on first run.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for safetensors save_safetensors + load_safetensors roundtrip
description:
Companion to 9ec1 for the safetensors path. There's already an ft_serialize_safetensors fuzz target (decode-only), but no encode+decode round-trip target. Adding one would catch any drift between save and load paths for the safetensors format.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_serialize_safetensors_roundtrip
- generate small tensor map, save → load → compare
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
close_reason: Shipped: ft_serialize_safetensors_roundtrip + extracted save_safetensors_to_bytes API. The bytes-only save variant avoids disk I/O for fast fuzz throughput; original save_safetensors now delegates. Roundtrip target builds 1..=4 small F64 tensors, encodes, decodes, asserts: count + shape + dtype + bit-exact f64 values. Companion to 9ec1 (checkpoint roundtrip). ft-serialize 87/87 still pass.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for matmul + bmm contiguous f64 (recently push-rewritten)
description:
matmul_tensor_contiguous_f64 (1512) and bmm_tensor_contiguous_f64 (1794) were recently rewritten with the push-based output pattern (z588, mg2p). These are the most numerically critical primitives in the workspace — every linear layer / attention layer routes through them — so a regression here corrupts every model trained against the kernel.

No fuzz target currently exercises:
- m, k, n shape extraction
- checked_mul overflow guards
- ensure_dtype_device_and_layout
- the inner triple loop output via push (catches off-by-one in Vec::with_capacity sizing)

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_matmul exercising matmul_tensor_contiguous_f64
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_bmm exercising bmm_tensor_contiguous_f64
- shape-volume cap at 4096 per output
- on Ok, asserts output.len == m*n (matmul) / batch*m*n (bmm)
- bonus: assert finiteness of output when inputs are bounded
- >=2 corpus seeds each
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_matmul + ft_kernel_cpu_bmm fuzz targets. Both cap shape extents at 16 (matmul) / 8 (bmm) and shape volumes at 4096. With input in [-3.2, 3.2] and k <= 16, worst-case |dot product| ~163 << f64 max, so the finiteness invariant on every output cell is a precise probe for NaN injection or stride miscomputation reading past the array. Two corpus seeds each (rectangular + square). Builds clean.

title: Add E2E training test for LayerNorm + Linear (Transformer FFN-like block)
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression for LayerNorm → Linear, the typical pre-norm FFN block in Transformers. Validates LayerNorm composition (mean/var/normalize/affine) chained with Linear under Adam.

Setup:
- 1x4x8 input (batch=1, seq=4, embed=8)
- LayerNorm(normalized_shape=[8])
- Linear(8 → 4)
- MSE against fixed target [1, 4, 4]
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.5 (relaxed since LN normalizes)

title: Fail closed on invalid functional dropout probabilities and embedding indices
description: ft-api functional helpers currently accept invalid inputs too permissively: functional_dropout does not reject p<0 or p>1, and functional_embedding lossy-casts float indices to usize instead of rejecting negative or fractional values. Tighten these APIs to explicit fail-closed behavior and add targeted tests.
close_reason: Completed

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR suite for tensor_special_ndtri (sign symmetry + monotonicity + roundtrip)
description:
tensor_special_ndtri (gf4d) ships with a torch oracle (2hbw) and 3 unit tests, but no metamorphic suite pins its oracle-free structural identities:

  1. EQUIVALENCE (sign symmetry): ndtri(1-p) = -ndtri(p) for any p in (0, 1). The standard normal is symmetric so its inverse CDF flips sign around p=0.5. Catches sign-routing bugs in the (2p-1) compose chain.

  2. INCLUSIVE (monotonicity): ndtri is strictly increasing across (0, 1). Catches sign-flipped erfinv or wrong-direction compose.

  3. INVERTIVE (roundtrip): ndtri(ndtr(x)) ≈ x for any real x. The CDF→ICDF roundtrip identity. Catches systemic drift in either op.

Property-based input generation with proptest. Accept loose tolerances (~1e-3 absolute) because of erfinv's accuracy floor.
close_reason: Shipped — 3 MRs (sign symmetry, monotonicity, inverse roundtrip) for tensor_special_ndtri. Loose 1e-3 tolerance covers erfinv's ~1e-4 accuracy floor. All 3 pass.

title: porting: add tensor_softmin functional API
description: tensor_softmin(input, dim) = softmax(-input, dim). Already implemented as nn::Module Softmin but missing as a top-level functional API. Add a one-liner wrapper composing tensor_neg + tensor_softmax — autograd-aware.
close_reason: Added tensor_softmin functional API in ft-api: softmin(x, dim) = softmax(-x, dim). One-line composition through tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; autograd-aware via the underlying ops. 3 unit tests verify softmax(-x) equivalence, sums-to-one + smaller-input-gets-higher-weight property, and gradient sum invariant (each row of softmax-Jacobian sums to 0). ft-api now at 1278 lib tests.

title: [parity-gap] torch.sgn missing in ft-api despite tensor_sign already present
description:
torch.sgn is the unified sign op: for real-valued tensors it equals torch.sign (returns -1/0/+1); for complex-valued tensors it returns x/|x| preserving angle (with 0 for x=0). FrankenTorch has tensor_sign but not tensor_sgn — code ported from torch using sgn must be rewritten to sign on the way over.

Concrete repro: any module that uses torch.sgn(x) — common in normalization layers and complex-valued networks — fails to compile in ft-api. The fix is trivial for the real path: alias to tensor_sign. Complex path is a separate concern (frankentorch's complex ops fail loud on requires_grad anyway).

Acceptance:
- tensor_sgn delegates to tensor_sign for F32/F64 inputs
- complex inputs (Complex64/Complex128) fail loud with a tracking-bead reference (matches the existing fail-loud-on-complex pattern in the rest of ft-api)
- regression test covering both real-path (matches sign) and the complex-rejection guard
- ft-api lib tests +2
close_reason: Shipped: 16104c0. tensor_sgn delegates to tensor_sign for real, fails loud on complex with bead reference, rejects other dtypes. 2 tests including a tensor_variable_from_storage Complex64 path.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_neg double-negation involution
description:
neg(neg(x)) == x bit-exactly for all finite x. Double negation is the simplest involution and a sharp probe for sign-bit manipulation in the neg implementation.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_neg_double_neg_involution
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Already covered: fuzz_metamorphic_neg_neg_is_identity exists at line 12606 (also a kernel-level fuzz_corpus_prop_unary_neg_neg_via_dispatch at line 10966). Discipline check passed by grep before implementing.

title: Sparse COO to_dense should sum duplicate indices
description: SparseCOOTensor::to_dense overwrote duplicate indices instead of summing; fix to accumulate duplicates (including dense-block case) and add regression tests.
close_reason: Accumulate duplicate COO entries in to_dense

title: [mock-code-finder] parameters_to_vector severs parameter gradients
description: mock-code-finder found ft-nn::parameters_to_vector flattening parameters by extracting session.tensor_values(parameter) into a Vec and rebuilding a fresh requires_grad=false tensor_variable. PyTorch's torch.nn.utils.parameters_to_vector preserves autograd by concatenating parameter views, so downstream losses through the flattened vector should propagate gradients back to the original parameters. Acceptance: rewrite parameters_to_vector to compose through tensor_reshape/tensor_cat (or equivalent autograd-aware primitives), preserve value order/roundtrip behavior, handle empty parameter lists deliberately, and add a regression proving sum(parameters_to_vector(params)).backward() produces gradients on each source parameter.
close_reason: Shipped: parameters_to_vector now reshapes each non-empty parameter and concatenates tensor nodes, preserving autograd to source parameters while keeping empty input behavior deliberate. Added regression proving sum(parameters_to_vector([weight,bias])) backpropagates ones to both source tensors and retained roundtrip restore coverage. Validation: rch cargo test -p ft-nn parameters_to_vector -- --nocapture; rch cargo test -p ft-nn parameters_vector -- --nocapture; rch cargo fmt -p ft-nn -- --check; rch cargo check -p ft-nn --all-targets; rch cargo clippy -p ft-nn --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: [porting-to-rust] missing tensor_special_i1 / i1e (modified Bessel of first kind, order 1) — unblocks i0 autograd
description:
Sister bead of 2vxa (i0/i0e). torch.special.i1 / i1e are the modified Bessel functions of the first kind, order 1. Used in:
  - the von Mises distribution gradient (d/dκ log_pdf involves i0 and i1)
  - heat-equation kernels and statistical priors
  - unblocking the autograd backward for i0/i0e: d/dx i0(x) = i1(x)

Implementation mirrors i0 via A&S 9.8.3 / 9.8.4 piecewise polynomials:

  - |x| < 3.75: power series
    i1(x)/x ≈ 0.5 + 0.87890594*t + 0.51498869*t² + 0.15084934*t³
             + 0.02658733*t⁴ + 0.00301532*t⁵ + 0.00032411*t⁶
    where t = (x/3.75)²
  - |x| >= 3.75: asymptotic (signed by sign(x))
    i1(x) ≈ sign(x) * exp(|x|)/sqrt(|x|) * P(3.75/|x|)

Unlike i0, i1 is ODD: i1(-x) = -i1(x). The asymptotic branch must preserve the sign.

i1e(x) = exp(-|x|) * i1(x) — same exp-scaling pattern as i0e, stays bounded for large |x|.

Acceptance: tensor_special_i1 + tensor_special_i1e on FrankenTorchSession; fail-loud on requires_grad initially (autograd composition would need i0 and yet another order); ≥6 lib tests covering known values (i1(0) = 0, i1(1) ≈ 0.565159), odd symmetry (i1(-x) = -i1(x)), NaN propagation, fail-loud, i1e bounded at large x.
close_reason: Shipped — tensor_special_i1 + i1e via A&S 9.8.3/9.8.4 piecewise polynomials. Odd in x; i1e bounded at large x via exp(-|x|) folded into asymptotic prefactor. 7 lib tests cover known values, odd symmetry, NaN, fail-loud, and bounded i1e. Wiring i0/i0e autograd via this i1 remains a follow-up (also needs i2).

title: porting: add tensor_logical_not + tensor_copysign for torch parity
description:
Two simple element-wise ops:
  - logical_not(x): 1 if x == 0 else 0. Composes via tensor_eq with a zeros constant.
  - copysign(magnitude, sign): |magnitude| * sign(sign_tensor). Composes via tensor_abs + tensor_sign + tensor_mul.
Both are non-differentiable (logical_not is integer-valued; copysign uses sign which has a Dirac at 0). Fail-loud on requires_grad.
close_reason: Added tensor_logical_not and tensor_copysign in ft-api. logical_not: composes via tensor_eq against zeros constant, returns 0/1 floats matching tensor_eq convention. copysign: tensor_abs(magnitude) * tensor_sign(sign_tensor); returns 0 at sign==0 (consistent with tensor_sign(0)=0). Both fail-loud on requires_grad (boolean / sign discontinuities are non-differentiable). 5 unit tests cover forward correctness, requires_grad guards, and shape-mismatch rejection. ft-api now at 1275 lib tests.

title: [testing-fuzzing] add fuzz target for ft-kernel-cpu expand_tensor_contiguous_f64
description:
Companion to xpk8 (ft_kernel_cpu_narrow). expand_tensor_contiguous_f64 (lib.rs:2647) validates that each input dim matches or broadcasts to the target dim, computes checked_shape_numel for the output, then computes broadcast_strides. All three steps do checked arithmetic on caller-controlled inputs — exactly the surface where a regression in any guard would surface as a release-build panic on user-provided shapes.

Existing fuzz coverage: state_dict, safetensors, checkpoint, raptorq, dispatch, sparse tensors, tensor_meta, dense_view, kernel_cpu_narrow. expand is the symmetric companion to narrow on the kernel side and is downstream of every tensor_expand / tensor_broadcast_to in ft-api (used heavily in batch_norm, attention, broadcasting arithmetic).

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_expand drives expand_tensor_contiguous_f64 with arbitrary input shape + target shape (each dim 0..=8)
- caps source numel at 4096 to keep the fuzzer iterating fast
- on Ok, asserts output length equals product(target_shape)
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml [[bin]]
- ≥ 2 corpus seeds covering broadcast and identity-shape paths
- compiles cleanly via rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml
close_reason: Shipped: 2dc9c38. New fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_expand drives expand_tensor_contiguous_f64 with arbitrary input + target shapes, asserts output length == product(target_shape) independently. Source + target numel both capped at 4096. 2 corpus seeds. Compiles cleanly on rch worker.

title: Reject invalid interpolate size and scale_factor
description: Discovery pass with empty ready queue: ft-api::tensor_interpolate accepts zero explicit spatial sizes and non-finite or non-positive scale_factor values. With scale_factor, it casts floor(input_dim * scale) directly to usize, so scale_factor=0/negative/NaN can become zero-sized outputs and +inf can route into huge/overflowed shapes instead of failing closed. PyTorch interpolate treats the result as an output size and errors when input/output spatial sizes are not greater than zero. Acceptance: validate explicit size dimensions are >0, validate scale_factor entries are finite and >0 before casting, use checked output-size conversion to reject overflow, add focused ft-api regressions for zero size plus zero/negative/NaN/+inf scale_factor, and validate via rch fmt/check/clippy plus targeted interpolate tests.
close_reason: Completed: tensor_interpolate now rejects zero input/output spatial dimensions, invalid scale_factor values, zero scale-derived outputs, and checked output-size overflow; code shipped in 472f79c with focused regressions plus rch fmt/interpolate/check/clippy validation.

title: perfect-e2e: Conv1d trains end-to-end with Adam
description: Add E2E perfect-e2e for Conv1d. Currently the perfect-e2e battery covers ConvTranspose1d (vyxi) but not Conv1d. Conv1d is a foundational op for audio / time-series / 1-D signal models. Tiny stack: Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, k=3, p=1) → MSE against zeros.
close_reason: Added conv1d_trains_end_to_end_with_adam in ft-conformance. Tiny stack: Conv1d(2 → 1, k=3, p=1) on a 1×2×4 input, MSE against zeros. Trains 200 Adam steps; expects 10x loss reduction. Locks the im2col + bmm gradient chain inside Conv1d (not previously exercised in the perfect-e2e battery). ft-conformance now at 152 base lib tests, 198 with --features fuzz.

title: Legacy packet microbench temp path omits thread id
description: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer audit found run_packet_e2e_microbench_legacy in crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs still writes to ft_conformance_packet_e2e_microbench_legacy_<packet>_<pid>.jsonl. The optimized run_packet_e2e_microbench helper immediately above includes both pid and sanitized thread id because the prior pid-only form collided when packet microbench tests ran concurrently. Legacy-vs-optimized tests can run under cargo test parallelism, so the legacy helper should mirror the pid+thread-id path construction and have a regression assertion that two concurrent legacy callers for the same packet use distinct output paths.
close_reason: Completed: legacy packet microbench now uses the shared pid+thread-id temp path builder, with a regression test proving same-packet paths differ across threads. Code landed in 39a6402.

title: [testing-fuzzing] tensor_round_decimals needs scaling-extreme fuzz coverage
description:
Finding: recent tensor_round_decimals coverage relies on targeted conformance but no fuzz target exercises decimal exponent scaling extremes, non-finite values, rank/shape variants, or output shape invariants through the public ft-api path. The implementation computes 10.0_f64.powi(decimals), which can underflow/overflow and should remain panic-free/fail-closed under adversarial inputs.

Repro steps:
1. Run rg -n "round_decimals" fuzz fuzz/Cargo.toml and observe no fuzz target exists for the op.
2. Inspect crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_round_decimals: nonzero decimals scale by 10.0_f64.powi(decimals), round, and divide by the same factor.
3. Build a libFuzzer target that drives FrankenTorchSession::tensor_round_decimals with bounded shapes, non-finite/finite values, and extreme decimal exponents; assert success preserves shape/numel and decimals=0 outputs integral-or-NaN values.
close_reason: Shipped ft_api_round_decimals fuzz target with scaling-extreme/non-finite corpus seeds. Validation: rch exec -- cargo fmt --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml -- --check; UBS on fuzz/Cargo.toml and target; rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin ft_api_round_decimals; rch exec -- cargo run --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin ft_api_round_decimals -- -runs=32 fuzz/corpus/ft_api_round_decimals (remote exit=0; local wrapper stopped during .rch-target retrieval).

title: Add metamorphic property tests: cat-narrow round-trip, softmax-positivity, sort-permutation-consistency
description:
Three more invariants:
1. fuzz_metamorphic_cat_narrow_round_trip — cat([x, y], dim=0) followed by narrow(dim=0, 0..n) recovers x bit-exactly
2. fuzz_metamorphic_softmax_positivity — every softmax output element is in [0, 1]
3. fuzz_metamorphic_sort_count_preserved — sort(x) and x have the same multiset of values

title: [mock-code-finder] tril_indices / triu_indices unnecessarily reallocate Vec via Vec::new() + per-cell branch
close_reason: Shipped: ce370bd. Both functions use Vec::with_capacity(row*col) + range-derived inner loop instead of Vec::new() + per-cell branch. Eliminates ~log2(N) realloc passes + ~50% inner-loop iterations. 4 tests pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_reshape inverse (reshape back to original)
description:
Round-trip identity: reshape(reshape(x, new_shape), original_shape) == x bit-exact for any compatible new_shape (same total numel).

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_reshape_roundtrip
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: reshape(reshape(x, flat), original_shape) == x bit-exact. Pure metadata op so the round-trip must preserve bits. Catches stride-recomputation bugs. Passes on first run.

title: [code-review] functional_conv_transpose1d/2d reject valid padded output shapes
description: [HIGH] crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:6953,7076,7096 compute functional_conv_transpose1d/2d output sizes as span - 2*padding + kernel + output_padding, and reject whenever span < 2*padding before adding kernel/output_padding. Valid PyTorch-style cases such as input length 1, kernel 3, padding 1, output_padding 0 should produce length 1, but FrankenTorch returns AutogradError. This mirrors the recently filed ft-nn ConvTranspose2d/3d arithmetic defect but affects the public functional API. Fix by computing span + kernel + output_padding with checked arithmetic, then subtracting 2*padding only if the final dimension remains positive; add focused regressions for valid padding-preserved and invalid padding-erased shapes.
close_reason: Fixed functional conv_transpose output-shape arithmetic and added padding-boundary regressions

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR suite for tensor_matmul / tensor_addmm identity + linearity invariants
description:
tensor_matmul, tensor_addmm, tensor_addmv, tensor_bmm, tensor_outer, tensor_dot all have natural metamorphic relations that don't require a torch oracle:

Identity:
- matmul(A, I) == A (where I is the identity matrix of compatible shape)
- matmul(I, A) == A
- dot(a, e_i) == a[i] (where e_i is the i-th basis vector)
- outer(a, e_i)[j, i] == a[j], all other entries 0

Linearity (scaling):
- matmul(k*A, B) == k * matmul(A, B)
- matmul(A, k*B) == k * matmul(A, B)
- dot(k*a, b) == k * dot(a, b)

Distributivity (additive):
- matmul(A, B+C) == matmul(A, B) + matmul(A, C)
- dot(a, b+c) == dot(a, b) + dot(a, c)

These MRs catch bugs that fuzz (shape/finiteness contracts) misses — they verify the underlying bilinear math is correct without needing a reference implementation.

Acceptance:
- new test module in ft-conformance (or ft-api lib tests) exercising the 4 identity MRs and at least 2 linearity MRs
- use proptest with bounded shape generators (m,n,k <= 16)
- FP tolerance: relative within 64*EPSILON*k for matmul-style ops
- closes with a list of which MRs were validated to catch which bug classes
close_reason: Shipped: 4 metamorphic relations for matmul + dot in ft-conformance (gated behind feature=fuzz). (1) matmul(A, I) == A bit-exactly both sides (right + left identity); (2) matmul(k*A, B) == k*matmul(A,B) within k*|a|max*|b|max*16eps; (3) matmul(A, B+C) == matmul(A,B) + matmul(A,C) within k*|a|max*|bc|max*32eps; (4) dot(a, e_i) == a[i] bit-exactly. All 4 pass on initial run; oracle-free MRs verify bilinear math directly.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for where_tensor_contiguous_f64 (three-operand ternary kernel)
description:
where_tensor_contiguous_f64 (ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:3118) is the kernel-level torch.where: a three-operand ternary that selects from x where condition != 0 and from y otherwise. The three operands must share shape, and the kernel does its own length checks (condition.len() >= offset+numel, y.len() >= offset+numel) before zipping into the output. None of this is fuzzed.

Unfuzzed risks: (1) shape-mismatch detection on either condition or y when storage_offset is non-zero — the offset+numel arithmetic could underflow or overflow on degenerate inputs; (2) the ensure_unary_layout_and_storage check only validates x's layout, not the others — bug in the offset slicing surfaces only at runtime; (3) zero-numel short-circuit returning early before exercising the body. The op underpins tensor_where which is the canonical autograd-aware blend (used by smooth_l1_loss, fmax, fmin, sinc, heaviside, xlogy, ...), so a kernel-level regression has wide blast radius.

Acceptance: ft_kernel_cpu_where fuzz target added under fuzz/fuzz_targets/, registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml; source numel capped at 4096; exercises the all-shapes-match path AND deliberate length-mismatch (truncated condition or y) paths; ≥2 corpus seeds.
close_reason: Shipped in 259b661 — ft_kernel_cpu_where fuzz target exercises the three-operand ternary kernel: success path with mixed condition (both branches fire), both length-validation paths (truncated condition / truncated y) asserting Err without panic, and a value-set check (output cells must be 1.0 or 2.0). Two corpus seeds. Compiles clean.

title: [code-review][reliability] tensor_glu emits opaque static error on dim-out-of-range instead of including dim and ndim
description:
tensor_glu (ft-api lib.rs:6517) validates dim against shape.len() and on failure returns DispatchKeyError::IncompatibleSet { reason: 'glu: dim out of range' }. The reason is a &'static str — it cannot embed the actual dim the caller passed or the rank of the input.

Sister ops in the same file (tensor_diag at 3567, tensor_diag_embed at 3483, tensor_dot at 2700) use ft_kernel_cpu::KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim } for the exact same failure mode. That variant is dynamic — Display includes both values, so a caller seeing the error knows immediately which dim was bad and against what rank.

Concrete repro: a user calling tensor_glu with dim=5 on a rank-3 tensor sees only 'glu: dim out of range', forcing them to print the input shape and the dim from their own code to debug. The InvalidDimension variant would emit something like 'invalid dimension 5 for tensor of rank 3'.

Acceptance:
- tensor_glu uses KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim } for the dim >= shape.len() guard
- existing glu tests still pass (the error type changes but the Result-is-Err contract is preserved)
- ft-api lib tests count unchanged
close_reason: Shipped: 2bcc7a6. tensor_glu now uses KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim } matching tensor_diag/diag_embed/dot policy. Diagnostic carries the offending dim and the input rank. Test glu_rejects_dim_out_of_range strengthened to assert both values appear in the error string.

title: Stabilize rch remote sync/artifact retrieval under volatile workspace churn
notes: Investigated repo-local rch causes. Findings: no project .rch/config.toml, no .cargo/config.toml, no legacy_pytorch_code tree in this checkout, no local .venv*, and no FrankenTorch-specific target-dir override. Global rch config already excludes target/, target_*/, target-*/, and .beads/. Remaining intermittent rsync/file-vanished noise appears daemon/artifact-sync side rather than a safe repo-local misconfiguration to edit here.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_fft + tensor_ifft round-trip identity
description:
tensor_fft and tensor_ifft form an oracle-free invertible pair: ifft(fft(x)) == x within bounded ULP. This is the strongest correctness probe possible without a numpy oracle (which 7z70 will provide separately) — any sign-convention flip in the phase or normalization mismatch breaks the round-trip.

Similar identities apply to:
- irfft(rfft(x)) == x for real input
- fft(ifft(X)) == X

Combined with the conformance oracle (7z70), this gives both bit-equivalence with the upstream reference AND self-consistency.

Acceptance:
- new MR test fuzz_metamorphic_fft_ifft_roundtrip in ft-conformance
- exercise on at least 4 shapes (varying length, varying complex/real content)
- ULP tolerance: N * 64 * EPSILON (scales with FFT depth — each butterfly stage doubles rounding)
- gated behind feature='fuzz' to match existing proptest! block
- bonus: irfft(rfft(x)) MR
close_reason: Shipped after w6x1 fix unblocked the path. Two FFT roundtrip MRs landed in ft-conformance (feature=fuzz): (1) irfft(rfft(x)) == x within N*64*EPSILON*|x|max; (2) ifft(fft(x)).real == x within same bound. The two paths use different reconstruction strategies (irfft does 2*(freq_len-1) conjugate-symmetry rebuild then inverse DFT; ifft does inverse DFT directly with 1/N at the end), so the MR pair gives independent coverage of both code paths. Both pass on first run.

title: porting: add tensor_deg2rad / tensor_rad2deg for torch parity
description: torch.deg2rad and torch.rad2deg convert between degrees and radians. Trivial scalar multiplications (* pi/180 and * 180/pi respectively). Useful for porting numpy/torch code that handles angles. Composes through tensor_mul against a scalar tensor; autograd-correct.
close_reason: Added tensor_deg2rad / tensor_rad2deg ops in ft-api as element-wise scalar multiplications by π/180 and 180/π respectively. Composes through autograd-aware tensor_mul; gradient is the constant scalar factor. 4 unit tests cover known angle conversions (0/90/180/360°), round-trip identity, and analytic gradient regression. ft-api now at 1254 lib tests.

title: Implement RMSNorm in ft-nn
description: Add RMSNorm normalization layer. Critical for modern LLM architectures (LLaMA, Mistral, DeepSeek). Simpler than LayerNorm: normalize by RMS without centering. Formula: x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + eps) * weight
close_reason: Implemented RMSNorm with batched forward, weight parameter, shape validation. 4 tests passing.

title: Migrate torch subprocess conformance tests onto bounded oracle runner
close_reason: Completed: migrated avg_pool2d and ldexp torch subprocess conformance tests onto the bounded ft-conformance oracle runner; code shipped in 36c9e52.

title: porting: add tensor_atleast_{1d,2d,3d} for torch.atleast_*d parity
description: torch.atleast_1d/2d/3d ensure a tensor has at least the specified rank by left-padding (and right-padding for 3d) size-1 dimensions. Useful when porting numpy/torch code that uses these for shape coercion. Compose through autograd-aware tensor_reshape so gradients flow unchanged. Add the single-tensor variant (PyTorch also accepts variadic inputs but the single-tensor form is the primary interface).
close_reason: Added tensor_atleast_1d / 2d / 3d ops in ft-api with PyTorch-matching padding semantics — 1d: scalar→[1]; 2d: scalar→[1,1], 1-D[N]→[1,N] (left-pad); 3d: scalar→[1,1,1], 1-D[N]→[1,N,1], 2-D[N,M]→[N,M,1] (asymmetric pad matching torch/numpy). Composes through autograd-aware tensor_reshape, returning the input id unchanged for higher-rank inputs (no extra graph nodes). Added 4 unit tests covering shape promotion, no-op for higher rank, asymmetric 3d padding, and gradient propagation. ft-api now at 1215 lib tests (up from 1211).

title: [code-review][reliability] migrate six rank-restriction diagnostics to KernelError::InvalidDimension
description:
Companion to oagi, 6zyy, c991, qaux. Six more functions emit static-reason 'must be N-D' diagnostics dropping the actual input rank:

  pdist                lib.rs:4532 (rejects non-2D)
  tensor_mv matrix arg lib.rs:4741 (rejects non-2D)
  tensor_mv vector arg lib.rs:4748 (rejects non-1D)
  repeat_interleave    lib.rs:13420 (rejects non-1D)
  matrix_norm          lib.rs:14500 (rejects non-2D)
  kthvalue             lib.rs:15141 (rejects non-1D)
  lstsq                lib.rs:15266 (rejects non-2D A)

All migrate to KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim: actual_rank, ndim: expected_rank } per the established codebase convention.

Acceptance:
- all 7 sites migrated
- existing tests still pass
- ft-api lib tests count unchanged
close_reason: Shipped: 9853408. 7 sites migrated to KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim }. 24 affected tests pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_quantile: q=0 is min, q=1 is max, monotone in q, NaN-input propagates
description: tensor_quantile contracts: quantile(x, 0) = min(x), quantile(x, 1) = max(x), monotonically non-decreasing in q, NaN input propagates NaN. No MR currently exercises these.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_quantile_contract: q=0 is min, q=1 is max, non-decreasing across 0.0..1.0 step 0.1, NaN propagates. Lib test passes; ft-conformance now 359 tests in feature=fuzz lib.

title: Replace placeholder perf-SLO baseline with higher-iteration bundle
description:
Follow-up to frankentorch-3cp.

The canonical baseline path for G5 is now artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_baseline_v1.json, but it is intentionally a single-sample full-profile anchor captured on 2026-04-08 UTC.

Remaining work:
1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with a stronger statistical envelope, targeting the intended 100 iterations plus warmup.
2. Preserve the generated artifact bundle directly instead of reconstructing it from stdout.
3. Replace the placeholder baseline and baseline summary with the stronger bundle, or version it explicitly if both need to coexist.
4. Re-assess regression-budget stability after the stronger baseline is committed.

This is quality-improvement work, not a prerequisite for the newly normalized baseline path contract itself.
close_reason: Completed

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_atan2 vs tensor_atan ratio identity for positive x
description:
atan2(y, x) == atan(y/x) for x > 0 within ULP. Catches atan2 vs atan ratio drift in the principal-branch case.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_atan2_equals_atan_of_ratio_pos_x
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: atan2(y, x) ≈ atan(y/x) within 16 ULP for x > 0. Catches atan2 vs atan principal-branch drift. Passes on first run.

title: tensor_interpolate severs autograd via tensor storage extraction + non-grad rebuild
description:
tensor_interpolate in ft-api (line 14674) extracts the input tensor's storage upfront, dispatches to one of nearest/linear/bilinear/bicubic/trilinear interpolation methods that all build output values in plain f64 and rebuild via tensor_variable(_, _, false). Silently severs autograd through every F.interpolate call — used in U-Net decoders, FPN, segmentation, super-resolution, etc.

For nearest interpolation, the autograd-aware path is straightforward: build a flat index_select index and use tensor_index_select. For linear/bilinear/trilinear/bicubic, the math is a weighted sum of nearby pixels, which can compose through tensor_index_select + tensor_mul + tensor_add but is more complex.

Interim fix: add a fail-loud guard on requires_grad input so silent severance becomes loud. Proper backward implementation tracked as a follow-up.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_interpolate (line 14674).

title: [modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis] logsumexp + logcumsumexp + movedim dimension-out-of-range rejections mask actual dim/ndim
close_reason: Shipped — logsumexp + logcumsumexp + movedim dim-out-of-range now surface KernelError::InvalidDimension {dim, ndim}. 13 lib tests pass.

title: perfect-e2e: Conv2d + AdaptiveAvgPool2d + Linear classifier training test
description: Add E2E perfect-e2e for the canonical CNN classification head pattern: Conv2d → AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1,1)) (global average pooling) → Flatten → Linear → cross_entropy. This is the standard ResNet/EfficientNet-style classifier and is currently missing from the perfect-e2e battery. Locks gradient flow through AdaptiveAvgPool2d, which our current E2E suite doesn't exercise.

Trains 200 Adam steps on 2 distinct constant-pattern samples (all-1 vs all-(-1)) with binary labels; expects 10x loss reduction. Locks gradient flow through AdaptiveAvgPool2d which the perfect-e2e battery did not previously exercise. ft-conformance now at 151 base lib tests, 197 with --features fuzz.

title: [modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis] grid_sample + searchsorted batch-mismatch static reasons mask actual shapes

Two more static-reason rejections that have actual shape info available:

  - searchsorted (14599): 'searchsorted: batch dimensions must match' — has both sorted_seq and v shapes

Migrate to KernelError::ShapeMismatch {lhs, rhs} carrying both full shapes (matches the cdist zf6b / view_as_complex 8c9m pattern).

Acceptance: both static reasons replaced with ShapeMismatch; existing tests pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] AlphaDropout must reject invalid probabilities
description: Metamorphic audit found ft-nn::AlphaDropout lacks the finite [0,1] probability validation already present in Dropout before it samples the training mask and computes SELU-preserving affine coefficients. Invalid p values can produce nonsensical masks or non-finite affine constants instead of fail-closed module behavior. Add the same finite inclusive-range guard, cover NaN, negative, and >1 probabilities, and keep p=0 eval/identity and valid training behavior unchanged with gradient-flow regression coverage.
close_reason: Shipped in a782858 — AlphaDropout's forward() now validates p with the same finite-and-in-[0,1] guard as Dropout; fires before training/eval short-circuit so eval-mode also rejects invalid p. 3 new tests cover invalid-p (NaN/-0.1/1.1/±inf), boundary p (0 and 1 succeed), and eval-mode + invalid p. All 6 alpha_dropout lib tests pass.

title: [deadlock-finder-and-fixer] run_packet_e2e_microbench races on shared temp file when same packet_id runs in parallel test threads
description:
run_packet_e2e_microbench (ft-conformance/src/lib.rs:4574) builds the per-iteration scratch path as:

  ft_conformance_packet_e2e_microbench_{packet_id_lowercase}_{pid}.jsonl

The path includes pid (good — uniqueness across processes) but NOT thread::id, so two cargo-test threads running tests with the same packet_id collide on the same file. There are at least two such collisions:

  - packet_e2e_microbench_cpu_kernel_produces_percentiles (14294)
    AND a sister test at 14353 both use 'FT-P2C-005'
  - 14322 and 14376 both use 'FT-P2C-008'

When cargo test runs these in parallel, both threads invoke emit_e2e_forensics_matrix_filtered against the same path. The function opens the file, writes JSONL, closes — non-atomic. Concurrent writes interleave, producing corrupt JSONL that subsequent passes might attempt to parse, leading to false test failures or — worse — silent data corruption masked by both threads producing structurally-similar output.

Fix: include thread::current().id() (or an AtomicUsize counter) in the path so concurrent threads with the same packet_id write to distinct files. Acceptance: shared-temp-file race eliminated; existing packet_e2e_microbench tests still pass; the same-packet pair (FT-P2C-005, FT-P2C-008) tests can run in parallel without interfering.
close_reason: Shipped in 567c8e1 — packet microbench scratch path now includes thread::current().id() (sanitized) in addition to pid + packet_id, eliminating the shared-temp-file race when same-packet_id tests run in parallel under cargo test. All 10 packet_e2e_microbench tests pass including the FT-P2C-005 and FT-P2C-008 same-packet pairs.

title: Implement tril, triu, tril_indices, triu_indices
description: Add triangular matrix operations: tril (lower triangle), triu (upper triangle), tril_indices, triu_indices. Critical for attention masks in transformers.
close_reason: tril and triu already existed. Added tril_indices and triu_indices with offset support. 4 tests passing.

title: Add gradient checkpointing (torch.utils.checkpoint equivalent)
description:
torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() allows trading compute for memory by not saving intermediate activations during forward, then recomputing them during backward. This is essential for training large models that don't fit in memory.

Implementation plan:
1. Add a session.checkpoint(function, inputs) method that:
   a. Runs the function in no_grad context (saves no intermediates)
   b. Records a special CheckpointedSegment node on the autograd tape
   c. During backward: re-runs the function WITH grad enabled to recompute activations, then computes gradients normally
2. The key invariant: gradients must be identical whether checkpoint is used or not (just slower with lower memory)

Design considerations:
- Must work with the existing TensorTape backward scheduler
- Must properly handle nested checkpointing
- Must respect the DAC contract (deterministic replay must still work)
- Re-execution during backward must produce identical activations (deterministic PRNG state needs saving)

Tests:
- Unit test: simple function with checkpoint vs without — gradients must match exactly
- Unit test: nested checkpointing (checkpoint inside checkpoint)
- Unit test: checkpoint respects manual_seed for reproducibility
- E2E test: train a deep network with and without checkpointing — final loss should be identical
- Memory test: verify peak memory is lower with checkpointing (if measurable)
close_reason: Implemented session.checkpoint() that trades compute for memory. Runs function in no_grad (no intermediates saved), registers backward node via tensor_apply_function that re-executes the function with grad enabled during backward. 3 tests: correct forward output, gradient equivalence with non-checkpointed, multiple inputs.

title: [deadlock-finder] read_stream_capped busy-loops after overflow flag is set
description:
ft-conformance lib.rs:9270 read_stream_capped sets overflow_flag.store(true) at line 9295 but does NOT break the read loop. After overflow is detected, the function keeps pulling bytes from the child's stdout/stderr until EOF, which only happens once the main thread sees the overflow flag and calls terminate_and_reap_child(child).

Effect: the reader thread sits in reader.read() consuming and discarding bytes from a child we're already going to kill. The cost is bounded (the read loop exits once the child's stdout closes after kill) but:
  1. Wastes CPU spinning on bytes we never use (everything past the cap is dropped — see line 9289 which guards the buffer copy, but read still pulls 8 KiB chunks).
  2. Delays stdout_reader.join() / stderr_reader.join() until child is fully reaped, even though the reader has all the data it can usefully capture.
  3. Holds the Arc<AtomicBool> reader_overflow clone alive longer than necessary.

A trivial fix — break after setting overflow_flag — releases the reader immediately. The capture's total_bytes ends at the chunk that crossed the threshold instead of the full stream length, which still satisfies validate_legacy_oracle_stream_bounds (it only checks > max).

Both existing tests pass with the fix:
  read_stream_capped_buffers_all_bytes_within_cap: 11-byte input, no overflow path triggered.
  read_stream_capped_truncates_buffer_and_marks_overflow: 96-byte input fits in one 8 KiB chunk, so overflow is set on the first iteration; total_bytes = 96 (unchanged), break exits cleanly.

Acceptance:
- read_stream_capped breaks the loop after setting overflow_flag
- both existing tests pass unchanged
- ft-conformance compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: a59a14d. read_stream_capped now breaks the loop on overflow, releasing the reader thread immediately. Validation logic unchanged — overflow_flag is still set + main thread still kills child + total_bytes still > max_bytes. Both existing tests pass.

title: MaxUnpool 2D/3D should check output shape overflow before allocation
close_reason: Shipped checked MaxUnpool2d/3d pooled and output shape arithmetic before allocation.

title: [parity-gap] torch.round(input, decimals=N) missing in ft-api
description:
torch.round has a 'decimals' kwarg that rounds to N decimal places (or to the 10^|N|-place when N is negative). FrankenTorch's tensor_round only supports the default decimals=0 case. Code ported from torch using torch.round(x, decimals=2) — common in metrics reporting, quantization-aware training, and display formatting — fails to compile.

Implementation: round(x, n) = round(x * 10^n) / 10^n. Composes through autograd-aware tensor_mul + tensor_round + tensor_div. tensor_round already uses a straight-through estimator on backward, so gradient flows correctly through the scale-round-unscale composition.

Acceptance:
- tensor_round_decimals(input, decimals: i32) present on FrankenTorchSession
- decimals=0 path matches tensor_round (consistency)
- positive decimals (round to N places after decimal point)
- negative decimals (round to 10^|N|, e.g. decimals=-2 rounds to nearest 100)
- regression tests covering positive, zero, negative, gradient flow
- ft-api lib tests +N where N >= 4
close_reason: Shipped: 0cfc995. tensor_round_decimals composes round(x*10^N)/10^N with a single shared factor tensor. Backward is 0 (matches torch.round non-differentiability). 4 tests cover positive/zero/negative/gradient.

description: mock-code-finder discovery: ft-nn PoissonNLLLoss::forward reimplements the loss and, when log_input=false, computes log(input) directly. The ft-api functional path documents and implements log(input + eps); the module can return NaN for zero input/zero target where the functional path is finite. Acceptance: make the module delegate to or match the functional eps-stabilized implementation, add a focused ft-nn regression for log_input=false at zero input/zero target, preserve existing log_input=true behavior, and validate via scoped rch.

title: Bilinear should reject scalar x1 before slicing batch shape
description: mock-code-finder discovery: ft-nn Bilinear::forward_bilinear computes &x1_shape[..x1_shape.len() - 1] when x1 is not rank-1. A scalar x1 has rank 0, so this underflows and panics before returning an AutogradError. Acceptance: validate both Bilinear inputs are at least 1-D before batch-shape slicing, preserve existing feature-dimension checks and valid forward behavior, add a focused scalar-input regression, and validate with scoped rch.
close_reason: Shipped scalar-input rank guard for Bilinear.

title: tensor_linalg_det / slogdet return raw f64 instead of TensorNodeId — breaks autograd
description:
tensor_linalg_det at lib.rs:12132 returns Result<f64, AutogradError> and tensor_linalg_slogdet at lib.rs:12146 returns Result<(f64, f64), AutogradError>. PyTorch's torch.linalg.det / torch.linalg.slogdet return 0-D tensors that participate in the autograd graph.

The current API: callers extract raw f64 → can't differentiate, can't compose with downstream tensor ops, can't carry through training loops.

Fix is a breaking API change but justified for parity:
  - tensor_linalg_det → Result<TensorNodeId, AutogradError>
    backward: grad_A = grad_out * det(A) * (A^{-1})^T = grad_out * Y * (A^{-1})^T
    Save A^{-1} (or A and recompute) for backward.
  - tensor_linalg_slogdet → Result<(TensorNodeId, TensorNodeId), AutogradError>
    sign is non-differentiable (constant in {-1, 0, 1}); logabsdet has
    backward grad_A = grad_logabsdet * (A^{-1})^T (Jacobi formula).

Migration path: add new tensor_linalg_det_node / tensor_linalg_slogdet_node returning TensorNodeId, deprecate the f64-returning forms with a documented removal target. Or change directly if the breaking change is acceptable.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass following the linalg severed-autograd cleanup (frankentorch-tw0y children: linalg_inv, triangular_solve, cholesky_solve).
close_reason: Already shipped 2026-05-01 in commit 46ab79c (autograd-aware tensor_linalg_det / tensor_linalg_slogdet). FD gradient checks in 26ch and veo5. JSONL drift from another agent's br operations through the corrupted DB keeps reverting the close-state. Re-closing in raw JSONL — DO NOT run br operations on this file before commit.

title: Add E2E training test for ReflectionPad2d → Conv2d image regression
description:
Add an end-to-end training test that exercises the recently fixed ReflectionPad2d (frankentorch-kae4) wired up before a Conv2d layer in a typical image-processing pipeline. This validates that gradient flow through the padding module doesn't break when stacked with downstream learnable layers and an optimizer.

Setup:
- Small image batch [1, 1, 4, 4]
- ReflectionPad2d((1, 1, 1, 1)) → [1, 1, 6, 6]
- Conv2d(in=1, out=1, kernel=3) → [1, 1, 4, 4]
- MSE against a fixed target image
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

Catches regressions in the padding module's autograd flow that unit-level gradient-mass tests would miss when chained with Conv2d and Adam.

title: [code-review][reliability] migrate four rank-restriction diagnostics in pinv / cond / matrix_rank
description:
Companion to oagi, 6zyy, c991, qaux, oitu. Four more rank-restriction sites still emit static-reason 'must be 2-D' diagnostics:

  pinv (eager check)        lib.rs:15093
  pinv (apply_function)     lib.rs:15116
  cond                      lib.rs:15403
  matrix_rank               lib.rs:15481

All migrate to KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim: actual_rank, ndim: expected_rank } per the established convention.

Acceptance:

close_reason: Shipped: 7554ff4. pinv (2 sites) / cond / matrix_rank all migrated to KernelError::InvalidDimension. 15 affected tests pass.

title: tensor_addr severs autograd; composable through outer + scalar combine
description:
tensor_addr at lib.rs:15783 computes rank-1 update out = beta * input + alpha * (vec1 ⊗ vec2). The body extracts values, computes the outer product with scalars in plain f64, and rebuilds a non-grad leaf. PyTorch's torch.addr IS differentiable.

Composable through autograd primitives:
   outer  = tensor_outer(vec1, vec2)              # [m, n], autograd-aware
   alpha_outer = full(shape, alpha) * outer
   beta_input  = full(shape, beta) * input
   out         = beta_input + alpha_outer

All ops autograd-aware so gradients flow to all three inputs.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_addr through tensor_outer + tensor_mul + tensor_add (all autograd-aware). Same observable arithmetic as before but the autograd tape edges are intact for input / vec1 / vec2. Added addr_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_vecs regression test (input=ones(2,2), v1=[1,2], v2=[3,4], beta=alpha=1 → grad_input=ones, grad_v1=[7,7], grad_v2=[3,3]). ft-api lib tests: 1171.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_kthvalue: k=1 is min, k=n is max, monotone in k
description: tensor_kthvalue returns the k-th smallest value of a 1-D input. The contract: kthvalue(x, 1) == min(x), kthvalue(x, n) == max(x), and kthvalue values are non-decreasing in k. No MR currently exercises this. Add fuzz_metamorphic_kthvalue_contract under feature=fuzz.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_kthvalue_contract: bit-exact total_cmp parity for every k, k=1 is min, k=n is max, non-decreasing in k. Lib test passes.

title: [porting-to-rust] missing tensor_bartlett_window (torch.bartlett_window parity, triangular window)
description:
Last of the 4 standard signal-processing window functions still missing from ft-api. Bartlett (triangular) window:

  w[n] = 1 - |2n/denom - 1|

where denom = window_length (periodic) or window_length - 1 (symmetric). Linear triangular shape — used in spectrum analysis where simple side-lobe behavior is desired.

Acceptance: bartlett_window method matching the hann_window/hamming_window/blackman_window signature; ≥3 lib tests covering periodic, non-periodic, length-1.
close_reason: Shipped — bartlett_window with linear triangular formula. Completes the 4-window family (hann/hamming/blackman/bartlett) for signal-processing parity. 4 lib tests pass.

title: Enable prod autograd for tensor_scatter_reduce
description: Discovery pass: tensor_scatter_reduce routes sum/mean/amax/amin through autograd-aware paths, but reduce='prod' still fails loud on requires_grad input/src even though the forward path exists. Implement product-rule backward for include_self=True scatter_reduce prod: gradients flow to input and src factors as product of all other factors at each output position, with explicit zero-count handling for one-zero vs multi-zero products. Add regressions covering input/src gradients and a zero factor case.
close_reason: Completed in f1172ab: scatter_reduce reduce='prod' now supports autograd for matching f32/f64 input/src via product-rule backward with explicit zero-factor handling; rch ft-api scatter_reduce tests, fmt, check, and clippy passed.

title: [profiling-software-performance] batch_norm1d / batch_norm2d allocate momentum + one_minus tensors twice per training step
description:
functional_batch_norm1d (around lib.rs:8200) and functional_batch_norm2d (lib.rs:8308) compute the running_mean update and the running_var update in two separate code branches. Each branch independently allocates 'one_minus = full([C], 1.0 - momentum)' and 'momentum_t = full([C], momentum)' when its corresponding running stat is provided.

Typical training: both running_mean AND running_var are tracked, so the function makes 4 full() allocations of just two distinct values — one_minus and momentum_t are duplicated across branches.

Same pattern as cc2o (heaviside/pow), d521 (sinc), 4w7g (atanh/focal_loss), x8r1 (entr), 10mv (softshrink), rbhb (xlogy/xlog1py): tensor_mul / tensor_add only READ their constant operands, so a single allocation per value is reusable across consumers.

Net per-call savings (for training-step typical case where both running stats are tracked):
  - functional_batch_norm1d: -2 full() + -2 tape nodes
  - functional_batch_norm2d: -2 full() + -2 tape nodes
  Total: -4 full() + -4 tape nodes per training step

batch_norm is one of the hottest layers in CNN training; the wasted work compounds proportionally to model depth.

Acceptance:
- both batch_norm1d and batch_norm2d hoist a single one_minus and a single momentum_t before the running_mean / running_var branches
- existing batch_norm tests still pass — no behavior change
close_reason: Shipped: 46bf090. Both batch_norm1d and batch_norm2d hoist a single (one_minus, momentum_t) Option allocated lazily once at the top of the training branch and reused by both running_mean and running_var update paths. -4 full() + -4 tape nodes per training step. 3 tests pass.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] torch_angle returns π for -0 input but oracle expects 0 (platform libm atan2 divergence)
description:
tensor_angle is implemented as atan2(0, x). For input=-0, libm atan2(0, -0) = π (IEEE 754 convention). The oracle expects 0 (current torch.angle on the test machine). The test currently fails.

Two valid resolutions:
1. Add a special-case to tensor_angle for -0 → return 0 (matches torch but diverges from atan2 contract)
2. Update the test to accept either π or 0 for -0 input (acknowledging platform variation)

For now, this is a known torch parity gap not blocking any user-facing functionality. tensor_angle on -0 input is exotic enough that real models won't hit it. Document as P4.
close_reason: Fixed: tensor_angle reformulated from atan2(0, x) to where(isnan(x), NaN, where(x < 0, π, 0)). The previous impl gave π for x=-0 (per IEEE 754 atan2(+0, -0) = π), but torch treats -0 == 0 → non-negative branch → 0. New impl also correctly handles NaN propagation. torch_angle_subprocess_conformance now PASSES. ft-api 1519/1519 still pass, all 81 MRs still pass. Bonus: this is the 2nd of the 7 vgj2 failures fixed (after the pow(-0) fix in vgj2 itself).

title: [deadlock-finder-and-fixer] sidecar cache _uncached path still writes to shared cache (skip-read-only honored)
description:
serialization_generate_sidecar_with_retry_inner (ft-conformance/src/lib.rs:10277) takes a use_cache: bool flag but only honors it for READS:

  - L10283 'if use_cache' gates the cache lookup (correct)
  - L10316-10319 inserts the computed result UNCONDITIONALLY,
    even when use_cache=false

This means serialization_generate_sidecar_with_retry_uncached (which passes use_cache=false expecting 'skip cache entirely') still grows the global SERIALIZATION_SIDECAR_CACHE, producing two real problems:

  1. Contract break: callers asking for 'no cache' nevertheless
     pollute the shared map. The map can grow unboundedly when
     _uncached is called repeatedly with distinct payloads,
     defeating the purpose of opting out.

  2. Concurrency race: when an _uncached caller writes
     concurrently with a _cached caller (or _cached caller's
     write after compute), the two threads race on the same
     mutex. Both insert. Whichever loses the race overwrites
     the other — benign for deterministic encodings, but
     observable as cache thrash and increased lock contention.
     The fix narrows the lock window for _uncached callers
     (they shouldn't be touching the cache mutex at all) and
     removes a class of cross-mode interference.

Fix: gate the insert on use_cache too. Use entry().or_insert() in the cached branch to avoid clobbering a value another thread inserted between our read miss and our write.

Acceptance: both the read and the write are gated on use_cache; the _uncached path no longer takes the cache mutex on hit; the _cached path uses entry().or_insert(...) so concurrent inserters don't clobber each other; existing tests still pass.
close_reason: Shipped in 1cb4116 — _uncached callers no longer take the cache mutex / pollute the global map; cached-branch insert switched to entry().or_insert_with() to avoid clobbering concurrent writers. All 6 ft-conformance serialization tests pass.

title: [code-review][reliability] migrate four rank-mismatch diagnostics to KernelError::InvalidDimension (triu / tril / vander / combinations)
description:
Companion to oagi (tensor_glu), 6zyy (4 dim-bound errors), and c991 (5 shape-mismatch errors). Four more functions emit static-reason 'expects N-D input' / 'must be 1-D' diagnostics that drop the actual input rank:

  triu          lib.rs:1715 (rejects non-2D)

  vander        lib.rs:4066 (rejects non-1D)

The variant name is mildly misnamed for rank vs reduction-dim, but it's the established convention and Display prints both values.

Acceptance:
- all four functions migrated to KernelError::InvalidDimension
- diagnostics carry both the actual rank and the expected rank
- existing tests still pass
