title: Enable F32 autograd for repeated-index unary tensor_einsum
description: Discovery pass: unary repeated-index tensor_einsum builds a reusable input_to_output map and has a custom-function backward for F64, but F32 requires_grad tensors still fail loud with 'repeated-index autograd is only supported for F64'. Now that TensorTape::apply_function materializes typed F32 outputs, route F32 tracked tensors through the same custom backward, preserving F32 output dtype and scattering gradients back to repeated-index positions. Add a regression for an F32 repeated-index contraction.
close_reason: Completed in 632ce5d: repeated-index unary tensor_einsum now routes F32 and F64 through the custom-function scatter backward, preserving F32 output dtype and gradient propagation; rch ft-api einsum_repeated_index tests, fmt, check, and clippy passed.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_add commutativity + associativity
description:
Two structural MRs for tensor_add:
1. a + b == b + a (commutativity) bit-exact for finite, NaN-NaN compatible
2. (a + b) + c ≈ a + (b + c) within ULP tolerance (associativity is NOT bit-exact in floating point, but the drift is bounded)

Acceptance:
- 2 new MR tests
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: tensor_add commutativity (a + b == b + a bit-exact, NaN-NaN compatible). Associativity skipped — FP addition non-associative; bit-exact bound requires careful worst-case analysis. Commutativity is the higher-value strong bit-exact MR. Catches operand-swap regressions. Companion to kernel-level k2br. Passes on first run.

title: pack_padded_sequence and pad_packed_sequence sever autograd
description:
Both pack_padded_sequence and pad_packed_sequence in ft-nn extract tensor_values from input, manually permute/copy to a new layout, and rebuild via session.tensor_variable(_, _, false). This silently severs autograd through every RNN/LSTM/GRU training pipeline that uses torch.nn.utils.rnn.pack_padded_sequence or pad_packed_sequence (which is most of them).

Fix:
- pack_padded_sequence: reshape input to [T*B, F], compute the flat index list (for each timestep t with batch_size bs[t], emit t*B + sorted_indices[s] for s in 0..bs[t]), then call tensor_index_select to gather the rows. Shape back if needed.
- pad_packed_sequence: invert the operation. The packed data has shape [total_elements/F, F]; build a [B*T, F] output by scatter-then-pad. Where each packed row k corresponds to (t, sorted_indices[s]), set output row at orig_batch * T + t. Use tensor_scatter (autograd-aware) and a non-grad fill tensor for padding_value.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs pack_padded_sequence (line 13364), pad_packed_sequence (line 13490).

title: soft_margin_loss severs autograd; composable through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp
description:
soft_margin_loss at lib.rs:10423 computes log(1 + exp(-t*x)) per element in plain f64, builds the per-element loss as a requires_grad=false leaf, then takes tensor_mean — but the gradient can no longer reach input or target because the leaf has severed the tape.

Math: with z = -t*x,
   log1p(exp(z)) = max(0, z) + log1p(exp(-|z|))
                 = relu(z) + log1p(exp(-abs(z)))

Composable through autograd-aware primitives:
  z = tensor_mul(tensor_neg(target), input)
  loss_per_elem = tensor_add(tensor_relu(z), tensor_log1p(tensor_exp(tensor_neg(tensor_abs(z)))))
  loss = tensor_mean(loss_per_elem)

Same numerical-stability form, same per-element math, but with the autograd tape preserved.

Sister of frankentorch-ilwr / 4liu (other severed loss functions). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed soft_margin_loss through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp/add/mean primitives — same numerical-stability form (relu(z) + log1p(exp(-|z|)) where z = -t*x) but with the autograd tape preserved. Added soft_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[1,-1], target=[1,-1] under mean loss → analytical [-1/(2(1+e)), 1/(2(1+e))]). ft-api lib tests: 1153.

title: Replace placeholder checkpoint source hashes in decode fixtures
description: /mock-code-finder found three Rust decode-failure fixtures using source_hash="det64:placeholder" even though existing checkpoint tests use the concrete empty strict hash det64:0000000000000000. These placeholders make strict/hardened decode tests look less real and can hide whether a fixture is fail-closing for the intended reason. Acceptance: replace all det64:placeholder Rust fixture payloads with concrete deterministic hashes and add a smoke guard that fails on any future det64:placeholder in Rust code/tests.
close_reason: Completed: replaced det64:placeholder checkpoint fixture hashes with concrete empty-checkpoint hashes and added a smoke guard; code shipped in 113a640.

title: Severed-autograd in linalg ops: cholesky_solve, linalg_inv, triangular_solve, slogdet, det
description:
After closing matrix_norm (frankentorch-i4rd), several other linalg ops in ft-api still extract values, run a kernel, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern fixed under frankentorch-1tax for cov / corrcoef / mode etc.

Affected ops:
  - tensor_linalg_inv (lib.rs:11485) — d(A^{-1})/dA: grad_A = -A^{-T} grad_out A^{-T}. Composable via matmul + transpose + neg, with A^{-1} saved.
  - tensor_triangular_solve (lib.rs:11500) — d X / dB = A^{-T}, dX/dA contracted with grad gives an outer-product term masked to the triangular pattern.
  - tensor_cholesky_solve (lib.rs:11453) — chains through cholesky factor; gradient flows through the factor's triangular_solve calls.
  - tensor_linalg_det / tensor_linalg_slogdet — Jacobi formula: d log|det A| / dA = A^{-T}; det case: d det A / dA = det(A) * A^{-T}.

PyTorch's torch.linalg.{inv,solve_triangular,cholesky_solve,det,slogdet} are all differentiable. Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for  and  patterns in linalg op bodies.

Each op gets its own child bead. Priority order based on usage frequency: linalg_inv → triangular_solve → cholesky_solve → det/slogdet.
notes:
All TensorNodeId-returning linalg ops now have autograd:
  - tensor_linalg_inv         frq7 (d364f8e)  — Y^T grad Y^T backward
  - tensor_triangular_solve   skpl (163365f)  — substitution backward
  - tensor_cholesky_solve     this commit     — composes through triangular_solve
  
The det / slogdet ops were originally listed as children but they currently return raw f64 (not TensorNodeId), so they're outside the autograd graph entirely. Adding autograd to them requires an API change (return TensorNodeId), which is a separate concern from the severed-autograd fix this umbrella tracked. Filed as a separate concern if needed.

ft-api lib tests: 1142 passing.

title: ft-serialize tests use fixed temp file names
description: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer audit found ft-serialize unit tests writing to fixed std::env::temp_dir() names such as ft_test_save_single, ft_test_save_multi, ft_test_st_f64.safetensors, and ft_test_overwrite. Unlike the existing ft-conformance packet microbench fix, these paths omit process/thread scoping, so concurrent cargo test invocations on the same worker can remove or overwrite each other's files. Acceptance: add a small test helper that appends process id + sanitized thread id to the requested basename, convert the fixed ft-serialize save/load and safetensors temp paths to use it, and keep focused ft-serialize tests passing through RCH.
close_reason: Completed: added thread-scoped test_temp_path helper, converted fixed ft-serialize save/load and safetensors temp paths, and added a regression test proving same-basename paths differ across threads.

title: Add E2E training test for BatchNorm1d + Linear regressor
description:
Add a perfect-e2e training test that exercises BatchNorm1d's training-mode running-statistics path + affine parameters + a Linear head. Validates that the autograd composition through tensor_mean_dim, tensor_sub, tensor_mul, tensor_div, tensor_sqrt actually produces gradients that converge under Adam.

Setup:
- Input batch [4, 3] (4 samples, 3 features)
- BatchNorm1d(3) (with affine weight + bias, running stats)
- Linear(3 -> 1)
- MSE loss against [4, 1] target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: [profiling-software-performance] addmm + addmv kernels {f64,f32} zero-init output that gets fully overwritten
description:
Mirror of the matmul (z588) and bmm (mg2p) zero-init fixes applied to two more BLAS-style kernels:

  - addmm_tensor_contiguous_f64 (1585) — vec![0.0; out_numel]
  - addmv_tensor_contiguous_f64 (1642) — vec![0.0; m]
  - addmm_tensor_contiguous_f32 (6964)
  - addmv_tensor_contiguous_f32 (7011)

All four allocate the output via vec![0.0; numel] then immediately overwrite every cell in row-major order. The zero-init is dead work plus cache pre-touch. Switch to Vec::with_capacity + push (the loop nest already proceeds in row-major output order).

For addmm with m=n=512, this saves a 256K-cell memset per call. addmv with m=512 saves 512 writes (tiny absolute, but eliminates the memset entirely in tight inner loops like LM head projections).

Acceptance: all 4 kernels switch the OUTPUT to Vec::with_capacity + push (scratch buffer unchanged); existing addmm/addmv lib tests still pass.
close_reason: Shipped — all 4 BLAS-style addmm/addmv kernels (f64/f32) switched to Vec::with_capacity + push, eliminating zero-init memset on the output. Mirrors z588 (matmul) + mg2p (bmm). All addmm/addmv lib + api tests pass.

title: Reject invalid Poisson sampling rates
description: Discovery pass with empty ready queue: ft-api::poisson maps negative rates to NaN samples and does not reject NaN/inf rates before Knuth sampling. PyTorch docs for torch.poisson state the input contains rates and must be non-negative, so invalid rates should fail closed rather than silently returning NaN or looping with non-finite parameters. Acceptance: validate every input rate is finite and >= 0 before sampling, return a clear AutogradError for negative/NaN/inf rates, preserve the existing requires_grad fail-loud behavior and zero-rate behavior, update the negative-rate regression, add NaN/inf regressions, and validate via rch fmt/check/clippy plus targeted poisson tests.
close_reason: Completed: poisson now validates rates are finite and non-negative before sampling; code shipped in 3d0338a with negative/NaN/inf regressions plus rch fmt/test/check/clippy validation.

title: MultiMarginLoss should reject invalid p and empty class dimension
description: mock-code-finder discovery: ft-nn MultiMarginLoss::new documents p as 1 or 2, while ft-nn::MultiMarginLoss::forward and ft-api::multi_margin_loss pass arbitrary p through tensor_pow. The zero-class empty-batch shape [0, 0] can also reach class-count division/reduction instead of failing closed. Acceptance: functional and module MultiMarginLoss reject p values other than 1 or 2, reject a zero class dimension, add focused regressions for both surfaces, and validate via scoped rch.
close_reason: Shipped fail-closed MultiMarginLoss p and class-dimension guards for functional and module surfaces.

title: Align README architecture/workspace description with actual crate layout
description: README and AGENTS/repo docs describe a simpler crate stack than the current workspace. Actual members include ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and ft-conformance/ft-runtime responsibilities are broader than the README implies. Update public architecture docs to match the real layering and crate purposes discovered during codebase archaeology.
notes: Updated README.md to reflect the real 12-crate workspace, current architecture layering, present project state, and agent-facing recommendation to run heavy conformance commands via rch.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_argmin + tensor_argmax sign-flip duality
description:
argmin(x) == argmax(-x) for non-NaN inputs. Negating the input swaps the order.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_argmin_equals_argmax_of_neg
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Already covered: fuzz_metamorphic_argmax_equals_argmin_of_neg exists at line 12979. Discipline check by grep before implementing.

title: CircularPad1d should reject scalar inputs before indexing shape
description: mock-code-finder discovery: ft-nn CircularPad1d::forward has the same pre-guard pattern as the 1D reflection/replication pads: it computes input_shape[ndim - 1] before checking ndim > 0. Scalar input can panic in debug builds instead of returning an AutogradError, while CircularPad2d already fails closed on insufficient rank. Acceptance: add a rank guard to CircularPad1d, add focused scalar-input regression coverage, preserve existing valid pad behavior, and validate via scoped rch.
close_reason: Shipped scalar-input rank guard for CircularPad1d.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for argmax_dim + argmin_dim contiguous f64 (index-returning reductions)
description:
argmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 and argmin_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 are sibling ops to max_dim/min_dim but return only the integer indices (no values). They share the (outer, inner) loop structure with the reduction family.

The index correctness is the key invariant: for every output cell, the returned index i must satisfy input[outer, i, inner] == max(input[outer, :, inner]) (resp. min). The reductions fuzz target (hka3) cross-checks max_dim/min_dim against mean — argmax/argmin need their own probe because they don't return values to cross-check.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_argmax exercising both ops
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok, asserts output.len == out_numel AND every returned index is in [0, dim_size) AND input[selected position] is the max/min along that slice
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_argmax fuzz target covering argmax_dim and argmin_dim. Index correctness invariant: for every output cell, target walks the input slice independently to compute actual max/min and verifies kernel-returned index points to that value. Catches off-by-one in stride computation and 'wrong position but valid index' regressions. NaN slices skipped (kernel contract: first NaN wins). Range check: indices integer in [0, reduce_size). Shape-volume cap 4096. Two seeds (2D dim=0, 3D dim=2 for inner_size>1 path). Builds clean.

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_ge(a, b) == NOT(tensor_lt(a, b)) for non-NaN
description:
For non-NaN: ge(a, b) + lt(a, b) == 1 (complementary). ge == 1 - lt.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_ge_is_complement_of_lt
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: ge(a, b) + lt(a, b) == 1.0 for non-NaN. ge is complement of lt under standard ordering. Catches dispatch drift. Passes on first run.

title: Refresh FEATURE_PARITY custom autograd Function row after 2er close
description: Reality-check finding: FEATURE_PARITY.md still marks the PyTorch-compatible custom autograd Function surface as parity_gap and points to frankentorch-2er, but no-db tracker truth shows frankentorch-2er is closed with tensor_apply_function/FunctionCtx coverage. Update the row so the parity dashboard reflects the closed Rust-idiomatic custom autograd API and leaves only non-blocking trait-sugar wording if needed. Validate with no-db tracker evidence and a focused docs diff.
close_reason: Completed: updated FEATURE_PARITY custom autograd Function row to reflect closed frankentorch-2er Rust-idiomatic tensor_apply_function/FunctionCtx support instead of an active parity gap.

title: Mark GW-003 runtime durability conformance gap closed
description: /reality-check-for-project found artifacts/phase2c/USER_WORKFLOW_SCENARIO_GAP_LEDGER_V1.md still claims GW-003 is open because runtime durability evidence is not asserted in conformance logs. Current ft-conformance already emits runtime_durability_summary/runtime_evidence and tests strict_serialization_expected_error_logs_runtime_durability_summary. Acceptance: update the GW-003 ledger row to closed with code/test anchors and validation evidence, without touching active ft-conformance work.
