Asserts: each step preserves F32 dtype (catches silent F64 promotion), shape preserved, relu >= 0, all finite. Complements kernel-level f32 fuzz (apse, ef6c) and probes ft-api dispatch directly. Two corpus seeds. Module-level Linear/Conv F32 testing deferred to 7iqt (requires F32-aware module constructors which is a bigger lift).

title: Fail closed on non-finite histc inputs and bounds
close_reason: Completed

title: perfect-e2e: TransformerDecoderLayer trains end-to-end with Adam
description: Add E2E perfect-e2e for TransformerDecoderLayer. Currently the perfect-e2e battery covers TransformerEncoderLayer (dv5a) and the multi-layer TransformerEncoder (48ja), but not the decoder side. The decoder layer adds cross-attention against an encoder memory tensor — a third linear/attention path not exercised by the encoder tests.
close_reason: Added transformer_decoder_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam — TransformerDecoderLayer (d_model=8, nhead=2, dim_feedforward=16, GELU, post-norm). 1×4×8 tgt, 1×3×8 memory (different src_len from tgt to exercise cross-attention K/V dims), MSE against zeros. 200 Adam steps; expects 10x loss reduction. Locks the cross-attention gradient flow that the encoder tests do not exercise. ft-conformance now at 154 base lib tests, 202 with --features fuzz.

title: EmbeddingBag.forward_with_offsets severs autograd
description:
EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets in ft-nn (line 2366) extracts tensor_values from indices, offsets, weight, and per_sample_weights, computes the bag aggregation in plain f64, and rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(output, vec![num_bags, dim], false). This silently severs autograd through the embedding weight AND through per_sample_weights — breaking training for any model using nn.EmbeddingBag (NLP classification, recommendation systems, fastText-style models).

Fix: compose via tensor_index_select + tensor_scatter_add (or matmul with a precomputed indicator matrix). Strategy:
1. Look up rows: emb = tensor_index_select(weight, 0, indices) → [N, D]
2. If per_sample_weights: emb = emb * psw_broadcast (autograd-aware)
3. Per-bag aggregation:
   - Build a [N] non-grad bag_assignment tensor: bag_assignment[i] = which bag i belongs to
   - For Sum: scatter_add into [num_bags, D] zeros. Mean: divide by bag_size after.
   - For Max: needs different routing (gather max per bag) — defer or composed via softmax-based approximation.
4. Handle padding_idx by zeroing out those rows pre-aggregation via mask.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets (line 2366).

title: [code-review] ConvTranspose2d/3d output-shape arithmetic rejects valid padding or underflows
description: [HIGH] Review of recent ConvTranspose1d bias work exposed the same output-length surface in adjacent transposed-conv implementations. In crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs, ConvTranspose2d computes h_span/w_span, rejects whenever span < 2*padding, then adds kernel/output_padding. That over-rejects valid PyTorch-style cases such as H_in=1, kernel=3, padding=1, output_padding=0 where output size should be 1. ConvTranspose3d still computes (dim-1)*stride - 2*padding + kernel + output_padding directly with usize arithmetic, so padding-erased outputs can panic/underflow instead of returning AutogradError. Fix should use checked arithmetic in mathematical order: span + kernel + output_padding, then subtract 2*padding only if the result stays positive, with focused tests for valid padding-preserved and invalid padding-erased shapes.
close_reason: Fixed ConvTranspose2d/3d output-shape arithmetic and singleton padded-output regressions

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] no snapshot for version_mismatch decode diagnostic
description:
version_mismatch_is_fail_closed (lib.rs:1837) verifies the diagnostic via err.to_string().contains('schema version mismatch') — substring match only. The exact wording (which includes both expected and found schema_version values) is unprotected.

The version-mismatch diagnostic is exactly the kind of error a downstream loader will pattern-match on (offering 'upgrade to schema vN' guidance), and a wording change could ship without breaking the substring check.

Companion to:
  - strict_checkpoint_json_contract_snapshot
  - hardened_checkpoint_json_contract_snapshot (mkgv)
  - strict_unknown_field_diagnostic_snapshot (it5r)
  - hardened_malformed_payload_diagnostic_snapshot (mkgv)
  - hardened_non_object_payload_diagnostic_snapshot (cn68v)
  - raptorq_sidecar_json_contract_snapshot (uptn)
  - decode_proof_artifact_json_contract_snapshot (6jfc)

Acceptance:
- new test version_mismatch_diagnostic_snapshot
- pinned via insta::assert_snapshot
- existing version_mismatch_is_fail_closed substring test left intact
- ft-serialize lib tests +1
close_reason: Shipped: c50e8b6. version_mismatch_diagnostic_snapshot pins exact wording 'schema version mismatch: expected=1 found=2'. 8th serialize-side golden in the family.

title: ft-api: 1x1 linalg_cholesky should propagate gradients
description: torch.linalg.cholesky supports autograd. FrankenTorch currently fail-louds for all requires_grad inputs. The full Cholesky backward remains non-trivial, but the 1x1 SPD case is exactly sqrt(A[0,0]) with derivative 1/(2*sqrt(A[0,0])). Add this narrow F64 autograd path while keeping larger Cholesky shapes fail-loud.
close_reason: Implemented F64 1x1 linalg_cholesky autograd via sqrt derivative; ft-api tests and clippy pass.

title: Add metamorphic property tests: clamp idempotence, mean=sum/n, std=sqrt(var)
description:
Three more invariants:

1. fuzz_metamorphic_clamp_idempotent_in_range — clamp(x, lo, hi) bit-exactly equals x when x is already in [lo, hi]. Use input * 0.5 to ensure all values stay within [-1, 1] then clamp to [-1, 1].

2. fuzz_metamorphic_mean_equals_sum_div_n — tensor_mean(x) and tensor_sum(x)/n agree within a small ULP envelope.

3. fuzz_metamorphic_std_equals_sqrt_var — tensor_std(x) and sqrt(tensor_var(x)) agree within ULP envelope (both unbiased).

title: ft-nn Bilinear severs autograd; composable through outer + reshape + matmul
description:
Bilinear at lib.rs:987 (forward_bilinear) computes y[b, k] = sum_{i,j} x1[b, i] * W[k, i, j] * x2[b, j] manually in plain f64, then rebuilds requires_grad=false leaf and adds optional bias on top. Severs autograd through x1/x2/W.

Composable via outer-product + matmul:
   x1_3d = unsqueeze(x1, 2)             # [batch, in1, 1]
   x2_3d = unsqueeze(x2, 1)             # [batch, 1, in2]
   outer = x1_3d * x2_3d                # [batch, in1, in2]
   flat  = reshape(outer, [batch, in1*in2])
   W_flat_t = transpose(reshape(W, [out, in1*in2]), 0, 1)
   y = matmul(flat, W_flat_t)           # [batch, out]
   reshape to original output shape, then add bias.

All ops autograd-aware so gradients flow to x1 / x2 / W / bias.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed Bilinear through reshape + unsqueeze + expand + mul + reshape + transpose + matmul + add (bias). The bilinear formula y_k = x1^T W_k x2 + b_k is reformulated as y = vec(x1 outer x2) @ vec(W).T + b, which composes through autograd-aware primitives. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: Fix orthogonal_ for wide matrices
description: /reality-check-for-project found FEATURE_PARITY marks torch.nn.init.orthogonal_ covered, but init_orthogonal_ in ft-nn generates a [rows, cols] random matrix and copies Q directly. For rows < cols, QR returns only [rows, rows], so the remaining columns are zero-padded instead of producing PyTorch-style semi-orthogonal rows via transpose-before-QR. Acceptance: implement the PyTorch algorithm for flattened tensors when rows < cols by transposing the sampled matrix before QR and transposing Q back; prove row-orthonormality for a wide shape such as [2, 4] with no zero-padded tail; keep tall-matrix test green; validate with scoped rch ft-nn tests/check/clippy.
close_reason: Completed: fixed ft-nn orthogonal_ wide matrix path by QRing the transposed flat shape, applying the R-diagonal sign convention, transposing back, and adding a wide row-orthonormality regression; code shipped in a0acd83.

title: tensor_kron generic path allocates a Vec per inner iteration (perf regression)
description:
fa1cf3d generalized tensor_kron to arbitrary ranks but the new generic path calls decode_coords inside the doubly-nested loop over a_vals x b_vals, allocating a fresh Vec<usize> of length rank for EACH iteration. For a 2D x 2D kron of 100x100 inputs that is 100M Vec allocations replacing what was a constant-time inner loop in the previous specialized 2D path.

Fix sketch: precompute strides for a_aligned, b_aligned, and out_shape ONCE before the loop. Inside the inner loop iterate over a_linear and b_linear directly, accumulating out_linear via stride math without intermediate coordinate Vecs. The stride for out at dim d is product(out_shape[d+1..]); a_aligned coordinate d contributes a_coord*b_aligned[d]*stride_out[d]+b_coord*stride_out[d] which can be precomputed as a per-(a_linear,b_linear) table.

Severity: MEDIUM. Functionality is correct; this is a hot-loop allocation regression that did not exist in the prior specialized 2D path. Stalls a primary tensor algebra op for any non-trivial input size.

Reproduction: cargo bench (or a hand-rolled timing harness) on tensor_kron of two 64x64 inputs vs the pre-fa1cf3d code.
close_reason: Hoisted decode_coords Vec allocation out of the doubly-nested kron loop by precomputing a_strides and b_strides via contiguous_strides() ONCE before the outer loop. Per-iteration heap allocation eliminated; output coordinates derived in-place from a_linear/b_linear via integer division/modulo. Existing 5 kron tests still pass; new kron_4d_matches_explicit_index_decomposition regression added to lock the stride-based output layout against future drift. ft-api 1087 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for var_dim + std_dim (Bessel correction edge case)
description:
var_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 and std_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 implement Welford-style (or two-pass) variance with optional Bessel correction (unbiased flag). The N=1 edge case is particularly bug-prone:
- unbiased=true, N=1 should return NaN (division by 0)
- unbiased=false, N=1 should return 0

Both ops were recently rewritten with push-based output (suw1). No fuzz target currently exercises:
- dim guard
- the N=1 edge case with both unbiased values
- the general path
- non-negativity (variance/std >= 0 for finite input)

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_var exercising BOTH var_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 AND std_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 across both unbiased=true and unbiased=false (chosen by input byte)
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok with finite input, asserts output.len == out_numel AND each finite element >= -eps
- >=2 corpus seeds, at least one with dim_size==1 to force the edge case
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_var fuzz target covering both var_dim and std_dim. Discovery: the kernels do NOT take an unbiased flag (always Bessel-corrected n-1); bead description anticipated a flag that does not exist, so the fuzz target probes the hardcoded-unbiased path. Three invariants: (1) length = numel/dim_size, (2) reduce_size<2 must return NaN for every cell, (3) std == sqrt(var) within 16*EPSILON ULP tolerance (couples the two ops; catches drift in either kernel and catastrophic-cancellation regressions in the recently-rewritten pairwise-sum scratch path). Plus var >= -1e-12 non-negativity. Two seeds: normal, reduce_size=1 NaN-edge. Builds clean.

title: [mock-code-finder] huber_loss has the same 5-op blend chain that uqtq replaced in smooth_l1_loss
close_reason: Shipped in aff2be8 — huber_loss now uses tensor_where(mask, quadratic, linear) mirroring the smooth_l1_loss fix in uqtq. -1 full() allocation, -1 sub + -2 mul + -1 add collapsed into 1 where. All 7 huber_loss tests pass.

title: tensor_inner is severed-autograd: composable through dot/matmul
description:
tensor_inner at lib.rs:15480 contracts the last dim of two tensors:
   c[..a, j_a, ..b, j_b] = sum_l a[..a, l] * b[..b, l]
where a has shape [a_outer..., a_last] and b has shape [b_outer..., b_last] with a_last == b_last. The forward extracts values, computes the contraction in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.inner is differentiable.

Compose through autograd primitives:
- 1-D case: delegate to tensor_dot (already autograd-aware).
- Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D (a_outer, a_last) and (b_outer, b_last), compute matmul(a_flat, b_flat.T), reshape to the inner output shape. matmul + transpose + reshape are all autograd-aware.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass continuing from sirh fix.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_inner through autograd primitives:
  - 1-D × 1-D: delegates to tensor_dot (autograd-aware via tape).
  - Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D (outer, last), transpose b, matmul, reshape to inner output shape. matmul + transpose + reshape are all autograd-aware.
The output shape contract ([1] for 1-D inputs that previously returned shape [1]) is preserved via tensor_unsqueeze on the 0-D scalar tensor_dot returns. Added inner_1d_propagates_gradient_via_dot regression test ([1,2,3] · [4,5,6] = 32 → grad_a = b, grad_b = a). ft-api lib tests: 1150.

2. fuzz_corpus_prop_unary_abs_is_non_negative — abs dispatch always produces ≥ 0 outputs

Follow-up to frankentorch-wze.

Once Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust#224 lands, rerun the archived xsp probe matrix against a freshly vacuumed recovery DB and confirm whether the blocked_issues_cache import path still leaks old pages.

title: [mock-code-finder] tensor_corrcoef duplicates zeros tensor across eq mask and where branch
close_reason: Shipped: 13380e0. tensor_corrcoef now reuses one [n,n] zeros tensor across the eq mask and where-true branch. -1 full() + -1 tape node per call (saves n² f64 allocation; most impactful in this dedup family since corrcoef builds an n² matrix). 3 corrcoef tests including gradient regression pass.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] no torch oracle for tensor_pow IEEE special-value matrix (negative-base + fractional exp / inf base / NaN propagation)
description:
tensor_pow already has the existing oracle torch_pow_ieee754_subprocess_conformance (20302), but it sweeps RANDOM finite inputs. The bug-prone special-value matrix isn't enumerated:

- pow(-1.0, 0.5)        → NaN (negative base + fractional exp)
- pow(-1.0, 1.0)        → -1.0
- pow(-1.0, 2.0)        → 1.0
- pow(0.0, 0.0)         → 1.0 (per IEEE 754-2008 §9.2.1)
- pow(-0.0, 0.0)        → 1.0
- pow(0.0, -1.0)        → +inf (1/0)
- pow(0.0, -inf)        → +inf
- pow(0.0, +inf)        → 0.0
- pow(1.0, NaN)         → 1.0 (per IEEE 754-2008 §9.2.1 — base 1 always returns 1)
- pow(NaN, 0.0)         → 1.0 (exponent 0 always returns 1)
- pow(2.0, NaN)         → NaN
- pow(NaN, NaN)         → NaN
- pow(+inf, 0.0)        → 1.0
- pow(+inf, +inf)       → +inf

This is a curated 14-case matrix. A bit-exact (or bit-or-NaN) oracle against torch would lock the kernel's IEEE 754-2008 §9.2.1 compliance.

Acceptance:
- new conformance test torch_pow_ieee754_special_value_matrix_subprocess in ft-conformance
- exercise all 14 cases
- bit-exact comparison vs torch.pow with the same special-value inputs
- gated behind feature='conformance' or torch availability skip
- documented PR description with the spec citation
close_reason: Already covered: the existing torch_pow_ieee754_subprocess_conformance (ft-conformance/src/lib.rs:20302) is already a comprehensive IEEE 754 §9.2.1 special-value matrix with 50+ pairs covering everything proposed in this bead: pow(x, 0) = 1 for any x including NaN; pow(±1, ±inf) = 1; pow(0, ±) special cases; pow(-0, integer odd/even); pow(±inf, ±); pow(negative, non-integer) = NaN; pow(NaN, _) and pow(_, NaN). Uses bit-equality with NaN-tolerance against libm pow via Python ctypes (the precise upstream oracle since torch.pow wraps libm). Bead filed without grepping for existing coverage — review-mode discipline reinforced. No code change needed.

title: [parity-gap] tensor_signbit missing — torch.signbit not exposed
description:
torch.signbit(x) returns a 0/1 mask, true where the IEEE 754 sign bit of x is set (negatives AND -0.0). It is a sister classification op to torch.isposinf and torch.isneginf, both of which were added in 01ca63d as tensor_isposinf / tensor_isneginf — but signbit was not.

Concrete repro: porting torch code that uses torch.signbit fails to compile because there is no FrankenTorchSession::tensor_signbit. Workarounds in user code (tensor_lt against zero) silently lose -0.0 (since -0.0 < 0.0 is false) and diverge from torch.

Fix direction: extract values via tensor_values; map each via f64::is_sign_negative (Rust's intrinsic that respects IEEE 754 — true for negatives, -0.0, -inf, and the negative-NaN bit pattern); rebuild a non-grad leaf. Fail loud on requires_grad like the other classification ops.

Acceptance:
- tensor_signbit present on FrankenTorchSession
- regression test for ±values, ±0.0, ±inf covers the IEEE 754 sign bit semantics
- requires_grad guard test mirrors tensor_isposinf
- ft-api lib test count increases
close_reason: Shipped: 7d14248. tensor_signbit added via f64::is_sign_negative; preserves IEEE 754 semantics for -0.0, -inf, and the negative-NaN bit pattern. 3 regression tests including ±0.0 distinguishability. ft-api lib tests +3.

title: [profiling-software-performance] matmul kernels {f64,f32} zero-init m*n output that gets fully overwritten
close_reason: Shipped in 0157faf — both f64 and f32 matmul kernels switched to Vec::with_capacity + push for the output, skipping the m*n zero-init memset. Saves 256K writes per matmul call for m=n=512, plus reduces cache pre-touch pressure. Scratch buffer (k cells, reused across iterations) unchanged. All matmul tests pass.

title: [testing-metamorphic] MR for tensor_cummax/tensor_cummin monotonicity + last-equals-extremum
description: tensor_cummax(x).values must be monotonically non-decreasing with last element == max(x); tensor_cummin(x).values must be non-increasing with last == min(x). No MR currently exercises these contracts. Add fuzz_metamorphic_cummax_cummin_contract under feature=fuzz.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_cummax_cummin_contract checking bit-exact running-extremum, monotonicity, and last-equals-global-extremum across 32-element random vectors. Lib test passes; ft-conformance now 357 tests in feature=fuzz lib.

title: [testing-metamorphic] add tanh odd-function symmetry MR (tanh(-x) == -tanh(x))
description:
tensor_tanh has subprocess parity coverage (existing tanh tests + tanh-sigmoid identity in fcb94ff) but no metamorphic property test pinning the algebraic symmetry tanh is bit-exactly odd: tanh(-x) == -tanh(x).

This MR is independent of tanh_equals_two_sigmoid_two_x_minus_one (which catches sigmoid/tanh inconsistency) — odd-function symmetry catches a different failure class: a regression to the tanh kernel that perturbed the negative-x path (e.g. wrong sign-handling in the Taylor expansion fast path or the saturation branch) would land here even if the sigmoid identity still held.

libm tanh on x86_64 / aarch64 is bit-exactly odd for finite args (it's defined as (e^x - e^-x) / (e^x + e^-x) and the implementation preserves sign symmetry). So this MR can assert to_bits()-equality, the tightest possible envelope.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz_metamorphic_tanh_is_odd_function property test
- to_bits() equality between tanh(-x) and -tanh(x) for finite samples
- proptest-based, samples in the existing MR range
- ft-conformance lib tests --features fuzz +1
close_reason: Shipped: 0358f36. fuzz_metamorphic_tanh_is_odd_function pins tanh(-x)==-tanh(x) bit-exactly via to_bits() across [-32, 32] (smooth + saturation branches). Independent of tanh-sigmoid identity — different failure-mode class (sign symmetry vs sigmoid coupling).

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for tensor_rsqrt + tensor_sqrt reciprocal identity (rsqrt(x) == 1/sqrt(x))
description:
rsqrt(x) == 1 / sqrt(x) within ULP tolerance for x > 0. Two paths to reciprocal sqrt.

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_rsqrt_equals_one_over_sqrt
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: rsqrt(x) ≈ 1/sqrt(x) within 8 ULP for x > 0. Catches drift between dedicated rsqrt and composed 1/sqrt. Passes on first run.

title: [parity-gap] tensor_clamp_tensor missing — torch.clamp with tensor bounds
description:
torch.clamp(x, min=lo_tensor, max=hi_tensor) accepts per-element TENSOR bounds (not just scalars). FrankenTorch's tensor_clamp only accepts scalar min/max. Code porting from torch using torch.clamp with tensor bounds — common in adversarial training (per-pixel epsilon balls), gradient clipping by per-parameter bounds, and quantile-bound regression — fails to compile.

Implementation: tensor_clamp_tensor(x, lo, hi) = tensor_minimum(tensor_maximum(x, lo), hi). Both tensor_minimum and tensor_maximum already exist and are autograd-aware (max routes upstream gradient to whichever operand achieved the per-element maximum, broadcast_to backward sums over the broadcasted dims). The composition is autograd-aware end-to-end. Same-shape requirement matches FrankenTorch's existing tensor_minimum/maximum (broadcasting comes via tensor_broadcast_to upstream).

Acceptance:
- tensor_clamp_tensor(input, min, max) takes three tensor args of matching shape
- composes via tensor_maximum + tensor_minimum
- regression tests covering: clipping above and below, in-bounds passthrough, NaN propagation (tensor_min/max kernel propagates NaN per cc_kernel_cpu), gradient flow
- ft-api lib tests +N where N >= 4
close_reason: Shipped: 4c5f261. tensor_clamp_tensor composes via tensor_maximum + tensor_minimum, autograd-aware end-to-end, NaN-propagating through the kernel. 4 regression tests including gradient + NaN propagation pass.

title: Clarify optional local oracle mirror in EXHAUSTIVE_LEGACY_ANALYSIS
description: Update only the top-level source-of-truth wording in EXHAUSTIVE_LEGACY_ANALYSIS.md so the legacy PyTorch checkout is described as the typical local mirror when present, without rewriting the historical measurement sections.
notes: Clarified the top-level legacy corpus wording so the local PyTorch mirror is described as the typical path when present, while leaving the historical measured-from path later in the document untouched.

title: masked_scatter all-false mask skips source validation
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs live masked_scatter rewrite. tensor_masked_scatter now returns Ok(input) when n_true == 0 before validating source dtype/device compatibility. Repro from code inspection: call tensor_masked_scatter with an F64 input, an all-zero mask with matching numel, and an incompatible source tensor such as F32. The new fast path returns the input unchanged instead of failing closed; with any true mask the path reaches tensor_where and rejects mismatched dtypes. This makes validation data-dependent on mask contents and can silently accept unsupported source tensors. Fix: validate source dtype/device against input before the n_true == 0 fast path, and add a regression test for all-false mask plus incompatible source.
close_reason: Hoisted dtype + device validation for input/mask/source above the n_true==0 fast path in tensor_masked_scatter, so the fast and slow paths share the same up-front contract regardless of mask contents. Added masked_scatter_all_false_mask_still_validates_source_dtype regression test.

title: Standardize top-level oracle references to repo-relative mirror wording
description: AGENTS.md, PLAN_TO_PORT_PYTORCH_TO_RUST.md, and COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md still use absolute example roots for the legacy oracle mirror. Update them to repo-relative wording (`legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch`) so they remain correct across /data and /dp checkouts.
notes: Standardized the top-level oracle references in AGENTS.md, PLAN_TO_PORT_PYTORCH_TO_RUST.md, and COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md to repo-relative mirror wording (`legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch`) so the docs no longer depend on /dp vs /data checkout prefixes.

title: [modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis] cdist three static-reason rejections mask the actual offending shapes
description:
tensor_cdist (ft-api/src/lib.rs:4396) has three opaque static-reason rejections that mask which shape and which dim were wrong:

  L4411: 'cdist: batch dimensions must match' — caller can
         see neither x1's batch nor x2's batch
  L4427: 'cdist: inputs must be 2-D or 3-D with matching batch'
         — caller can see neither rank that was actually given
  L4436: 'cdist: last dimension (feature dim) must match' —
         caller can see neither feature dim

All three are dynamic-info-bearing and migrate cleanly to KernelError variants:

  - L4411 batch mismatch → ShapeMismatch {lhs: x1_shape,
    rhs: x2_shape}: caller compares batch dims at position 0
  - L4427 rank mismatch → InvalidDimension {dim: 0, ndim:
    x1_shape.len()}: surfaces the actual rank that was given
  - L4436 feature mismatch → ShapeMismatch {lhs: x1_shape,
    rhs: x2_shape}: caller compares last dims

Matches the established pattern (oagi/6zyy/c991/qaux/oitu/pyl0/8sct/8c9m).

Acceptance: all three cdist static reasons removed and replaced with dynamic KernelError variants; existing cdist lib tests still pass.
close_reason: Shipped in d1f5b3e — cdist's three static rejections migrated to KernelError::ShapeMismatch (batch + feature mismatches with both shapes) and KernelError::InvalidDimension (rank mismatch with actual rank). All 3 cdist lib tests pass.

title: Add metamorphic tests: relu idempotence, transpose-of-product, dropout p=0 identity
description:
Three more metamorphic property tests:
1. fuzz_metamorphic_relu_is_idempotent — relu(relu(x)) == relu(x) bit-exactly
2. fuzz_metamorphic_transpose_of_product — (A @ B)^T ≈ B^T @ A^T within ULP
3. fuzz_metamorphic_dropout_p_zero_is_identity — dropout(x, p=0) returns x bit-exactly

title: [testing-metamorphic] no MR for sin^2 + cos^2 == 1 Pythagorean identity
description:
Fundamental trig identity: sin^2(x) + cos^2(x) == 1 for all real x. Within ULP tolerance scaled by the magnitude of the individual squares.

This catches:
- sign drift in sin or cos
- libm trig precision regressions (would surface as systematic deviation from 1)
- autograd severance through trig (each square is exact)

Acceptance:
- new MR fuzz_metamorphic_sin_cos_pythagorean
- gated behind feature='fuzz'
close_reason: Shipped: fuzz_metamorphic_sin_cos_pythagorean. sin^2(x) + cos^2(x) ≈ 1 for x in [-12, 12] within 16 ULP. Squares are exact under f64 so the ULP budget is purely libm sin/cos precision. Catches sin/cos drift, sign flips, autograd severance. Passes on first run.

title: functional_conv_transpose1d / 2d sever autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
ft-api functional_conv_transpose1d (line 5866) and functional_conv_transpose2d (line 5985) both extract input/weight tensor_values, run the transposed convolution in plain f64, and rebuild via tensor_variable(_, _, false). Silently severs autograd through both input and weight — breaks training for upsampling models, U-Net decoders, GAN generators, segmentation networks.

The corresponding ConvTranspose1d / ConvTranspose2d nn.Module impls already compose correctly via tensor_narrow + tensor_squeeze + tensor_matmul + tensor_unsqueeze + tensor_pad + tensor_add (transposed convolution as scatter-and-accumulate). Mirror the same pattern in the functional ops.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs functional_conv_transpose1d (line 5866), functional_conv_transpose2d (line 5985).

title: [parity-gap] create_graph expand backward collapses broadcast gradients
description: Review-mode finding from recent higher-rank expand changes. Repro: add a test that builds x = tensor_variable([1.0, 2.0], [2], requires_grad=true), y = tensor_expand(x, [3, 2]), loss = tensor_sum(tensor_mul(y, y)), then call tensor_backward_with_options(loss, BackwardOptions { create_graph: true, ..strict_default() }). Expected first gradient node/values match PyTorch/autograd contract [6.0, 12.0] and support second backward. Actual create_graph Expand arm in crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs collapses incoming gradient to one scalar with incoming_vals.iter().sum() and creates a gradient leaf with the original [2] shape, producing the wrong per-element gradient and a shape/storage mismatch risk. Normal backward has the correct per-axis broadcast sum, but create_graph does not.
close_reason: Fixed create_graph expand backward to sum broadcast gradients per original input element via a tracked SumToShape node; added prepended-dim and singleton-broadcast create_graph double-backward regressions. Verified with targeted ft-autograd tests, fmt, workspace check, workspace clippy, UBS, and metamorphic roll inverse test.

title: Fix poisson_nll_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]
description:
poisson_nll_loss at lib.rs:10357 computes elementwise:
  log_input=True:  loss_i = exp(inp_i) - tgt_i * inp_i
  log_input=False: loss_i = inp_i - tgt_i * log(inp_i + eps)
plus optional Stirling correction tgt*log(tgt) - tgt + 0.5*log(2*pi*tgt) for full=true.

Composable through autograd primitives — the Stirling correction is a function of target only (no input gradient) and can be precomputed as a non-grad scalar tensor and added back in.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Composed poisson_nll_loss through autograd primitives:
  log_input=true:  exp(input) - target * input → mean
  log_input=false: input - target * log(input + eps) → mean
The Stirling correction (full=true) depends only on target, so we precompute its mean as a non-grad scalar and add via tensor_add. Added poisson_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[1,2], target=[1,1] → analytical [(e-1)/2, (e^2-1)/2]). ft-api lib tests: 1157.

title: porting: add tensor_sinc with safe-zero handling for torch.sinc parity
description: torch.sinc(x) = sin(pi*x) / (pi*x) for x != 0, 1 at x = 0. Compose via tensor_sin + tensor_div with safe-zero handling: build a mask = (x == 0), use a safe denominator that's pi*x except where x=0 (use 1 to avoid 0/0), then where(mask, ones, sin_term / safe_denom). Gradient at x=0 is 0 (correct limit) since the where routes to the ones constant which has no requires_grad. Useful for signal processing / interpolation.
close_reason: Added tensor_sinc with safe-zero handling matching torch.sinc. Composes through tensor_mul (pi*x) + tensor_sin + tensor_div with a safe-denominator guard (where x=0 the denominator becomes 1 to avoid 0/0; the result is then masked back to 1 via tensor_where). Gradient at x=0 is correctly 0 since the final where routes that position to a no-grad ones constant. 3 unit tests cover known values (0/0.5/1/2), zero-input no-NaN (the safe-denom guard), and analytic gradient regression (0 at x=0, -4/π at x=0.5). ft-api now at 1286 lib tests.

title: Implement TripletMarginLoss non-L2 Lp path
description: /reality-check-for-project found README/FEATURE_PARITY claim TripletMarginLoss as part of the parity-green nn/loss surface, but ft-nn currently fails loud for any p != 2 after frankentorch-9c06. Acceptance: implement non-L2 p-norm path in TripletMarginLoss::forward_triplet via autograd-aware abs/pow/sum/pow composition; preserve current L2 behavior; reject only invalid p values that PyTorch would reject; add focused ft-nn tests for p=1 and p=3 value/gradient behavior; validate with scoped rch ft-nn tests/check/clippy.
close_reason: Completed: implemented finite positive non-L2 TripletMarginLoss Lp path via autograd-aware abs/pow/sum/pow composition, added p=1 and p=3 value/gradient regressions plus invalid-p guard; code shipped in 48666c8.

title: tensor_linalg_svd doesn't complete orthonormal basis for zero singular vectors
description: On rank-deficient matrices, FrankenTorch's tensor_linalg_svd returns U columns corresponding to zero singular values as all-zeros instead of orthonormal vectors completing the basis. numpy.linalg.svd (and torch.linalg.svd) always produce a fully orthonormal U with U^T U = I_k, regardless of rank. Repro: 3x3 matrix [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]] (rank 2). numpy returns U[:,2] as a unit vector orthogonal to col([1,2,3],[4,5,6]); FrankenTorch returns U[:,2] = 0. Surfaced by torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance during the frankentorch-c36b work; current conformance test trims the rank-deficient case to land. Severity: medium — breaks the U^T U = I invariant that downstream code (e.g., least-squares via SVD) may rely on; doesn't affect the U @ diag(S) @ Vh reconstruction.
notes: Fixed in this commit: unified the orthonormal-basis completion in svd_tall to handle both (a) rank-deficient inputs (zero singular values) and (b) full_matrices mode with m > k. Any column of U that ends up with norm <= tol now gets a unit vector orthogonal to all previously-set columns via Gram-Schmidt against e_0..e_{m-1}, retrying basis vectors as needed. Restored the rank_deficient_3x3 case in both torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance and torch_linalg_pinv_numpy_subprocess_conformance — both pass.

title: Add metamorphic property tests for index_select identity, relu decomposition, cumsum/diff round-trip
description:
Extend the testing-metamorphic pass with four additional property tests in ft-conformance::tests under #[cfg(feature=fuzz)]:

1. fuzz_metamorphic_index_select_sequential_is_identity — for any 1-D x of length n, index_select(x, dim=0, [0, 1, ..., n-1]) must equal x bit-exactly.

2. fuzz_metamorphic_relu_decomposition — for any x, relu(x) - relu(-x) == x bit-exactly. relu(x) + relu(-x) == abs(x) for x != 0; both are 0 at x = 0.

3. fuzz_metamorphic_cumsum_diff_round_trip — diff(cumsum(x)) == x[1:] bit-exactly (since both are pure addition/subtraction of the same values).

4. fuzz_metamorphic_pow_two_equals_self_mul — pow(x, 2.0) and x * x must agree within a few ULPs (tensor_pow may use exp(2*log(|x|)) for general exponents, but should special-case integer exponents OR at least match within a small ULP envelope).

title: [testing-fuzzing] no fuzz target for lerp_tensor_contiguous_f64 (3-operand interpolation)
description:
lerp(start, end, weight) returns start + weight * (end - start). Three-input op with broadcast-free contiguous f64 path. Boundary behavior is bug-prone:
- weight == 0 must return start exactly
- weight == 1 must return end exactly
- weight outside [0,1] is permitted (extrapolation) — important contract not to clamp

No fuzz target currently covers shape-validation across the three inputs or the value-correctness of the boundary identities.

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target ft_kernel_cpu_lerp exercising lerp_tensor_contiguous_f64
- shape-volume cap at 4096
- on Ok, asserts output.len == numel
- additionally drive at least one weight == 0.0 case (assert output == start within ULP tolerance) and one weight == 1.0 case (assert output == end)
- >=2 corpus seeds
- registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml
- compiles cleanly under rch exec
close_reason: Shipped: ft_kernel_cpu_lerp fuzz target. Drives lerp_tensor_contiguous_f64 with weight selector that funnels 25% of inputs each to weight=0 and weight=1 boundary cases, with the remaining 50% covering interpolation/extrapolation in roughly [-2, 2]. Boundary identities checked with ULP_TOL=16*EPSILON (kernel does sv + w*(ev-sv), not direct copy, so w=1 path has ULP-level slack). Non-finite cells skipped. Shape-volume cap 4096. Two corpus seeds. Builds clean.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] tensor_sinc lacks numpy subprocess oracle
description: tensor_sinc/tensor_special_sinc (normalized sinc: sin(πx)/(πx), sinc(0)=1) has no subprocess oracle. Add torch_sinc_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering: x == 0 (mask branch), small |x|, large |x|, negative x, inf, NaN. Pattern: numpy.sinc, ~few ULP libm-quality tolerance.
close_reason: Added torch_sinc_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering mask branch, tiny x, integer/half-integer x, large x, inf/NaN. 4 ULP relative + 1e-15 absolute floor. Lib test passes.

title: Apply PairwiseDistance eps and keepdim semantics
description: Discovery pass with empty ready queue: ft-nn::PairwiseDistance stores eps and keepdim from with_eps()/with_keepdim(), but forward_pair() and Module::forward() ignore both fields. That makes torch.nn.PairwiseDistance(p, eps, keepdim) parity observably wrong: eps should shift the elementwise difference before the p-norm, and keepdim=true should preserve the reduced last dimension. Acceptance: apply eps before tensor_norm_dim in pairwise forward paths, preserve the last dimension when keepdim=true, add focused ft-nn regressions proving non-default eps changes identical-vector distance and keepdim keeps shape [N, 1], and validate via rch ft-nn pairwise tests plus fmt/check/clippy.
close_reason: Completed: PairwiseDistance now adds eps before the p-norm and unsqueezes the reduced last dimension when keepdim=true; code shipped in 7c959c7 with pairwise_distance regressions plus fmt/check/clippy validation.

title: Implement tensor creation functions: eye, arange, linspace, logspace
description: Add torch.eye, torch.arange, torch.linspace, torch.logspace to ft-api. These are used everywhere in model code and tests. All are non-differentiable leaf tensors.
close_reason: Already implemented: eye, arange, linspace, logspace all present in ft-api with tests.
